In The News

At T.I.M.E. Organization, our work continues to make headlines. From community partnerships and program expansions to impactful client success stories, we’re proud to be recognized for the difference we’re making across Baltimore and surrounding counties.

Stay up to date on recent features, press releases, and media spotlights that reflect our commitment to behavioral health, substance use recovery, early childhood mental health, and more.

VIEW MORE IN THE NEWS

Discover personalized mental health solutions and support at T.I.M.E. Organization Inc., where we’re committed to empowering individuals and fostering mental well-being through our diverse range of services.

Outpatient Mental Health
Experience compassionate care and personalized treatment plans with our outpatient mental health services, tailored to support your journey to wellness.
LEARN MORE
Outpatient Substance Abuse (OP)
Our outpatient program offers flexible drug/alcohol treatment sessions with counseling, education, and support groups to fit into clients’ lives seamlessly.
LEARN MORE
Early Childhood Mental Health Services
Diagnosing mental health issues in young children is challenging due to unique emotional responses, making early identification crucial.
LEARN MORE
Psychiatric Rehabilitation for Adults and Children
A mental health recovery support program designed as an alternative to and prevention of inpatient psychiatric hospitalization.

Kirsten C.
One of the best decisions i made in my life, got clean from all narcotics ,and TIME also helped me secure permit housing, so that forever grateful, peace

I would recommend this program to anyone who is looking to re-enter into society.

John O.

Highland Food Pantry of Winchester, Virginia

A member agency of the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank network, Highland Food Pantry serves people in the northern Shenandoah Valley experiencing chronic hunger. Our main activity is the distribution of food to individuals and families in need. We are a United Way partner.



Our Valued Clients

Anyone who is in need of food is welcome. You do not need to bring any papers or proof of residency. Childcare is provided while clients are getting food.



Connect With Us

Please contact us if you have any questions:

Welcome to River of Life Worship Center!
Thank you for taking the time to visit our website. We are a non-denominational, multi-cultural body of believers with a rich heritage of faith.
Our mission is to create an atmosphere where people experience God’s love through Worship and the Word.
 
Join us on Sundays and Wednesdays as we experience God’s presence in the River!

Where the Holy Spirit Resides

place where the Holy Spirit resides, this is it. Our focus is worship and love! Come
see us! You will be happy you did.”

-Esther Griffin, Esq.
TESTIMONIAL
A Loving Place to Be

“As Moses was drawn to the burning bush, God impressed upon my heart to visit

that Shepard people by the power of love, how to wait on Him and how to

would be equipped for the work of the ministry. I thank

forever encouraged and grateful!”

Why the name… Church of the Incarnation

 The very name of our parish expresses the heart of our faith: that in Jesus Christ, the eternal Word took on human flesh without surrendering His divinity. This mystery forever transformed the human condition, for in Christ, God has united Himself with all humanity. Here at Incarnation, we seek to live fully into this reality—celebrating that all that is authentically human is made holy in Him. We lift our voices in prayer and song, we gather in fellowship and joy, and we honor the presence of Christ in one another, especially in those who are most in need.

At the same time, we recognize that our humanity bears both weakness and grace. Within each of us exists the struggle of the old self alongside the beauty of Christ dwelling within. For this reason, we walk together with patience, supporting one another as Christ continues His transforming work in our lives. We are delighted you have joined us in this holy journey, and we look forward to welcoming you more deeply into our community of faith and friendship in Christ.

A Worldwide Church Family

The Seventh-day Adventist Church is a mainstream Protestant church with approximately 19 million members worldwide, including more than one million members in North America. The Adventist Church operates 173 hospitals and sanitariums and more than 7,500 schools around the world. The Adventist Development and Relief Agency (ADRA) works within communities in more than 130 countries to provide community development and disaster relief.

BETTER EDUCATION
leads to better jobs

Early Childhood & Family Support offers families exemplary childcare services that ensure children start school ready to learn and succeed.

As children grow and prepare to succeed in school, parents are encouraged to actively participate in their education and strengthen family bonds.

Learn more
BETTER JOBS
lead to better lives

Workforce & Career Services creates hope and prepares people of all ages for more competitive jobs. Our customers want to identify more promising career paths that fit their skills and interests. We serve youth, migrants, seasonal farmworkers, and low-income individuals.

Learn more
BETTER HOMES & LIVES
create better communities

With financial stability, families can achieve their dream of stable housing — and even homeownership. Housing & Financial Empowerment services ensure that families receive support so they can reach these important goals.​

Learn more
BETTER COMMUNITIES
supply better education, and the cycle of improvement from individuals to communities continues.

Through our community partnerships, we can provide wraparound services that build strength in individuals and families. Our partners and volunteers are inspired to keep the cycle going and motivate others to build better lives.

WHY WE NEED YOU

An important part of our mission is to be an engaged partner that contributes to the local community. That means working with community service providers to ensure our customers thrive. Won’t you join us?

HOME
 
ABOUT
 
PROGRAMS
 
VOLUNTEER
 
DONATE NOW
 
Be the hero you always wanted to be - support our SAFE for All Seasons Campaign. Help us reach our $10,000 goal by December 31! Donate now.


	
	
	
	
SAFE Food Pantry provides food assistance to individuals and families experiencing both food insecurity and food allergies. We offer access to allergy-friendly food so our clients can address their medical needs and maintain their health during a challenging period.

We serve clients regardless of age, gender, ethnic background, or socio-economic status. Our all-volunteer organization is focused on ensuring that all individuals and families with food allergies have reliable access to safe, nutritious food when they need it most.

💙 Donate today: Your financial gifts make the biggest impact, allowing SAFE to purchase exactly what’s needed most. Learn how to give online, by check, or by text.

🥫 Food donations: Non-perishable and non-expired gluten-free and allergy-friendly items can be dropped off at Christ Episcopal Church in Columbia and Grace Episcopal Church in Elkridge. Details here.

If you need SAFE’s services, please contact us.

For regular food resources, visit the Howard County Food Map.

Together, we can make sure no one has to choose between their health and hunger. 💙
Food Distributions:  S.A.F.E. Food Pantry serves clients with food allergies. We distribute gluten-free and allergy-friendly food on the 3rd Saturday of the month from 10am-noon at the NonProfit Collaborative located at 9770 Patuxent Woods Drive in Columbia, MD.  If you or a member of your household experience food allergies, you may register for our services during the monthly distribution.

Pickups at the NonProfit Collaborative during the week following the monthly distribution may be arranged.  All items will be prebagged. 

New and current clients can also receive gluten-free and allergy-friendly pre-bagged food from SAFE at the Howard County Food Bank. You must be registered with both the Food Bank and SAFE. You may complete both registrations on site. Please ask for your S.A.F.E. Food Pantry bag when you check-in to visit the Howard County Food Bank.

Remember, before consuming any items, you are responsible for checking all food labels to ensure each item is safe for your household.

Who We Are
Serving qualified residents of Blacksburg and McCoy, the Blacksburg Interfaith Food Pantry is a joint ministry of over twenty-five faith-based communities in Blacksburg. We are also supported by individuals, organizations, and clubs that are members of our community.
About Us
Hours
Mon. and Tues. 4:00 PM – 5:30 PM
Wed. and Thur.  10:00 AM – 11:30 AM
Fridays – Closed

 

The Blacksburg Interfaith Food Pantry is closed on major holidays: Memorial Day, July 4th, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve and Christmas Day, New Year’s Eve, and New Year’s Day. Check the Blacksburg Interfaith Food Pantry FB Page in case of inclement weather.
Contact Us
Needs
The Blacksburg Interfaith Food Pantry relies on cash and food from faith-based communities, individuals, and other organizations.
Needs

During the COVID-19 crisis, eligibility screening has been suspended. If you need food, just bring proof of residency in our service area (greater Blacksburg and McCoy). You will be registered and can come once a month for food.

To sign up to volunteer at the BIFP please visit the link below

WELCOME!

We at Saint Luke Lutheran Church believe that every person is a child of God and God’s unconditional love, grace and mercy is there for all people. Every person has worth as an individual and every person is entitled to dignity and respect.

We publicly welcome people of any race, nationality, age, ability, sexual orientation, gender identity, and gender expression into the community of this congregation, and we encourage everyone to share their faith, gifts and talents in worship, service, study, leadership, and fellowship.

We commit to remove all barriers that exclude people from full and active participation in this congregation.

We will continually strive to eliminate prejudice from our own thoughts and actions and discourage prejudice by others at every opportunity. We strive to promote racial equity in the congregation and wider community.

Welcome one another, therefore, as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God, proclaims Holy Scripture, Romans 7:15. Let us indeed welcome one another as Jesus Christ has first welcomed us.

Statement of Intentional Welcome – Adopted, Congregation Council, November 2025

Welcome To AfriThrive Inc.
Creating Thriving Families and Promoting Culturally Relevant Food

AfriThrive is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Maryland and Washington D.C., committed to building vibrant, resilient communities through food security, education, cultural connection, and sustainable development. 

News & Stories
Donate Today
AfriThrive Events
Volunteer
At the heart of AfriThrive is a vibrant network of volunteers, donors, partners, and community leaders who believe in food justice and sustainability.

AfriThrive mobilizes, connects, and empowers communities with culturally rooted, sustainable solutions that support family wellness and economic stability. Our programs are designed to uplift communities and foster lasting change.

Expore Our Programs

very purchase supports access to healthy, culturally relevant food, hands on gardening education, and Food as Medicine programming for families across Montgomery, Prince George’s, and Baltimore Counties

The AfriThrive Store is a simple way to show what you care about. A mug in the morning. A t shirt that starts a conversation. A tote bag that carries groceries home. Most importantly, each purchase helps AfriThrive expand food access and community wellness.

Shop The AfriThrive Store
We’re Hiring: Bilingual Community Resource Navigator (Korean/English)
April 6, 2026  No Comments

Language access is food access. AfriThrive is expanding its team to reach Korean-speaking families in Montgomery County with the dignity-centered, culturally rooted support they deserve.

Read More »
Celebrate Earth Day Where It Matters Most: In the Soil
April 3, 2026  No Comments

Food has always been more than sustenance. It carries memory, culture, and care. At AfriThrive, we believe that building healthier families starts with reconnecting people to the foods that nourish them in every sense of the word.

Foundations for Nutritional Family Foods | AfriThrive Food Is Medicine Webinar Series
March 26, 2026  No Comments

Food has always been more than sustenance. It carries memory, culture, and care. At AfriThrive, we believe that building healthier families starts with reconnecting people to the foods that nourish them in every sense of the word.

Read More »

AfriThrive is proud to collaborate with a network of mission-driven organizations, funders, and community allies who believe in the power of cultural connection, food equity, and immigrant empowerment. These partnerships help us expand access to culturally relevant food, support African immigrant families, and grow sustainable community initiatives in Montgomery, Baltimore, and Prince George’s Counties.

Together, we are building stronger, healthier communities rooted in culture, driven by compassion, and united by purpose.

 

Interested in partnering with AfriThrive? Contact us to explore collaboration opportunities.

Download Our Strategic Plan

Maryland
 
 
 
 

When I first came to Maryland, I struggled to find food that felt like home. AfriThrive not only gave us fresh African vegetables but also helped me learn how to grow them myself. They didn’t just feed my family—they gave us roots in a new place

Maryland
 
 
 
 

I signed up to volunteer at a food distribution and left with a new sense of purpose. AfriThrive is more than a nonprofit - it’s a family. Watching neighbors connect over shared heritage and fresh food was powerful. I’m proud to be part of the movement

Ohio

B.R.I.D.G.E.S.

Barnabas Renewal Initiative Developing Grace Enriching Systems

BRIDGES is a non-profit ministry that was launched by Manna Bible Baptist Church.

Its initiatives reflect Manna’s goal to transform the community for the glory of our Lord, Jesus Christ.

It offers social and economic services that will enrich and enhance lives. The services include:

Educational mentoring including tutoring
Healthcare awareness and resources
Teaching Employment Skills
Constructing/Improving Homes to facilitate a community with affordable residence housing


BRIDGES Community Development Corporation exists in order to engage and partner with the local community to effectuate transformation in socially challenged urban centers through enriching and empowering services and to create sustainable change in the human spirit, all to the Glory of God.

Visit the BRIDGES Website

The Dulles South Food Pantry﻿

We are a community-driven nonprofit serving Dulles residents and the surrounding area. We provide nutritious food, personal supplies, and life-changing social services to our neighbors in need.

Need help with food and other grocery items?

Life can be unpredictable and we understand that everyone faces challenges. If you or your family could use extra support, we are here to help.

WELCOME TO OUR NEW WEBSITE.  WE’LL BE UPDATING THIS SITE OVER TIME, SO PLEASE BE SURE TO VISIT US FREQUENTLY TO DISCOVER WHAT’S NEW.


Here at St. Bernardine’s with Historic St. Peter Claver, we celebrate our faith in Jesus Christ within the African American tradition. We worship with joy and purpose. We support our youth, young adults, elders, and families with children. We engage issues of justice and extend hospitality to all who seek a home in the Roman Catholic Church.

Located in Edmondson Village and Sandtown-Winchester, our parishioners come from throughout the Baltimore Metropolitan Region. Our members are attracted to our life-giving liturgies, characterized by Song, Praise, Word, and Sacrament. They come for our energetic ministry, which nurtures our Catholic faith and reaches out to the local community and the larger world.

As pastor, I extend to you a warm welcome and can assure you that members of our parish family are also ready to welcome you graciously and lovingly. Together we can do great things for God and for one another.

If you are looking for a church home, or are just looking to praise the Lord, we welcome you to St. Bernardine’s with Historic St. Peter Claver– our Home Under the Dome! Please click on “Contact” above, and let us know how we can help you.

Peace and blessings in our Lord Jesus Christ,
Fr. Rich & the St. Bernardine’s with Historic St. Peter Claver Church Family

FOOD PANTRY
1st Saturday of Every Month 8:00AM to 10:00AM

 

The RTBC Food Pantry was founded in 1999 by several ladies from RTBC. It operated from a closet in the church kitchen, serving 12-15 families each month. The pantry currently operates the first Saturday each month serving more than 50 families. More than thirty volunteers serve on the various pantry days. As part of Riva Trace’s benevolence ministry, the Food Pantry receives its funds from offering donations. While a portion of the food we give away each month is free to us from the Anne Arundel County Food Bank, the majority of food given away is purchased at a reduced amount from the Maryland Food Bank.

The RTBC Food Pantry serves specified zip codes within Anne Arundel County area. Individuals and families living in the below listed ZIP codes and qualify for the service, are eligible to enroll for continuing food assistance.

Welcome
Welcome to Holy Comforter Catholic Church

Welcome to the Catholic Church of the Holy Comforter. We are a vibrant parish community located in Historic Downtown Charlottesville, Virginia.

View Mass Times
Fr's Message
A Welcome Message from Our Pastor, Fr. Joseph Mary Lukyamuzi:

Welcome to Holy Comforter Roman Catholic Parish Website. I am delighted you found your way to our Parish Family. I hope you enjoy this short time of getting to know our parish community better. Thank you for your interest and many blessings to you and your loved ones. I strongly believe that as you browse through the different ministries and activities that take place at this parish, you will be inspired to live out your faith in a more profound way.

Photo credit: Aaron Watson Photography

Mission Statement
Our Mission Statement

We, the members of Holy Comforter Catholic Church, united in faith by God, nourished by Jesus through the Word and the Eucharist and guided by the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, believe that we are called to worship, formation, service and evangelization within our parish, our local communities and all those in need.

Prayer
“Come Holy Spirit, enkindle your love into the hearts of the Faithful”

— Father Joseph Mary Lukyamuzi

Photo credit: Aaron Watson Photography

News at Holy Comforter

Keep up to date with the latest announcements and events at Holy Comforter.

Announcements
Events
March - Saint Joseph
The Month of March is Dedicated to Saint Joseph

He is the icon of God the Father—silent but active and perfectly providing for the needs of all who call on him. The Church constantly invokes the protection of St. Joseph, admonishing us to go to “go to Joseph” (Gen. 41:55). Let us ask his intercession for the Church and all men who exercise natural or spiritual fatherhood.

Thank you for stopping by! Whether you are checking us out for the first time on this website or following up after a recent visit to one of our gatherings, we thank you. You are always welcome at Harvest! Our services are on Sundays at                                   8:00 am, 9:30 am & 11:15 am. 
Learn more about us and don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions at all.
UPCOMING EVENTS
Jump right in to all that is going on at Harvest!
GO TO UPCOMING EVENTS
OUR MINISTRIES
Kids

Our Kids team is dedicated to helping children encounter God, have fun, and feel loved through engaging, age-appropriate, Bible-based programs that point to new life in Jesus. Jan Chapdelaine oversees childcare for infants through age 5, while Children’s Pastor Bill Sparkman leads KidzTown for ages 6–12. All staff and volunteers are background-checked and interviewed to ensure a safe environment. For more information or to get involved with TotzTown, KidzTown, or KidzClub, contact Pastor Bill at bsparkman@harvestva.org or Jan at childcarecoord@harvestva.org.

Youth

Harvest Youth is a place for students in 6th–12th grade to encounter the Holy Spirit, build lasting friendships, and pursue Jesus together in a fun and meaningful way. Our heart is to see this generation wholeheartedly seeking after God. Join us on Wednesday nights at 7:00 PM in the Student Ministries Center for a time of worship, connection, and growth. Bring a friend—or your whole crew—and be part of what God is doing! For updates on events, small groups, and Sunday nights, follow us on Instagram @harvestvayouth or contact Youth Pastor Skye Grishaw at youth@harvestva.org.

MomCo

MomCo is a welcoming community for moms of young children to find connection, encouragement, and support in every season of motherhood. We meet on the first and third Tuesdays of each month at 9:30 AM, creating a space to build friendships, grow in faith, and be refreshed alongside other moms who understand the journey. For more information or to get connected, contact Brittney Cavallaro at theharvestmomco@gmail.com.

Small Groups

You were never meant to walk your faith journey alone—real growth happens in community. That’s why we offer Small Groups: welcoming, diverse gatherings that meet throughout the week for connection, prayer, encouragement, and Bible study. There’s a place for you to belong and grow. Click here to learn more and find a group that fits you.

Worship & Media Team

If you have a heart for worship and are gifted as a singer or musician (keyboard, drums, bass, or electric guitar), prayerfully consider joining the Worship Team—contact Worship Director Rebekah Thomson at rthomson@harvestva.org or click here to apply. If you’re passionate about making worship look and sound excellent, the Media Ministry would love to have you—reach out to Media Director David Stump at media@harvestva.org or click here to apply.

House of Blessing

The House of Blessing is a powerful outreach ministry making a real difference in our community—bringing hope, help, and practical support to those in need. Each week, hundreds of families are served through food distribution, along with opportunities for financial assistance during difficult seasons. Click here to learn more and view food distribution hours. If you’d like to be part of what God is doing and serve with us, call the church office at (757) 547-7717.

WELCOME TO THE SAMARITAN COMMUNITY

Welcome to The Samaritan Community, a non-profit 501(c)3 organization in Baltimore City, Maryland. We provide assistance to meet the needs of families and individuals who are in crisis and wish to improve their lives. Our program is rooted in respect, hope, and healing.

We are a small organization with a BIG impact. Our Food Pantry, Clothing & Household Goods "Shop", Individual & Group Counseling, Case Management, Emergency Financial Assistance, and Enrichment Programs enable our members to face both short-term crises and long-term challenges.

By forming connections and a community within Samaritan Community, our members are empowered to transform and strengthen their lives. Ultimately, these stronger individuals and families help create a stronger community in Baltimore and beyond. 

For information about Samaritan Community or ways to get involved with us, call
443-438-9286.

Baltimore, MD 21217

The Compassion Center
Upcoming Events
Donate

Meeting Needs – Creating Community – Transforming Lives

Meeting Needs

We address food insecurity through our twice-weekly food distributions at The Compassion Center. Additional physical needs are met through our programs: baby essentials, household goods, and warm referrals to trusted partners across our network.

Creating Community

Community grows through consistent presence and genuine connection. We build relationships that create belonging, where neighbors are known by name, not processed through a system. Independence emerges from being seen and supported.

Network Partners

We are cultivating a robust network of service providers offering coordinated care. Together, we address food access, mental health, addiction, workforce development, family support, and more. Individuals thrive through whole-person, networked partnerships.

What Our Volunteers Say

Hear from the people who make our mission possible.

"My experience was great interacting with people. I love all the volunteers there... Enjoying great people around you is so much fun. 🙏 🥰"

— Shirley P.

"The people I served with were great and I felt like I made an impact."

— Ericka B.

"I enjoyed the prep and meeting new people, also being hands-on to accomplish the goal of getting the food out."

— Don P.

FOOD PANTRY

The Welborne Community Food Pantry is a partnership of community churches and groups including St. Matthew’s Episcopal, All Saints Episcopal, Good Shepherd UMC, Westhampton UMC, Trinity UMC, Derbyshire Baptist, Gayton Kirk Presbyterian, Saint Mary’s Catholic Church, River Road Baptist, Cut the Clutter RVA, Tuckahoe YMCA, and The Period Patch.

We open for all people in need on Mondays from 5 pm to 6:30 and is a drive through service. Clients are welcome to visit twice a month.

The Pantry will be closed Easter Monday, April 6th and Memorial Day, May 25th.

DONATIONS:

We are thrilled to have the support of our community. We accept shelf stable donations 24 hours a day in a drop off bin at the end of the handicapped ramp.

If you have a large donation contact the pantry director to coordinate drop off. Financial donations can be made by clicking here. If you have questions about the pantry email us here: pantry@welbornechurch.org

Welcome

 

Welcome!  Emmanuel United Methodist Church offers a  warm welcome, a traditional worship service with a staffed nursery, regular fellowship and Christian education opportunities for all ages, special seasonal events, and a place to belong within a caring family of believers.  Worship with us on Sunday mornings, 10:30 a.m.  We are located at                  401 N. Main Street in Amherst, VA.

 

 

 

Please visit our Events Page to see the full schedule of worship and activities.

WELCOME TO THE

St. Columba Ecumenical Ministries, Inc.
Providing support to the less fortunate since 1987.

LEARN MORE OUR HISTORY

Who We Are

St. Columba Ecumenical Ministries, Inc. is a 501 (c) 3 nonprofit organization dedicated to helping those who have fallen on hard times. Our organization has been providing support to the less fortunate since 1987.

Learn More

Our Mission

It is our mission to operate an ecumenical ministry in response to the Biblical Mandate to minister to persons in need, whether these needs are economic, social or spiritual. The ministry is a direct, hands on ministry recognizing the integrity of individuals and working with them where they are. The ministry is indirect, working with churches and other organizations to establish activities that address people's needs.

Our Programs

St. Columba has a variety of programs that serve the homeless and less fortunate in the Hampton Roads community, including a full list of regularly occurring programs, as well as special programs offered throughout the year.

Day Center


The Day Center provides a safe shelter for the community we serve to take care of their most essential needs. Guests can get showers, sandwiches, coffee, and other snacks.

Learn More

Food Pantry


Our food pantry provides households with enough food for up to three days. We take into consideration restrictions clients may have on what they can eat or prepare.

Learn More

Housing Assessment


Clients facing a housing crisis should call the Housing Crisis Hotline at 757-587-4202 for assessment information.

Learn More

Prescriptions


Funded through private foundation monies, this program is specifically for homeless individuals in Norfolk who are in need of assistance with prescription medications.

Learn More

Vision/Dental


Vision & Dental Program Funded through private foundation monies, this program is specifically for homeless individuals in Norfolk who are in need of emergency dental work (fillings and extractions) & eye exams.

Learn More

Special Programs


Easter Baskets, School Supplies, Thanksgiving Baskets, Christmas Baskets. For Christmas, we provide food similar to Thanksgiving, but also coordinate a gift program. Join our mission to help others.

Learn More

Give Donations

You have two hands. One to help yourself, the second to help others.

DONATE NOW

Subscribe

Sign up for our newsletter for updates and opportunities to support our mission here at St. Columba Ecumenical Ministries!

Pratt Free Market

In the United States, 1 in every 8 people face hunger. Food insecurity affects 42 million people and impacts every county in America, and libraries are increasingly becoming more involved in helping to fight hunger in the communities they serve. The Pratt Free Market (PFM) aims to create a safe space for the community through food. Our goal is to help address the issue of food insecurity in Baltimore City and alleviate hunger in the local community. PFM is a judgment-free, accessible, modern, and bright, FREE grocery store located at the Southeast Anchor Library. PFM is stocked with produce, dry goods, and more by generous local partners. The market serves as a hub for food and nutrition based community programming.

Schedule

Each customer will be allowed to fill one bag per visit. If possible, we strongly encourage you to bring your own reusable tote or grocery bag.

A free ticket is required for admission— see below for details.

As we approach the end of the year, our team has made some necessary adjustments to the Pratt Free Market schedule to ensure we can continue offering this service. Moving forward the market will be open at the Southeast Anchor Library as supplies last:

Every Friday (except Fourth Fridays, see Pantry on the Go! information below): 2:00 PM – 5:00 PM

Every Third Wednesday of the Month: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM

Every Fourth Friday of the Month (Pantry on the Go!): 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM  

We’re also excited to continue our partnership with the Department of Social Services (DSS) as part of our ongoing efforts to better serve the community. DSS will be on-site every third Wednesday in conjunction with the market, to assist community members with Food Stamp (SNAP) enrollment and benefits status inquiries. 

Please note that the Pratt Free Market will not be open during scheduled Library closures and holidays.

The Pratt Free Market will receive food from accredited partners including Plantation Park Heights, Leftover Love, Benbow Concession Services, and Moon Valley Farms.

Pantry on the Go!

On the 4th Friday of every month, the Pratt Free Market will also serve as a Pantry on the Go! location in partnership with the Maryland Food Bank, giving away free produce in a farmers’ market-style setup outside the library. Available beginning at 10 am, while supplies last. Tickets are not required for Pantry on the Go! distribution. There is no regular Friday distribution on Pantry on the Go! days.

Upcoming Pantry on the Go! Dates
March 27, 2026
April 24, 20246
Tickets

The Pratt Free Market operates on a numbered ticket system to ensure a fair and organized shopping experience for all visitors.

Wednesday	Ticket distribution 8 am — sellout
Friday (except fourth Friday—see below*)	Ticket distribution 10 am — sellout

Tickets are given out on a first-come, first-served basis. Each ticket is numbered and secures a place in line for market entry. If you miss your place in line or need assistance, please see the guest attendant at the entrance for help.

We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to serving you!

*Tickets are not required for Pantry on the Go! distribution.

The Pratt Free Market will receive food from accredited partners including Plantation Park Heights, Leftover Love, Benbow Concession Services, and Moon Valley Farms.

Support the Pratt Free Market
Give

Your gifts provide critical support for Library initiatives that serve our community. An online donation form is available for those wishing to support the Pratt Free Market.

Volunteer

If you are interested in becoming a volunteer for PFM, please complete the interest form below:

Volunteer Interest Form (English)

Volunteer Interest Form (Spanish)

Community Partners

If you are interested in partnering with PFM, please send us an email at prattfreemarket@prattlibrary.org.

Special thanks and appreciation to our partners: Maryland Food Bank, Plantation Park Heights, Moon Valley Farms, Leftover Love, and Benbow Concessions!

For more information about the Pratt Free Market, please contact prattfreemarket@prattlibrary.org.

For over 30 years Bethesda Cares has worked to raise the level of care for individuals experiencing homelessness throughout Montgomery County.
Learn
Click here to find out how you can help us make homelessness a distant memory.
Donate
We can’t do this without you. Join us today and help us support our most vulnerable neighbors.
Participate
What We Do

Street Outreach

We don’t wait for our population of individuals experiencing homelessness to come to us; we go out to them where they are. By meeting them where they live our outreach workers are able to develop meaningful relationships with their clients so we can help them find a home.

Supportive Housing

Our goal is not to manage homelessness, but to end it. Our hope for every one of our clients experiencing homelessness is to place them into permanent supportive housing, where they will have a home to call their own forever. Our staff work with these individuals to help them obtain housing subsidies, and move into housing units they can call their own.

Critical Time Intervention

Once a client experiencing homelessness agrees to accept housing, we work with them to ease the transition back into normal society, through our Critical Time Intervention Program. Our CTI staff work individually with these clients to help them move into their housing, and stay in touch with them over the course of the first year of their housing.

Upcoming Events
See the Latest Events
Become a VOLUNTEER

Our dedicated volunteers are essential to our ability to provide our clients with a wide array of services. We would love to have you join us; there are lots of ways you can help.

About Us

 The Verona Community Food Pantry (VCFP) is 150+ volunteers drawn together by their commitment to provide food to those in need in Augusta County, Staunton, and Waynesboro, Virginia. We are an independent all-volunteer, 501(c)3 nonprofit corporation. 

Our Mission

  Our mission at Verona Community Food Pantry (VCFP) is to help provide food to those in need.

Our Hours

 Monday, Wednesday and Thursday 9:00 am to 11:00 am

Tuesday 4:30 pm to 5:45 pm

Donations

 A general donation for purchasing food and general/administrative expenses is always a welcome gift. Please make checks payable to VCFP and send to 

Verona Community Food Pantry

P. O. Box 187, Verona, VA 24482.

FOOD PANTRY
APPOINTMENTS
Schedule Your Food Pantry Appointment

For residents of Chesterfield, Dinwiddie, Prince George, Colonial Heights, Petersburg, and Hopewell, CCHASM schedules and coordinates appointments each week at 15 local food pantries. Appointments are based on availability by service area.

 

​Traditional food is provided; however, there is an opportunity to submit requests for those with dietary restrictions (i.e., restrictions versus preferences that are subject to availability).

Request Your Appointment 
Phone Call

 Call our Client Services team at 804-796-2749 Monday through Wednesday, 8:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. (Closed for major holidays.)

An appointment confirmation date/time will be provided.

Register Online

Complete the Food Pantry Appointment Request Form. Requests are processed Monday through Thursday, 8:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.

An appointment confirmation date/time will be provided.

Program Rules
Appointment Frequency

Patrons may receive one food pantry appointment per month.

Confirmation

Appointments are not confirmed until you receive a verbal or emailed confirmed appointment date/time.

Cancellation

Please call 804-796-2749 at least 24 hours in advance if you need to cancel or reschedule your appointment.

Unfortunately, food pantry appointments are limited and files are noted for those who consistently miss appointments or cancel with short notice.

Relief Starts Here
Connecting Communities to Hope, Access, Support, & Meals

WELCOME TO THE COMMUNITY CHURCH AT OCEAN PINES
WORSHIP SERVICES OUR MINISTRIES

Welcome to the website of Community Church at Ocean Pines. Thank you for your interest in this community of Faith of the United Methodist Church. The ministries of Community Church at Ocean Pines are designed and planned to help persons deepen their Faith, worship God together, and live out our Faith in service and love. There are a wide variety of ministries and opportunities to be involved. Please contact the church and pastor to learn more. Again, welcome and thank you for your interest.

Our Worship Services

Informal Worship
SUNDAYS | 8:00 AM

Contemporary Worship
SUNDAYS | 9:15 AM

Traditional Worship
SUNDAYS | 10:30 AM

Worship

Looking for a new place to worship? Our entire congregation would like to officially invite you to the Community Church of Ocean Pines. We are a vast community who worship the Lord through our informal services, contemporary services, traditional services and special church services. We also worship through events, traditional and contemporary worship music.

LEARN MORE
Connect

In these modern times, you'll find the spiritual connection you desire here at the Community Church of Ocean Pines. We love to connect with you in person. Our campus is designed to accommodate all age groups. We take great pride in being a comfortable space of worship. You can also choose to connect with us online through our Facebook and our YouTube channel.

LEARN MORE
Ministries

Our ministries here at the Community Church of Ocean Pines work diligently to provide a safe and secure environment of worship for all age groups. Our adult groups, adult ministries, children groups, children ministries and youth missions were created to nurture your growing relationship with Jesus Christ. Family education ministries are very important to our church.

LEARN MORE
Give

Remember the old adage we learned as children: "It's better to give than to receive"? We welcome charitable donations from congregation members and beyond. However, giving can come in many forms. You can volunteer your time and talents to spreading the word of Jesus Christ. You can also donate items directly to the church that another members might need.

LEARN MORE

Food Distribution
Updated on February 16, 2023  by baradmin

At our Radford location, we provide free food boxes every Tuesday from 11 am to 4 pm. Anyone can sign up for a box. We share donated food from individuals, groups, and stores.  Customers use our online signup which allows them to not only select a pick up time, but let us know the types of foods they do and don’t like in their box.  To get signed up, call 540-980-4111.

At HOPE SMC, we empower individuals and families in St. Mary’s County to overcome challenges, achieve stability, and realize their full potential. THE SON'SPOT
CHRISTIAN FELLOWSHIP CENTER
BIBLE STUDY PRAISE MUSIC PRAYER & FELLOWSHIP
Welcome!

The Son’Spot is a missionary outreach of the Body of Christ that emphasizes salvation and victory in Jesus. From June to September we gather everyday (except Wednesay) at 10am for sharing the Word and prayer, then complete work projects, and at 5pm go on the boardwalk for outreach ministry. We gather again at 8pm for praise, worship, Word, prayer, fellowship, and minstry. Additionally, the food ministry includes dinner on Thursdays at 5:30pm, and breakfast on Mondays, Tuesdays, Fridays, and Saturdays at 7:30am. We also deliver food bags and minister to people in their homes each week.

During the winter we meet Thursday, and Saturday evenings at 7pm for Bible, prayer, and fellowship. On Sunday Mornings at 10am we gather for worship, ministry, and fellowship. We also have an outreach meal on Thursdays at 5pm and we deliver food bags each week to many families. And we host a Bible study each week at the county jail. Our purpose in all activities is to lift up the name of Jesus. People are born again, encouraged, and set free. Praise the Lord!

For God loved the world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not die but have eternal life. John 3:16

I alone know the plans I have for you, plans to bring you prosperity and not disaster, plans to bring about the future you hope for. Jeremiah 29:11

Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God. Matthew 4:4

Nourish yourselves spiritually on the Words of faith. 1 Timothy 4:6

LEARN MORE
JESUS AT THE BEACH

32nd Annual - July 27, 28, & 29, 2026

MORE DETAILS VIEW ALL EVENTS
FEBRUARY / MARCH 2026
February 01, 2026

Dear Son’Spot Family,

Praise the Lord. It is a blessing and privilege to be in God’s presence and to see Him manifest. That’s our desire each time we meet, to invite the Lord’s presence and for His Holy Spirit to manifest through us however He wants. We meet Thursday and Saturday evenings at 7pm for Bible, prayer, and fellowship. On Sunday mornings at 10am we gather for worship, ministry, and fellowship. We also have an outreach meal on Thursdays at 5pm and we deliver food bags each week to people’s homes. Our purpose in all activities is to lift up the name of Jesus. (John 12:32) God continues to change lives, one soul at a time. Praise the Lord!

About us

Success Stories

Services/Programs

Recipes

Impact Reports

Providing Nutritious Food
and
Essential Support to Families in need

Ensuring over 2000 individuals per month have access to nutritious food through our weekly food distribution events.

Providing clothing, hygiene items, and household necessities to holistically support families.

About us
Services/Programs
Food Distribution:

Monday

9am-12pm

Our food distribution service is at the heart of the Henry County Food Pantry’s mission. Eligible participants can conveniently receive a box of staple goods, fresh produce, and frozen meats through our drive-thru service. Our dedicated volunteers warmly welcome and guide visitors, embodying our commitment to not only providing meals but also nurturing dignity, compassion, and hope for a brighter future.
Clothes Closet

Monday

9am-12pm

We recognize that access to clothing and household essentials is as crucial as access to food. Our clothes closet, operating alongside our food distribution service, provides additional support to individuals and families in need. Stocked with a diverse range of clothing, hygiene products, household items, and even pet food, it ensures our clients have the essentials to thrive.
Referrals Program

Scheduled

time needed

Referred by partnering companies, individuals needing extra support can access our referral closet, stocked with essential household items, clothing for all ages, and toys for children and infants. Through local partnerships and volunteer support, we aim to empower individuals and families on their journey toward self-sufficiency and stability.
Learn more
Photos
Videos
Volunteers and Donations

Volunteers are the backbone of the Henry County Food Pantry, driving our mission to combat hunger and uplift our community. Their dedication and hard work are essential to ensuring our operations run smoothly, from sorting donations to assisting clients during food distribution. Regardless of background or experience, volunteers play a vital role in making a tangible impact on the lives of individuals and families in need. As a volunteer, you’ll join a diverse and passionate team united by a common goal: serving others with compassion and empathy.

At the Henry County Food Pantry, volunteering isn’t just about giving time; it’s about making meaningful connections and experiencing the joy of making a difference firsthand. Whether you’re a student, professional, or retiree, there’s a place for you on our team. Through volunteering, you’ll gain valuable skills, forge lasting friendships, and contribute to a cause that truly matters. Join us in the fight against hunger and be a part of a community dedicated to creating positive change, one volunteer shift at a time.

The Housing Partnership serves communities in both Baltimore City and Baltimore County. Home buyers should seek services from the housing counseling agency where they are most likely to purchase a home.
Our Community Relations program has the most far-reaching positive impact on all of our member associations in the Northeast.
We offer confidential, compassionate, and evidence-based care to support recovery and mental well-being. Whether you need treatment for substance use, mental health, or both, we are here to help. We serve both adults and adolescents in Baltimore City and the surrounding areas.

Welcome to Rockville HELP
	
Rockville HELP is a team of volunteers on duty seven days a week to extend a listening ear, provide financial assistance, deliver emergency food, and offer clients additional referrals when needed in Rockville, Maryland. We try our best to help every Rockville resident who contacts us seeking support.

Financial (rent/utility) assistance is currently referral-only. Food assistance continues as normal, with no referral needed.


	
Our Service Area
We serve Rockville residents in an area bounded by Redland Road, Needwood Road, and Muncaster Mill Road on the north; Norbeck Road and the Rock Creek Trail on the east, Randolph Road and Montrose Road on the south, and Rockville City limits on the west.
Our service area is within the thick red border. Here are some of the nearby HELP organizations:
Gaithersburg Help:  301-216-2510  gaithersburghelp.org
Olney Help:               301-774-4334  olneyhelp.org
Bethesda Help:        301-365-2022  bethesdahelp.org 
MUM (Mid-County United Ministries): 301-929-8675  mumhelp.org
Our Services
Request Help
	
Donate
We deliver food to your home and offer limited help with rent and utility payments and prescriptions to those in need. (In exceptional cases, we try to help with other needs as feasible.) Please call (301-564-0800) and leave a message to begin. A volunteer will call you back soon. (Please note that since our volunteers use their own phones, the call back might come from a private number or a non-local area code.) Due to limited funds, financial assistance is currently only available to those referred by a social worker. Food assistance continues as normal, with no referral needed.

Volunteer
We do not have an office or physical location other than our food room. We work from our homes, using our own phones and vehicles.  Some give a few hours a month, others are on call for a week or more, but we're all committed for the long term. Please visit our Volunteer page for more information or contact us if you are interested. 

	
As a registered 501(c)(3) charity organization, all contributions to Rockville Help are tax-deductible. Please visit our Donate page for more information. 

Contact
To request help, please call our phone number: 301-564-0800. Our email address and Facebook account are not continuously monitored. For general inquires, contact us at rockvillemdhelp@gmail.com.

Rockville HELP Volunteers at Work

Food Pantry

The food pantry at Emmanuel Church is a continuing outreach project supported through donations of cash, food, and volunteer time. We believe that our food pantry is one way our parish can answer Christ’s call to love our neighbors as ourselves.

​

Each month we distribute large bags of food to over 150 individuals. This number does not show the faces of hunger in our community. The people we serve, who come through our doors, are the poor, elderly, the disabled, the mentally ill, the infirm, men and women who have lost their jobs or missed a paycheck, or facing a real financial crisis. Food insecurity is a source of stress and desperation. With donations from our parish and community, we are able to help the people who depend on us. The needs of the Food Pantry are continuous and we are grateful for your donations.

 

Volunteers are always welcome to help with this ministry. Please call the church office, (540) 434-2357, for more information about volunteering or visiting the church pantry.

The Corner Cupboard Pantry is open on Mondays and Tuesdays from 10:00 am - 12:00 pm

For the Daily News Record article (2/18/2022), click here.

Since we follow the same schedule as the public school system, we will be closed when school is closed as well as during inclement weather

Hello
Welcome

We are a church family that believes first and foremost in loving our neighbors. Here you will find a loving and accepting church that will help you tackle the tough challenges of life whether you are a longtime follower of Christ or even if you are not yet a believer

FIND OUT MORE
FLOWERING THE CROSS EASTER 2026
OUR FOOD CLOSET IS OPEN AND READY TO HELP
If you need help we're here

First Baptist Church of Silver Spring, The Church Downtown, has been able to keep our Food Closet open, even during the COVID-19 crisis, and we remain open now if you need us.




If you need help making ends meet the Food Closet is open every Saturday from 10am to 12n.  You can access the Food Closet from the Wayne Street entrance.




For more information please call us at 301-754-1100 or email us at info@thechurchdowntown.com





  

THE CHURCH DOWNTOWN
Our Story

n 1924 Silver Spring was a tiny little village made up mostly of apple orchards and dirt paths.  It was in this setting that 14 people met on October 22, 1924 to establish the Silver Spring Baptist Church.  Since that time the First Baptist Church of Silver Spring has had a presence in downtown helping people in the community to make their lives better through ministry and fellowship.  Our commitment to be a shining presence in today's urban environment is stronger than ever with a brand new building in that same location - the heart of Silver Spring. 




Look here for more about our 100 year anniversary!




In January of 2023 we welcomed Interim Senior Pastor, Rev. Phillip Moschenrose to lead First Baptist Church of Silver Spring into the future.




You can still join us every Sunday at 11am for the weekly Sunday Service at the church building at 828 Wayne Avenue in downtown Silver Spring or right here on our homepage - as a video stream.




We are also affiliated with three other churches who offer Sunday Service at our downtown Silver Spring facility in French/Haitian Creole and Spanish.  For information on the French/Haitian Creole Church please visit:  https://eglisebaptistedelafoi.org and for Spanish language service visit Iglesia Baptista Immanuel at https://www.facebook.com/ibessmd828 or call them at (301) 879-5155.  You can also attend service by Hope of Glory Believers Ethiopian Church.  You can get more information at https://hopeofglorymd.org/about-us/

STAND WITH US - MAKE A DIFFERENCE
Learn More

 #BlackLivesMatter was founded in 2013 in response to the acquittal of Trayvon Martin’s murderer. Black Lives Matter Foundation, Inc is a global organization in the US, UK, and Canada, whose mission is to eradicate white supremacy and build local power to intervene in violence inflicted on Black communities by the state and vigilantes. By combating and countering acts of violence, creating space for Black imagination and innovation, and centering Black joy, we are winning immediate improvements in our lives. 

FIND OUT MORE
COMING UP AT THE CHURCH DOWNTOWN

  

Every Tuesday and Wednesday

Bible Study: 7-8pm  Virtual by way of Zoom. If you are interested in participating in our prayer and devotional session on Tuesdays or Wednesdays, please send your name and contact information to us at info@thechurchdowntown.com for login details. Or you can call the church office for more information.

  

Every Saturday

Food Closet:  The Food Closet is open every Saturday from 10:00am to 12:00pm.  You can come once a month to get food.  Use the Church's main entrance on Wayne Ave.

 
Every Sunday

Bible Study-Sunday Service: Adult and Child Bible Study begin are held at our building in downtown Silver Spring at 828 Wayne Avenue.  Additionally Bible Service is conducted via zoom and the 11am service is streamed right here on our website homepage.  Sunday Worship Service begins at 11am. Church services also in Spanish and French/Haitian Creole.




You can also watch our Sunday services after the fact here on the website as well.  We post all of our services, first on the homepage and then in the past video archives.

General Information & Location

The Wilderness Food Pantry serves area families who are in need. The Food Pantry is operated by  the LOW Lions Club with the LOW Lioness Lions Club. The Pantry building is located on Route 20 in Locust Grove behind and to the right of the Locust Grove Town Center mall. Call (540)518-2440 and select 3 after the prompt for  information. The Wilderness Food Pantry is an equal opportunity provider.  (See full statement.)

Donations

Donations of non-perishable food items may be dropped off in the donation box located in the members lobby of the Holcomb Building (the LOW Clubhouse lobby drop is unavailable during renovations), at the Velona building during the weekly LOW Lions yard sale when open (adjacent to the trash compactor/recycling area) or at the Pantry during open hours (see schedule link above).  Please note that we cannot distribute food items that are past their expiration or best by date. Thank you for your understanding.

WHERE FAITH FINDS HOME, HEARTS FIND HOPE

Praise The Lord my friend, we’re glad that you took the time to visit our website. We would like for you to know that we do not take you for granted and that you are important to us. We hope to see you at one of our services in the near future. When you come to our church, come expecting a dynamic Praise & Worship experience, a Rhema Word from God, and a friendly environment. We are a Bible teaching, Bible preaching, Bible believing Church, and we believe that you are not being the church if you are not affecting the communities that are around you. Therefore, we engage the community with numerous resources such as HEFD Center (The Harvest Emergency Food Distribution Center) where we partner with the Department of Social Services and other agencies to provide food for food deprived families throughout the week; in addition to, providing holiday meals for at least 400 families annually. We also provide Christmas toys for hundreds of families in the Baltimore Metropolitan Area annually with our Harvest Toy Campaign. We host Summer Enrichment Programs that provide academic and social development for inner-city children, as well as employment opportunities for Baltimore City’s youth, through the Baltimore City OED YouthWorks Program.

Empowering the Church Square Community: The Impact of the Mr. Mack Lewis Foundation

As we look back on the last seven years, it is with great pride that I reflect on the transformative work my team and I have achieved through the Mr. Mack Lewis Foundation. Our steadfast mission is to reduce juvenile delinquency and combat community deterioration in the Church Square community. This journey has been nothing short of inspiring, and our collective efforts have borne fruit in ways that resonate deeply within our hearts and the lives of those we serve.

​

Five years ago, we faced a significant challenge when the only grocery store in our community closed its doors, plunging hundreds of low-income families into a food desert. Determined not to let our neighbors go hungry, we established a weekly grocery distribution that has since become one of the most significant free grocery initiatives in Baltimore City. This vital lifeline has ensured that families receive the nourishment they need, fostering resilience and hope in the face of adversity.

​

Our commitment to fostering a sense of community and togetherness goes beyond providing food. We distribute over 350 turkeys each year, ensuring that every family can celebrate Thanksgiving with dignity and joy. Our free Thanksgiving dinners, complete with entertainment, have become cherished events, bringing smiles and warmth to many faces. The festive spirit continues throughout the year with our Christmas and Halloween parties, creating magical moments for the children and reinforcing the bonds that hold our community together.

​

We have also invested in the future through our free summer camp, recognizing the importance of nurturing the next generation. Serving over 50 young people, the camp offers fun and learning and free breakfast and lunch, ensuring our children are well-fed and ready to embrace new experiences.

​

Our partnership with esteemed institutions such as Johns Hopkins and GBMC/Gilchrist has enabled us to provide free health screenings, blood pressure checks, and vaccines at our gym. This crucial access to healthcare ensures that our community remains healthy and informed, a testament to the power of collaboration and shared goals.

Additionally, our collaborations with organizations like Maryland Volunteer Services have brought invaluable services to our community, further enhancing the quality of life for our residents.

The work of the Mr. Mack Lewis Foundation is far from complete. With unwavering dedication and the continued support of our partners, we remain committed to creating a brighter, safer, and more vibrant Church Square. Our journey is one of hope, resilience, and the unyielding belief that we can overcome any obstacle together and create lasting change.

​

​

Gregory Wilkes, Executive Director

Mr. Mack Lewis Foundation 

Our Mission 

The Mr. Mack Lewis Foundation continues to foster the ideals of our founder by striving to reduce violence, juvenile delinquency and the effects of Post Traumatic Syndrome. 

​

We Achieve these goals by offering our members and community a better path via the disciplines of boxing, the arts and academics.   

​​

Mack Lewis Foundation in Action!
Commentary: Mack Lewis Gym inspiring a community through grit and transformation
By Cory McCray

A helping hand for those in need in our community.

GET HELP NOW
DONATE
Our Goal is Simple:

To improve the quality of life for the most vulnerable residents of Western Upper Montgomery County by providing them with direct assistance to meet their basic needs, connecting them to other resources, and advocating on their behalf with the goal of helping them achieve and maintain self-sufficiency.

We provide food, financial assistance for household and medical needs, transportation help, and local access to health and other resources.

How WUMCO helps:
FOOD PANTRY
WUMCO accepts donations from local businesses and residents and runs a food pantry for those in the community who are in need of everyday supplies.
TRANSPORTATION
WUMCO comes to the aid of residents who need rides to medical appointments and social services, and help securing food supplies and medicines.
FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
WUMCO provides financial assistance to help pay household bills such as utility and similar daily living expenses. Recipients must apply and pass a needs assessment analysis.
HEALTHCARE ACCESS
WUMCO works to increase access to healthcare.
ENGLISH AS A SECOND LANGUAGE CLASSES (ESOL)
WUMCO offers free adult English classes
Serving our community for over 60 years
Proudly serving zipcodes: 20837, 20838, 20839 20841, 20842

In the late 1960’s, two dear friends, Jane Stearns and Beulah Harper, joined forces to provide food and rides to those in need in the Western Upper Montgomery County. From this humble start a small non-profit, WUMCO Help, grew. Financial assistance services were added, the pantry was housed in a large garage, and the office pre-empted the living room of the home of Jane and her husband Fred. The organization continued to grow, was incorporated and received non-profit status from the IRS.

MAILING ADDRESS – P. O. BOX 788, ORANGE, VA  22960

(540) 223-6674

EMAIL ADDRESS – LOVEOUTREACHORANGE@GMAIL.COM

***Love Outreach Food Pantry and Clothes Closet are open Wednesdays from 9:00 am to 12 noon and on the third Monday of each month from 5:00 pm to 7:00 pm.
The next Monday opening will be on April 20th.***


OUR PARTNERS
FOOD PANTRY & CLOTHES CLOSET DONATION SUGGESTIONS
Country Time, etc. powdered drink (can be any brand)
Juice/juice boxes
Instant milk
Cereal/Oatmeal
Pancake mix/Syrup
Canned beans: black beans, pinto, garbanzo, kidney, baked, pork n beans, etc.
Canned fruit: applesauce, peaches, pineapple, pears, fruit cocktail, etc.
Canned beef stew, hash, manwich
Canned meat: tuna, chicken, salmon, spam, ham, potted meat, Vienna sausage, etc.
Canned vegetables: mixed vegetables, spinach, asparagus, carrots, whole potatoes, okra, corn, green beans, peas, beets, etc.
Spaghetti, pasta sauce
Tomatoes: stewed, whole, sliced, diced
Soup, Ramen noodles, cup of soup
Jelly, peanut butter
Ketchup, mustard, mayonnaise, salad dressings, spices
Rice, spaghetti, pasta-elbows macaroni, any pasta noodles
Mac & cheese
Dry beans
Baking items--brownie, cake, cookie, muffin mix, etc.
Side dishes--Rice-A-Roni, instant /Scalloped potatoes, etc.
Salt & pepper (we give every 3 months--Nov., Feb., May., Aug.)
We also need Hygiene Products--
Deodorant, feminine hygiene items, diapers, adult briefs, etc.
Tooth paste, toilet paper, soap (we give out each month)
Tooth brushes, shampoo (we give out Nov., Feb, May, Aug.)
Laundry detergent (we give out Dec., March, June, Sept.)
Dish detergent (we give out Jan., April, July, Oct.)

The Clothes Closet is located at the front of the building and is open the same hours as the Food Pantry. Clients may come to the Clothes Closet once a month. Donations of SEASONAL, CLEAN, GENTLY USED clothes, shoes, bedding, blankets, and towels are accepted during open hours and on Tuesdays from 8:00 a.m. to 12 noon. We can only accept NEW, PACKAGED underwear. All items at the Clothes Closet are FREE. We gladly accept monetary donations to purchase new items.

Thank you for any donations you can share!!

Love Outreach is an all volunteer 501C nonprofit organization.  The Food Pantry partners with the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank and is also supported by food and financial donations from the Orange County Community.  The Clothes Closet is also supported by clothing donations and financial donations from the Orange County community.  Donations can be brought to the Food Pantry and Clothes Closet during open hours or on Tuesdays from 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 noon.

Harvest Intercontinental Church is a Christian church made up of families from more than 50 nations, committed to helping people discover God’s love, explore their purpose, and impact their community. We believe that every person is loved by God, and that God desires a personal relationship with them through Jesus. We’re on a mission to bring the message of Jesus to the whole world. Imperfect people are welcome here, because we’re imperfect ourselves. It’s a place where you can experience God’s move in your life, and watch Him move through your life.

- Bishop Darlingston and
Pastor Chrys Johnson, Senior Pastors

METRO DC HISPANIC CONTRACTORS COMMUNITY SERVICES INC

MISSION:

Metro DC Hispanic Contractors Community Services, Inc. (MDCHCCS) is dedicated to empowering low- to moderate-income community members in the Metro DC area by providing essential support in food and shelter security, employment assistance, civic education, and construction safety and job training. Our goal is to foster economic stability, workforce development, and community engagement for individuals and families in the Metro DC area. 

​

VISION

A thriving, self-sufficient community with equitable access to economic opportunities, safe working conditions, and civic participation. 

OUR STORY

As the non-profit arm of the Metro DC Hispanic Contractors Association, we were formed to serve the community and build a brighter future for all of us.

​

​Our impact focuses on construction safety & job training, employment assistance, food & shelter security, and civic education. We accomplish our goals through our community-focused programs through partnerships with local organizations.

Leadership
BUILDING
STRENGTH
BREAKING
BARRIERS
EMPOWERING WOMEN
Subscribe to our newsletter • Don’t miss out!
Email*
Join

I want to subscribe to your mailing list.

OUR VALUES

Empowerment – Providing individuals with the resources, skills, and knowledge needed to achieve self-sufficiency and success. 

​

Equity – Ensuring fair access to opportunities and resources for all members of the Hispanic community. 

​

Collaboration – Building strong partnerships with local organizations, businesses, and government agencies to amplify impact. 

​

Integrity – Operating with transparency, honesty, and accountability in all initiatives and programs. 

​

Safety – Prioritizing workplace safety and ensuring workers receive proper training and protections. 

​

Community Engagement – Encouraging civic participation and fostering a sense of belonging and advocacy within the community. 

​

Sustainability – Implementing long-term strategies to create lasting economic and social change.

MEET THE PASTOR
       PASTOR MICHAEL A. PARKER, II

The Reverend Michael Anthony Parker, II is a native of Baltimore, Maryland. Reverend Parker is a proud graduate of the Baltimore School for the Arts, where he majored in Vocal Music with an emphasis on classical voice and opera. He holds a Bachelor of Arts in English from Coppin State University and a Master of Divinity degree from Wesley Theological Seminary. The eldest of three siblings, Reverend Parker was raised in a household where both having and practicing faith were central and important. Pastor P, as he is affectionately called, accepted his call to ministry at 16 years old and from that time has committed himself to living out his call however and wherever God desires. An ordained elder in The United Methodist Church, Pastor P currently serves as Senior Pastor of Asbury United Methodist Church in Annapolis, Maryland. Asbury Church, at 222 years of continuous ministry, holds the distinction of being the oldest African American church in the entire county. Under pastoral appointment in The United Methodist Church since 2012, Pastor P has served the Church in a variety of ways and places. Prior to serving Asbury Church, Pastor P served as the pastor of Journey United Methodist Church in Temple Hills, MD; Bells United Methodist Church in Camp Springs, MD; United Methodist Church of the Redeemer in Temple Hills, MD; St. John United Methodist Church in Pumphrey, MD; Ames United Methodist Church in Bel Air, MD; St. James United Methodist Church in Jarrettsville, MD; Tabernacle United Methodist Church and New Beginnings Fellowship United Methodist Church, both in Fallston, MD. Reverend Parker’s ministry beyond the local church includes serving on several boards, and committees within The United Methodist Church and in the greater community. He currently serves on the Baltimore-Washington Conference Board of Ordained Ministry and the Baltimore-Washington Conference Episcopacy Committee. He has been blessed to preach and teach across denominational lines, among diverse racial and ethnic groups, and around the world. Embracing John Wesley’s thought that “the world is my parish” and aiming to live out the words of Isaiah 61:4, “They will rebuild the ancient ruins and restore the places long devastated; they will renew the ruin cities that have been devastated for generations,” Reverend Parker’s aim is to bring God glory in all he does and to be a vessel through which God can transform lives.

WELCOME TO ASBURY UMC!

If you love the great hymns of Christian tradition, gospel music sung with great passion, liturgy (sometimes with a twist), biblical preaching, and, most important, warm, friendly, down-to-earth folks, welcome, we’re glad you’ve found us! Whether you’re visiting our fascinating town, new to the area, or just curious about who we are, Welcome! 

We are known to many as “Mother Asbury”, the progenitor of several other African-American congregations in the area. However, we are more than our storied history. We are also a vibrant part of 21st century Annapolis. We are an open, affirming group of people who love Jesus and love our neighbors. All are welcome here. 

We have a small but committed staff and leadership team who make big things happen to benefit our community. Whether it’s our partnership with local arts organizations, working with agencies that support healing and hope for the disadvantaged, or providing space for meetings, gatherings and events, they support and resource with kindness and efficiency. 

Our church’s mission and vision statements say it all. We are geographically and spiritually rooted in the heart of Annapolis. We build, create and share, and provide help and hope -- all to the glory of a loving God. 

We are part of The United Methodist Church. Our region is the Baltimore-Washington Annual Conference which includes Washington, DC, all of Maryland except the Eastern Shore, and the panhandle of West Virginia. 

As part of the United Methodist Church we seek to fulfill the denomination’s mission: “To make disciples of Jesus Christ for the transformation of the world.”

OUR CHURCH HISTORY

Asbury United Methodist Church was officially established in 1838. That makes us the oldest African-American congregation in the City of Annapolis and Anne Arundel County. To learn more about the history of our church, visit our church history page.

Saint Vincent de Paul Society

24 hour help line 410-228-4770 ext 104
Food Pantry open the 1st and 3rd Thursday mornings of each month from 10 AM till 12 Noon (weather permitting)
P. O. Box 218
Cambridge, MD 21613
410-228-4770 Parish Office


 
The St. Vincent de Paul Society is volunteers, Catholic men, and women who take seriously the words of Jesus:


“You have my Father’s Blessing:
          when I was hungry, you gave me food
          when I was thirsty, you gave me drink
          when I was a stranger, you welcomed me
          when I was naked, you clothed me
          when I was ill, you came to my help
   Whenever you do this…you do it to me.”

Our volunteers give freely of their time and our purpose is to assist the poor and needy in Dorchester County. There are about 15 members who respond to calls for assistance that come through the Church or Social Service Agencies. What sets us apart from other volunteer groups is that we go to the homes of those needing assistance in pairs to better assess their needs and provide any help we can.


Our funds come from the poor box at Church, individual donations, and member contributions.
We also have an annual fundraiser to help raise money.
You can be assured that 100% of all contributions to our mission are used to give comfort to the poorest of the poor.

Inspired by Gospel values, the Society of St. Vincent de Paul, a Catholic lay organization, leads women and men to join together to grow spiritually by offering person-to-person service to the needy and suffering in the tradition of its founder, Blessed Frederic Ozanam, and patron, St. Vincent de Paul.   

As a reflection of the whole family of God, members, who are known as Vincentians, are drawn from every ethnic and cultural background, age group and economic level.  Vincentians are united in an international society of charity by their spirit of poverty, humility and sharing, which is nourished by prayer and reflection, mutually supportive gatherings and adherence to a basic Rule.                    

"Organized locally, Vincentians witness God's love by embracing all works of charity and justice.  The Society collaborates with other people of goodwill in relieving need and addressing its causes, making no distinction in those served, because, in them, Vincentians see the face of Christ."
                                                               United States Manual of the Society of St. Vincent de Paul 1999

Food Pantry

 

Our Food Pantry is open on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Saturdays from 9:30AM – 12:00PM. It can be reached by telephone during these hours at 410-287-2230.

The Food Pantry is open to all neighbors who need food. Unfortunately, monetary assistance is not available.

Neighbors who desire food for the first time need to:

Present a current photo ID
Show Cecil County residency
Complete a Food Pantry application
Limit visits to once a month

Our volunteers collect and donate food and staff the Food Pantry. We also receive donations and assistance from generous community members, churches and civic organizations. We are grateful to Bethel Lutheran Church, North East Rotary, and Dexter House for their ongoing support and assistance. If you or your organization would like to help, please call the Food Pantry at 410-287-2230.

Home

Getting There Ride Share – Providing transportation services for seniors in north Harford County and parts of west Cecil County.


The name itself, “Getting There Ride Share”, defines the efforts this group shares. Getting There Ride Share aims to help seniors stay active, maintain independence, connect with others, and participate in more of life’s daily activities. From doctor’s appointments to visits with friends, the program will help those seniors who request the service get to events that make their life meaningful and rewarding. We assist northern Harford County’s age-eligible residents (and parts of western Cecil County residents) by providing a safe, dependable, and reasonably priced supplemental transportation service, utilizing community volunteers and resources.




Helping others is a reward in itself and neighbor-helping-neighbor is the best kind of volunteerism.  Check out “Get Involved” for more information.  We are now accepting applications for both volunteer drivers and coordinators.

WHO WE ARE

Calvary is a family of believers called to PRAISE God in worship, GROW as disciples of Jesus, and LOVE one another and our community, by the power of the Holy Spirit. We are passionate about sharing God’s love, and we seek to do that in worship services, small group studies, fellowship events, and service opportunities.




We’re committed to shining the light of Christ here in the beautiful Shenandoah Valley and throughout the world. We believe that every person is a beloved child of God, and we welcome you to partner with us in mission and ministry!﻿

STAFF & LEADERS
PASTOR: Rev. David Vaughan

﻿Pastor David arrived at Calvary in the summer of 2018.  He is a graduate of the University of Virginia and Duke Divinity School, and he served as a youth minister, camp counselor, and hospital chaplain before becoming a pastor a decade ago. You're likely to find him reading Dr. Seuss, rehearsing lines for a play, training for a marathon, cheering on the Virginia Cavaliers, or humming along with a Bruce Springsteen song, but most importantly he is the husband of April and father of Micah and Anna, a follower of Jesus, and an explorer searching for new ways to share God's love with the world.

 

Pastor David is passionate welcoming people to the church of Jesus Christ, helping them discover God's call for their lives, and walking with them on this journey of faith. If you would like to connect with him or learn more about our church family, drop him a line or sign up for the Calvary Mid-Week Message.

The Episcopal Church Welcomes You.

Welcome to the website of Church of the Advent, Norfolk. We hope the website provides easy access to the information about us that you are seeking. Most of all, we hope you will be led to join us for Sunday worship, fellowship, Christian formation, community outreach, and world mission as we journey together in our quest for the deeper meaning of life through our faith in Jesus Christ.

We strive to reach out in genuine hospitality. We are eager to make you welcome. Our parish congregation represents a wide range of ages and lifestyles – all beloved children of God to whom none is a stranger, all come together to the Table of our Lord.

We seek to be the Church – the Body of Christ serving the world in the name of Christ. We pray you will join us, feel that you are at home, and blessed by your presence at The Church of the Advent.

Church of the Advent is part of the Episcopal Diocese of Southern Virginia.
