Celebrating 60 Years of Service & Impact
Community Assistance Network proudly marks six decades of empowering communities, supporting families, and building brighter futures.
Thank you for being part of our journey!
Community Choice Pantry

Prior to our 2022 renovation and expansion, Community Assistance Network distributed pre-packed food boxes. Today, the Community Choice Pantry allows individuals and families that live in Baltimore County to shop with dignity by selecting their own food in a grocery store–style setting. This model empowers neighbors to choose items their families will eat while considering cultural and religious preferences, dietary restrictions, and chronic health conditions.

​

The Community Choice Pantry provides a one-week supply of healthy food to income-eligible Baltimore County residents, based on USDA food pyramid guidelines. Food is distributed according to household size (1-6+) to ensure families receive an appropriate amount. Neighbors may also receive pet food, household items, hygiene products, and clothing. Item selection may vary based on donations and available inventory. If you have additional needs, please let the pantry staff know, and we will do our best to assist. 

Shopper

1/7
Please bring your own bags and grocery carts. CAN grocery carts must remain on site and cannot be used to transport your groceries home. 

You must sign in by 10:45am or 2:45pm respectively to receive services. We reserve the right to close early if we reach capacity for the day.

 

We strongly encourage you to follow the Community Assistance Network on Facebook and pay close attention to our announcement screen in the lobby as we post valuable information regarding events, closings, and alternative pantry operation hours. The information posted on our Facebook page is up to date and can prevent you from coming to the pantry on a day we are closed or observing alternative distribution hours.

Inclement Weather Delays and Closings

The CAN Pantry follows Baltimore County Schools for ALL closings and delays. If Baltimore County schools are closed due to inclement weather, the pantry will be closed. If Baltimore County Schools are late due to inclement weather, the CAN pantry and main office will open late. Please be advised that there may be inclement weather situations during which county schools do not close or delay, but CAN will. We strongly advise you to call 410-285-4674 or visit our Facebook page (Community Assistance Network) for up-to-date information regarding inclement weather delays or closings before traveling to the Community Choice Pantry. 

 

When we delay opening for inclement weather, the Community Choice Pantry will open at 11 am and we will service neighbors until 2:45 pm.

Food Pantry
About The Parkway Church Food Pantry

Our Food Pantry operates as a direct distribution center in Woodbridge for those in the community who are facing food insecurity. We provide nutritious food to individuals and families in need. Unlike food banks, which store and distribute food to agencies, we directly serve the community to ensure that those facing hunger receive the assistance they need promptly. Our mission aligns with our core belief in restoring relationships with God and others, as we strive to support our neighbors in need.

How To Receive Food

The Food Pantry is located at Parkway Church, 3713 Pennington Lane, Woodbridge, VA 22192.

Monthly Distribution: The 4th Saturday of each month from 10:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m. we host a food distribution event to serve as many families as possible.
Weekly Distribution (as available): When food supplies allow, we will also hold weekly food distributions on Thursday evenings for those needing extra support. Availability will be announced via our text systems, social media or church website.
No pre-registration required
Walk-ins are welcome!
Please bring a valid ID and proof of residency (if available). Services are confidential and open to all regardless of background and we welcome everyone with compassion and respect.
To receive immediate notifications about food distribution dates, text “FOOD” or “COMIDA” to (703) 590-5199.
Ways To Get Involved
We rely on the generosity of our community to keep the Food Pantry stocked and operational. Here’s how you can contribute:
Donate Food

Non-perishable items such as canned goods, pasta, rice, and cereals are always in demand. Donations can be dropped off at the church during office hours.

Volunteer

Join our team of dedicated volunteers to help sort, stock shelves, distribute food, or help with client intake during pantry hours. To volunteer, please contact us at food@tryparkway.com or call (703) 590-5199.

Monetary Contributions

Financial donations enable us to purchase fresh produce and other essential items. Donations can be made online through our church’s giving platform or in person. Indicate “Hunger Relief” for processing and

Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP): Offers nutrition assistance to eligible low-income individuals and families. LEARN MORE

About Us

How you Can Help Elkton Area United Services
Thrift at Heart

Our thrift store is located at: 149 W Spotswood Ave., Elkton, VA. 

We sell reasonably priced used clothing, furniture and miscellaneous items.  The store, which is open to all,  generates approximately 40 percent of the funds to support Elkton Area United Service’s programs.  Go to facebook/ElktonAreaUnitedServices-EAUS for weekly specials and specialty items.

Donation of gently used clean items such as clothing, household items, furniture (items we cannot accept are mattresses, tube tvs, computers, appliances). Donations are accepted Tuesday – Friday 7 a.m. until 5 p.m. and Saturday 7 a.m. – 2 p.m.

Hours of operation:  Monday-Thursday 10 a.m. – 5 p.m., Friday 10 a.m. – 6 p.m., Saturday 10 a.m. – 2 p.m.

We accept cash, local checks and VISA/Mastercard only on purchases over $10.00

Help with non-monetary assistance also available from the Thrift Store.  For help contact client services at 540-298-8685 #1 or clientservices@elktonatheart.org

VOLUNTEERS NEEDED:  Information at 298-8685 #4 or volunteers@elktonatheart.org

THE EPICENTER IS COMING TO PARKVILLE

A new chapter is beginning in a treasured historic space – one filled with hope, help and a place to belong. We’re building something beautiful with Parkville, for Parkville.

YOUTH PROGRAMS

We believe in building up tomorrow’s leaders. Our Before and After School Care program, The ZONE, and our summer program, Camp EPIC are some of our strongest pieces of the organization.

IN EDGEWOOD
IN ABERDEEN

A healthy body and mind is a critical part to living a healthy life. Through our food pantry, fitness classes, and health professional partnerships, we love to provide opportunities to thrive in health and wellness.

IN EDGEWOOD
IN ABERDEEN

Everyone deserves a chance to succeed. From case management, employment connections, and free internet/laptop access, we’re here to empower every individual that walks in the door.

IN EDGEWOOD

Taking the road to recovery together because we believe life transformation happens when we take steps with others. Whatever your past may be, we believe everyone can be free from addictive cycles.

IN EDGEWOOD

The EPICENTER at Parkville
Our newest location is coming soon!
LEARN MORE
	
123
ABOUT US

At The EPICENTER, our mission is to empower people, strengthen families, and build a sense of community and trust. We strive to empower people by providing strong support, resources, and opportunities for growth. We believe that by strengthening families, we can create a foundation of community where trust flourishes.

Through our dedicated programs and initiatives, we see individuals empowered from all walks of life. By fostering inclusive environments and promoting education, we enable people to unleash their potential and achieve remarkable personal and professional growth.

We understand that families are the building blocks of a thriving society. Therefore, we work tirelessly to provide support and resources that strengthen familial bonds, promote healthy communication, and foster resilience. By doing so, we aim to create a nurturing environment where families can flourish and individuals can thrive.

At The EPICENTER, we believe in the power of community. We actively engage with our neighbors, forging meaningful connections and fostering a sense of belonging. Through collaborative efforts and innovative projects, we strive to build a vibrant community where everyone feels valued, supported, and empowered to make a difference.

LEARN MORE
WE’D LOVE TO SEE YOU!

There’s always something happening at The EPICENTER!

Each week, our locations host a variety of activities and programs designed to bring people together and strengthen our community.

Check out our online calendar for more specific programming and events information.

HELP provides shelter, healthcare, and support services to residents of Hampton and Newport News.
Shelter

We provide emergency shelter, transitional shelter, and case management to help our clients achieve permanent housing.


Low-Cost Dental Care

Our clinic provides comprehensive dental care and access to low-cost dentures, crowns, and night guards to uninsured patients in Virginia.

Support

Support comes in the form of meals, clothing, showers, laundry, mail, document assistance, food pantry, and fellowship.

We are in the dignity business.

At HELP, we meet people in their hour of greatest need—people who are homeless, who are hungry, who are hurt, who are at the fringes of society—people who need their faith and dignity restored.

FOOD BANK

We have been serving the community since December 1978 by providing food to those in need based on the size of the family and specific needs.

We are one of eight Food Banks in Frederick County affiliated with the Religious Coalition for Emergency Human Needs (RCEHN).

The Food Bank works closely with Brunswick’s ERF, local schools, and fraternal organizations to help families with additional needs.

We are located at:
Brunswick City Annex
601 East Potomac St.
Brunswick, MD 21716

Why We Give
 
Our hearts are filled with gratitude and thanksgiving to God for giving us His Son, Jesus Christ, to save us and give us abundant life!
Giving reflects the heart and intentions of God, so we generously give to be more like Him.
Giving is a very practical and tangible way to honor God for who He is and all He has done for us.
Giving is a key to not only having our needs met by God, but also meeting the needs of others.
Giving is the mark of a growing Christian who is being transformed from “a taker”  to “a giver.”
Giving releases the resources of God in our lives and opens the doors to God’s miraculous provision for His Church.
 
Did You Know?
Most companies exclude Churches, but if you read the fine print, you may find an exception for religious organizations if they also provide secular benefits to the community -i.e. food pantry support, educational programs, etc. Give it a shot – the worst thing they can do is say no! 
 
Through your contributions, you empower people in your community with the resources to help change people’s lives through the power of Christ.
 
GIVE ONLINE NOW
 
Online Giving allows you to give via our website using a credit card, debit card, or bank account information. This option allows you to make a one-time contribution or you may create an account and set up recurring scheduled payments. You are also given the option to select specific designations for your online gifts. All payments processed through our website are secure. 

CLICK HERE TO GIVE NOW

 
Checks & Cash
Checks and cash can be placed into the offering plate during Sunday services or checks can be made payable to HFNL and mailed to our administrative office
Hereford Faith & Life Church 
P .O. Box 400
Monkton, MD 21111.
Safe Sanctuary
 
Following the words of Jesus who said, “Whoever welcomes a child welcomes me” (Matthew 18:5), we vow to foster, nurture and nourish a safe and loving sanctuary at the Hereford Faith & Life Church. Jesus heeded all, “If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones, it would be better for you if a great millstone were fastened around your neck and you were drowned in the depth of the sea” (Matthew 18:6).Hereford Faith & Life Church is committed to upholding biblical principles of protecting children and preventing abuse.Hereford Faith & Life Church is an inclusive and welcoming congregation. Building upon our historical commitment in discipleship through our loving service, protecting children and youth is a top priority at our church. The purpose of this Safe Sanctuary Policy is to address the safety of our children and youth, and those responsible for their care. The Safe Sanctuary Policy is under the auspices of the Church Council. Copies are available in the church, on the church website, and in the church office.
 
Volunteers 18 years and older working with children must watch the Safe Sanctuary Training Powerpoint, review the Safe Sanctuary Policy, and complete the Criminal Background Check Form and Safe Sanctuary Acknowledgement form below.

Check out the food businesses based at the TPSS Community Kitchen!
	
Video Player
Crossroads Community Food Network is building a healthier, more inclusive food system in the Takoma/Langley Crossroads, a vibrant immigrant community in Maryland just outside Washington, DC.
Expanding Healthy Food Access

Crossroads Farmers Market has been making fresh, healthy food more accessible in the Takoma/Langley Crossroads since 2007. We pioneered a choice-based model to address food insecurity, an innovative approach that has been replicated nationwide.
Read more…

Making Healthy Food Choices

Our Healthy Eating Program brings fun and interactive farm-to-fork activities to shoppers of all ages at the market. Bilingual food demos offer a visual guide to preparing sometimes unfamiliar fruits and veggies, encouraging the exploration of diverse local options.
Read more…

Empowering the Community

Our Microenterprise Development Program and TPSS Community Kitchen provide free, bilingual business support and affordable prep and storage space to reduce barriers for local, small-scale food entrepreneurs.
Read more…

Strengthening, Educating, and Elevating Women, with a special heart for Single Mom Families in the VA Peninsula.

Grounded & fueled by our faith and the dedicated support of our communities and contributors, The GLOWW Center is a volunteer-led non-profit (501c3) organization that seeks to care for the physical, educational, emotional, and spiritual needs of our single mom families. 

Vision & Mission

We envision a community where single mom families are nurtured and empowered to lead successful lives regardless of income or social status. Our mission is to help women, with an emphasis on single moms in need with resources to alleviate their lives, and through education and training, encourage them to be active participants in the resolution of their current circumstances and long-term stability. 

Learn More
Food Pantry

Our emergency food assistance includes non-perishable canned & boxed food items. Single mom families can receive a bag of canned goods, boxed goods, or both. 

Learn More
Hygiene Bank

The GLOWW Center is tackling Period Poverty in the VA Peninsula by providing feminine hygiene products to women. 

Learn More
Clothing Closet

The GLOWW Center provides clean, gently-used, like-new clothing for single moms (and their children when available).

Learn More
Agency of the Diaper Bank

The GLOWW Center is a Diaper Bank Agent in Yorktown, VA for single mothers in need. 

Learn More
Volunteer

Our non-profit is 100% volunteer-led. Come join us!

Learn More
Give

Consider giving a one-time or monthly donation as we help Strengthen, Elevate, and Educate women, with an emphasis on single mom families in the Virginia Peninsula.

Give Today

BALTIMORE CHAPTER
		
	
WELCOME TO THE BALTIMORE CHAPTER OF THE SO WHAT ELSE HUNGER RELIEF PROGRAM!

The So What Else Baltimore Food Chapter was founded in 2020 in response to the COVID-19 Pandemic and pervasive, systemic hunger in Baltimore. Today, it provides over a million pounds of food to 40+ sites around the City plus dozens of home deliveries to those who cannot travel to any of these distribution sites for various reasons, such as physical disability. Unlike other food banks, So What Else does not charge for food delivery. In order to meet demand, additional donations and volunteers are needed. 

BALTIMORE RESOURCE CENTER IN SOUTH BALTIMORE

In 2023, So What Else Baltimore proudly opened its very first brick-and-mortar Resource Center in the heart of Southwest Baltimore, allowing the chapter to further support the surrounding Carrollton Ridge community. Though most clients are from the area, all Maryland residents are welcome to stop by and pick up clothing, shoes, toiletries, toys, and limited household goods from the center.

Address:
424 S. Pulaski Street
Baltimore, MD 21223

Phone: 410-429-0657
Email: baltimore@sowhatelse.org

	
Hours:
Tuesday 9:00 am – 2:00 pm
Wednesday 9:00 am – 2:00 pm
Thursday 9:00 am – 2:00 pm
Friday 9:00 am – 2:00 pm
Saturday 9:00 am – 2:00 pm
FOOD WAREHOUSE IN CENTRAL BALTIMORE

We recently expanded our Baltimore food storage capabilities by adding warehouse, refrigeration and freezer space located on the northwest side of the city. Although our warehouse is not open to the public, we do accept donations and allow partner organizations to pick up from us there (by appointment only).

Warehouse Hours:
By appointment only
Email: baltmore@sowhatelse.org

*We do not serve clients at this location


	
Warehouse Address:
4111 Menlo Drive
Baltimore, MD 21215

FIND A FOOD DISTRIBUTION NEAR YOU IN BALTIMORE

The Baltimore chapter of So What Else hosts over 40 food distributions, community deliveries, and ad-hoc drop-offs in all four corners of Baltimore City.

Please Note:

Food is first come, first served. No holds.
Our fresh food is recovered — meaning that the type, amount, and quality changes each day.
Please bring a bag or cart with you. We don’t have bags.
Some distributions are open to the public. Some are not. The weekly schedule is divided into public and not public. Please contact the local organization in charge of the distribution if you have questions.
The schedule is different each week. Please follow us on Instagram or Facebook and/or check this webpage each week.
Last minute schedule changes happen. Please check Instagram, Facebook, or this webpage for updates. Stay in touch with the organization hosting the distribution, too; sometimes changes don’t make it to social media.
A Map of So What Else Baltimore Food Distributions
The map below is regularly updated with the latest pop-up distributions and partner sites that we support. 
Many of our partner sites provide other services besides food distributions. 
Click on any of the numbered locations to learn more and find their contact information.
This Week’s Food Distribution Schedule
The schedule is different each week. 
The weekly schedule is posted on this webpage, and on both Instagram and Facebook. 
Call or text if you have any questions about a distribution: 410-429-0657
123456789101112131415
HOW TO GET INVOLVED WITH SO WHAT ELSE
Volunteer for Baltimore

We need your help! The more volunteers we have, the more people we can serve right here in Charm City. Some specific volunteer opportunities are listed on our Volunteer Hub page, but if you have an idea for how you would like to contribute that isn’t listed, please email us details. 

Donate to Baltimore

So What Else would not be able to help so many people throughout Baltimore without the kindness of those who have the means. We accept both monetary and in-kind donations of any amount! There are many ways to donate:

Monetary Donations

You can donate via Credit Card or PayPal here.

If interested in donating via cash or check, please contact: info@sowhatelse.org

In-kind Donations

Donations can be brought to either of our locations in Baltimore. Please contact us first to schedule a time so we can make sure staff is available to assist you.

Phone: 410-429-0657

Email: baltimore@sowhatelse.org

We will gladly accept any of the following gently used or new items:

Clean Clothing (all ages and genders)
Clean Shoes and Accessories (all ages and genders)
Household Goods (no broken items)
Electronics and Appliances (must work and include all cables)
Toys and Games (no broken items, must include all necessary pieces and work)
Diapers and Baby Wipes (unopened packages only, no loose diapers)
Baby Toys and Baby-specific Furniture (no broken items, must include all necessary pieces and work)
Cleaning Supplies (unopened packages/bottles only)
Toiletries (soap, toothpaste, deodorant, etc.)

CONTACT US
Love Beyond Missed Meals

$25 a month supports local families in need

Donate Monthly

 

Each night in the United States, 17.4 million families go to bed hungry.

An additional 6.9 million families live on the verge of food insecurity. That means they don’t always know where their next meal is coming from.

In Maryland, alone, 640,180 people are facing hunger. This can be broken down to 1/9 people facing hunger and 1 in 7 children combating the same fight. (cited by Feeding America's website)

The Salvation Army of Central Maryland provides sit-down programs that ensure nutritious hot meals and valuable human interaction, to mobile meals delivering sustenance to those who cannot reach a food distribution center, to feeding programs. We help the most vulnerable members of society escape the daily burdens of food insecurity. As a community who cares.

 

 

 

 

 

 
OUR FEEDING PROGRAMS

 
FOOD PANTRIES

Several of our locations are equipped with an onsite food pantry, which is stocked with nonperishable food and toiletry items. Walk ins and scheduled clients are eligible to receive items upon verification of identification and proof of housing with cooking facilities. (Applicable every 30 days).

Contact your nearest Food Pantry
Pantry on the Go 2023
Social Services

 
 
MOBILE FEEDING

Our FeedMore mobile feeding program provides hot, healthy meals as well as clothing, warm blankets, and toiletries, to homeless and impoverished men, women, and children, six nights a week.

 
 
PANTRY ON THE GO

During the warmer months, The Salvation partners with the local food bank to host food give aways or “Pantry-on-the-Go”, which is a large food distribution event hosted at various sites to provide immediate supplementary food assistance throughout the community. We currently host this food event in Glen Burnie and Howard County. (No ID/documents required)

 
 
COMPLIMENTARY CAFE’S

To expand dining options for the community, we operate two complimentary cafes in central Maryland. Brass Hat Cafe in Westminster serves free, hot meals every Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday from 4:30 pm – 6:00pm. Temple Corps also hosts Cornbread Cafe, which provides free lunch every Thursday from 1:00 pm – 4:00 pm in West Baltimore.

 

 

 

 
IF YOU NEED HELP, PLEASE

CONTACT US

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.





Commitment to Diversity

WELCOME TO FISH WILLIAMSBURG
What is FISH?

FISH is a volunteer-operated, non-profit organization that provides food, clothing, and small housewares to persons in need who live in Williamsburg, James City County and York County.

FISH fulfills its mission through the generosity of the Williamsburg community and the efforts of over 250 volunteers.

 

 

Our Services

FISH provides food, clothing, and small housewares to residents of Williamsburg, James City County and York County. More Information

Donations

FISH accepts financial gifts, as well as donations of food, clothing, and housewares. Donation Information

Volunteer

We have a variety of volunteer positions in both the Pantry and the Clothes Closet. Volunteer must be at least 18 years old. Volunteer Information

Mission

Loaves & Fishes provides nourishing food with respect and dignity to all who seek assistance, while offering robust opportunities for community engagement through volunteerism, partnerships, and nutrition education.

Tax ID 45-1498743

There are a number of ways to support our mission with donations, as well as goods and services, and easy giving programs.

 Support our Mission
How things work at Loaves & Fishes
We are working to strengthen our community by combatting hunger.
What kind of food will I get?

Loaves & Fishes is happy to provide groceries for you two times each month. Each time someone visits Loaves & Fishes, they will receive a grocery cart (about 100 pounds) of fresh fruit and vegetables, bread, pastries, and dessert, milk, eggs, frozen meat, and a variety of canned and dried fruits, canned vegetables, and other shelf-stable foods like oil, cereal, pasta, and rice.

The Food We Provide
What to expect when you visit.

Groceries are distributed on a first-come, first-served basis three days a week—no appointment is needed. Tuesday's grocery pickup is by appointment only.

We invite you to come to the Pantry, follow the parking instructions, stay in your car, and wait for your groceries to be delivered to your vehicle.

Come to the Pantry

Schedule Tuesday Pickup
How to Support Loaves & Fishes Food Pantry
Volunteer, Donate Money, Donate Food & Supplies, Purchase Loaves & Fishes Items
Lend a Hand • Volunteer!

Loaves & Fishes relies on the support of 125 volunteers each week to help us inspect, sort, pack, and give out fresh produce, meat, bread, and shelf-stable commodity items. Shifts are offered six days each week and range in length from 90 minutes to four hours. Volunteers work alongside staff members, and training is provided at the start of each shift, so no prior experience is needed for any of our jobs.

Volunteer! 
Monetary Donation

People struggle with hunger right here in Charlottesville. Map the Meal Gap, a project of Feeding America, found that Charlottesville in 2022 had the highest level of food insecurity in our area, with one in 7 people (14.1%) unable to access enough healthy food each month, and child food insecurity at 17.3%.

27% of Charlottesville families live at or below the poverty level and cannot afford the essentials of life (food, shelter, clothing, utilities) and the costs of working (childcare, transit).

Tax ID 45-1498743

Ways to Give 
Donate Food & Supplies

The food Loaves & Fishes gives out comes from the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank, local groceries, farmers, orchards, food distributors, food drives, and purchases from wholesale vendors.  We accept donations of frozen meat, eggs, fresh fruits and vegetables, and canned and dried foods. Click button below to see items we need.

Donate Food & Supplies 
Purchase Loaves & Fishes merchandise and show your support wherever you go!
More Information
Nutritionist Corner

Registered Dietitian Matt Paysour helps sort and select the best foods when assembling groceries for our guests. Matt provides samples, recipes, and tips on ways to store and prepare the food we provide and writes a quarterly nutrition newsletter that is included in each visitor’s bags of food. Matt offers nutrition education activities at the pantry.

Nutritionist Corner & Recipes
Frequently Asked Questions

We have answers to the many questions regularly asked by clients, volunteers and supporters on our FAQ page. If you do not find an answer to your query, please get in touch with us using the email form below.

Thank you for your participation and support.

​Items We Will Accept:
CLOTHING:
Clean clothes, no stains, no holes, no odors, buttons attached, zippers in working order
Shoes that are very gently used; clean, no holes
Children’s clothes/shoes without stains or holes
Jewelry
Household:
Antiques or Vintage items
Crock ware
Flowerpots
Holiday decorations in good/perfect condition
Complete sets of dishes or glassware, no chips or cracked glasses or plates
Clean, new-in-package pillows
SETS of sheets
Gently used quilts, comforters or blankets
Gently used towels, any size
Clean rugs
Furniture in good condition. Please, call first to see if we can accommodate the pieces
Small appliances in good working order; toasters, microwaves, blenders, etc.
Cast iron pans
Nonstick pans that are not scratched or damaged
Cooking utensils and silverware in good condition
Plastic containers with the appropriate lids, large or small – not lunchmeat packaging
MISCELLANEOUS:
Puzzles and games that are complete
Bicycles
	
We Will Not Take
PERSONAL ITEMS:
Soiled, stained clothes without buttons or zippers that don’t work
Used undergarments
Dirty, scuffed or old shoes
OPENED Personal Toiletry Items; shampoo, toothpaste, lotion, etc.
ELECTRONICS:
Small Appliances That Don’t Work
Older TV’s – Must Be FLAT SCREEN & SMART TV
Computers, Monitors, Printers, Keyboards, Fax Machines,
Radios, Paper Shredders, Clock Radios
Cassette Tapes, 8 Track Tapes
House Phones and Answering Machines
HOUSEHOLD:
Mattresses or Box Springs
Used Bed Pillows, Mattress Covers, Bed Skirts
Dishes that are chipped, glued or broken
Scratched Cookware
Crockpots without lids
CHILDREN’S:
Infant Carriers, Car Seats or Crib Bumpers
Incomplete or Broken Children’s Toys
Clothes With Holes and Stains
MISCELLANEOUS:
Books
Large exercise equipment
Volunteer With Us
Proceeds from every sale help feed our neighbors in need through the food distribution and Community Café activities of the St. Michaels Community Center. 

Treasure Cove Thrift Shop is conveniently located on the corner of N Talbot St and Railroad Ave, with ample parking and an outdoor area for shopping. 

Treasure Cove is run in part by volunteers and partners with other local non-profits and Talbot County’s Department of Social Services to provide emergency assistance to people in need, including those suffering from the effects of fire, flood, eviction, job loss, or other special circumstances. 

Treasure Cove donations and purchases help ensure children and adults from throughout the Bay Hundred have the resources they need through the programs and activities of the St. Michaels Community Center. 
Thrift Shop Volunteers Are Needed
	
Want to make a difference in our community while connecting with people? 
Our Treasure Cove Thrift Shop has so much new inventory in donations that we need a few more volunteers to help make a difference in our community in meaningful ways.

​Volunteers Needed - See Openings and Sign Up Here

We need help from 10-3 on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, Fridays and Saturdays, for as long as anyone can help.

Volunteers work together sorting, pricing, and placing newly donated items to the store, and in the front of the store at register.
​
All proceeds benefit the children and adults served by the St Michaels Community Center.

Thank you!

Manna Food Pantry

When all you need is a little help to keep food on the table, Manna Food Pantry is here.

Manna is a weekly grocery box distribution program serving struggling families in the Roanoke region. Each food box contains approximately 60 pounds of food including, non-perishable food staples, fresh produce and frozen meats. Dog and cat food is available for the pets in the family.

Registration is open to all. To enroll contact us by TEXT ONLY at 540-598-5203. Once enrolled, you will receive a text with a reservation for either Wednesday or Thursday. The text will have a scan bar and QR code. Please save these texts to speed your check-in!

If you receive a text reservation confirmation, there will be a box for you that you may pick up between 1:00 – 3:00 pm
It is not necessary to come early! We will have a box for you and do not want you to burn your gas.
We recommend adding the number you receive the notification from to your contacts with a name you will remember, like MANNA, so you can easily find your QR Codes.
We plan to make multiple reservations for you, so you may see more than one text from us. Save the text stream.

We partner with Feeding Southwest Virginia. Manna Food Pantry operates as a drive-thru food pantry. Manna recipients may receive free clothing vouchers for use at the Donation Center on 421 Fourth Street. Find out more.

We are an equal opportunity provider.

 VOLUNTEER

Volunteers are needed throughout the week to assemble boxes, organize food donations, and help with grocery box distribution. Click here to volunteer for Manna Food Pantry.

ENDING HUNGER THROUGH FOOD RECOVERY

Nourish Now recovers surplus food from dedicated food donor partners including restaurants, grocers, caterers, farms, sporting venues, and more. Food is then directly donated to children, families, seniors and veterans in the need primarily in Montgomery County, MD along with the DC metro area.

 

GET FOOD
GIVE FOOD

POUNDS OF FOOD RECOVERED & DONATED SINCE 2011
OUR PROGRAMS
NOURISH NEIGHBORHOODS

The two main objectives that are the driving force of the Nourish Neighborhoods program are the family dinner and building community ties.

OFF-SITE FAMILY DISTRIBUTIONS

We donate a five-day supply of nutritious perishable and nonperishable food and beverages to 19,200 individuals, equating to 4,800 families.

FOOD TO NONPROFIT PARTNER ORGANIZATIONS

We work closely with other nonprofits to ensure every family, senior, student, and veteran gets the food they need.

S.N.A.C.K.

Student Nutrition Access in Classes K-12 (S.N.A.C.K.) serves to eliminate student hunger as a barrier to education during the school day.

MoCo Service Consolidation Hub Distributions

We donate food weekly to the 8 Montgomery County Hubs. This partnership focuses on donating a wide variety of multicultural food to those in need.

This is God’s house, not our own, and all are welcome.
60+ years of purpose…
Who We Are and How We Came To Be

The people that gather at the Oak Park Church of the Brethren have come to understand the distinct Scriptural call of God for its people and its two-fold reason for its existence:

We exist to reach people for the Kingdom of God.

We exist to help each of our Christian brothers and sisters in this place come to a state of full maturity, unity and completeness and to help one another reach his/her highest spiritual potential.

Worship Services

Held every Sunday morning at 10:30am in the church sanctuary. You are also welcome to tune in to 98.5FM and worship with us from the parking lot.

We’d love to see you!
Lessons Beyond Our Services

Two opportunities to learn beyond weekly Sunday Services: On our Facebook page and with Sunday School Classes. Our Pastor, Carl Fike, has been sharing video messages on our Facebook page since Spring of 2020 - click below to watch! Sunday School classes are available for all ages each Sunday at 9:30am.

Visit our Facebook Page
We love kids!

Our youngest visitors are welcome to enjoy Sunday School classes, Vacation Bible School, the annual Christmas Program & Family Dinner, the church nursery and Children’s Church (during church service). 

They keep the church energized and full!

Enjoy hands-on kids activities, gardening tips, games, demonstrations and giveaways, all focused on simple ways to make Rockville greener. Rain or shine; activities will move indoors if needed. Features

Rockville’s Senior Center is an active facility dedicated to serving those who are aged 60 and older within and around our community. We offer nutritious meals, educational classes and workshops, art programs, fitness, and much more. Our large center features a computer lab, fitness room, library, community garden plots, woodworking studio, and plenty of rooms for hosting your next special event.

Chesapeake Cares Food Pantry

More Than A Bag Of Groceries

VISITING THE FOOD PANTRY

It would be our honor to serve you and your family during one of our Food Pantry openings. Below is information to help make your visit to Chesapeake Cares Food Pantry more comfortable and to give you an idea of what you can expect. 

 

HOURS AND LOCATION

Tuesday: 4:30PM – 7:30PM
Thursday: 9:00AM – 12:30PM

6021 Solomons Island Road, Huntingtown, MD 20639
Email: foodpantry@chesapeakechurch.org
Phone: 410-257-3444

DIRECTIONS
IF YOU HAVE A NEED, YOU QUALIFY FOR FOOD

If you have a need, we’re here to help. Simply stop in during one of our openings  to receive support on a weekly basis. No other qualification necessary!

FOOD SELECTION

You know your family best and what food and household items you need. When you visit, you will be given a menu to choose the items that work best for your family. Once you make your selection, our incredible volunteers will get to work shopping for your items! Once your order is complete, they load them directly into your vehicle for you.

COMMUNITY RESOURCES

In addition to Chesapeake Cares Food Pantry services, we have also provided a list of community resources to help serve you best in your personal circumstances:

End Hunger’s Food Pantry Finder
Maryland Assistance Program
Applying for SNAP Assistance
Calvert County Resources
Shelf Stable Food Safety

SERVING YOU

There is not a one-size-fits-all approach to serving because everyone’s circumstances are different, so we want to meet you where you are! To serve you best, we offer the following services for you. 


Drive-Thru Service:
Pull onto the Chesapeake Church driveway and continue past the church. Turn right at the “Food Pantry” flag and continue down to the bottom of the hill. You will be directed by a traffic safety volunteer to a parking space. Remain in your vehicle and a pantry team member will visit you in the order of your arrival. Using our electronic menu system, our pantry team members will assist you in the selection process of groceries to best fit your family needs. For your convenience, you will remain in your vehicle for your entire visit.

Delivery Service:
For our homebound seniors, we offer direct delivery. Please call for details.

MEDICAL CARE

Chesapeake Cares Food Pantry provides free, routine outpatient health and wellness services to the medically underserved in our community. Through our partnership with Calvert Health and their Mobile Medical Outreach, we are able to serve many people with safe, quality care.

SUNDAY SERVICES: 9AM & 11AM

Supportive Services
A Place to Find All the Essentials

LifeStyles’ Samaritan Project provides basic safety net services to assist households in meeting their most essential needs. 

Food Pantry & Distribution
LifeStyles food pantry offers emergency food for a three-day period. Households can receive assistance every 30 days. Larger food distributions are held periodically throughout the year in partnership with other local organizations. Food is available on a first-come, first-served basis.

Holiday Baskets
LifeStyles annually provides Thanksgiving and Christmas turkey baskets to families to offset their needs around the holidays.

Household items

LifeStyles provides households who are transitioning into permanent housing with items needed. This includes connections to available furniture, small appliances, dishware, etc. LifeStyles also provides toiletry items for those that are homeless and households in need.

Clothing
The clothing closet provides casual and professional wear clothing for all persons in the household. We provide items for those fleeing domestic violence through “Rosemary’s Closet.”

Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
LifeStyles works to connect individuals with this federal program that provides nutrition benefits to supplement the food budget of needy families so they can purchase healthy food and move towards self-sufficiency.

For more information about the Samaritan Project, email Resources@lifestylesofmd.org.

Who we are

Love Wins Recovery provides a welcoming and safe home for women in active recovery. We strive to create a community that brings hope and restoration while supporting each woman in her walk to create a full and successful life.

Who We Serve

We serve adult women who have recently completed an inpatient addiction treatment program. Residents can be from anywhere in the United States, but we primarily serve Anne Arundel County, Baltimore City, and the surrounding areas in Maryland.

Gaithersburg Meals-on-Wheels, Inc.
Serving the Gaithersburg, Maryland Area Since 1981

Gaithersburg Meals-on-Wheels, Inc. is an all-volunteer, non-profit organization incorporated in 1981 with the mission of providing home delivery of low cost, nutritious meals to fragile and vulnerable adults who are unable to shop for or safely prepare their own meals due to a temporary or permanent disability. This service enables home-bound clients to remain in their homes with a level of independence.

Fighting hunger one family at a time.
Providing dignified access to free groceries, allowing families to devote their limited financial resources to obligations such as housing, utilities, medication, and other basic needs.
Donate Today
Feed a Neighbor

AFAC is 91% privately funded - every dollar you donate makes it possible for us to buy groceries for the many families who come to our doors weekly.

Donate Funds
Make an Impact

AFAC volunteers donate over 51,000 hours of their time each year, the equivalent of 25 full-time employees. Come be a part of our mission.

Volunteer
Support our Community

30% of the food we distribute is donated by people like you through local food drives, providing families in need with variety and choice.

Donate Food
Around 4,200 Arlington families weekly rely on AFAC groceries for their regular meals.

Supplemental groceries from AFAC mean that families can remain in their homes, workers can stay on the job, children are ready to learn, and mothers and babies receive essential nutrition. We help relieve the food budgets of our clients, allowing them to make other necessary purchases without sacrificing their health and nutrition needs.

Welcome to American Diversity Group

American Diversity Group (ADG) is a nonprofit organization based in Montgomery County, Maryland. We are committed to improving the quality of life for underserved and culturally diverse populations. Through our free weekend clinics, wellness programs, digital and financial literacy workshops, and educational drives, we strive to make health and learning accessible for every age group.

 

We believe in the power of community care — bringing medical support, preventive screenings, senior engagement, and education directly to those who need it most. Our mission is simple: health and education for all.

Learn More
Our Partners:
What we do:

Explore our programs that promote inclusion, equality, and cultural awareness. We offer initiatives to support underrepresented communities, foster dialogue, and celebrate diversity. Join us in creating a more inclusive society for all.

Free Health Clinic

Every Saturday (9 AM–3 PM) & Sunday (9 AM–2 PM)

Digital Health Literacy

​Helping all age groups navigate health technology​

​Senior Coffee Club

​Yoga, hot meals, digital literacy & screenings every Saturday

Toy Drive & School Supplies

Annual December and August drives for families in need

Preventive Screenings

Free vision, bone, BP, glucose & more — weekly

Student Opportunities

​Internships and clinical rotations for high school and college students

What Our Community Says

Asma Siddiqi

"I would like to thank the American Diversity Group, especially Mr. Mayur Mody for providing my son Sahil opportunities for serving our communities through food drives and health/vaccine clinics during COVID-19. It broadened his perspective about the challenges faced by many members of our community, and he learned how to provide some solutions for the same. Through ADG, he was also able to shadow doctors like Dr. Thacker and get invaluable insights into the practice of medicine. Additionally, he was able to discuss these meaningful experiences during his college applications and I am sure it has positively impacted his application. I am happy to share that he has been accepted by Cornell University! Thanks again for providing students with the chance to serve the community and also grooming them for their their future in college and beyond! He looks forward to volunteering with ADG in the future too!"

The Cluster Food Bank

If you need food for you or your family, we’re here to help.

Remember. There is no charge for food or services to individuals or families in need!

Providing nonperishable food items such as, canned goods, canned meats, canned fruits, canned vegetables, canned soups, fruit cups, cereal, oatmeal, pasta, sauces, lunch box items, coffee, tea, juices, sugar, flour, condiments, personal hygiene products, paper goods, cleaning products, and more.

When available, frozen meats, fresh vegetables, eggs, milk, and fresh bread.

Special holiday food boxes: are given at Easter, Thanksgiving, and Christmas to registered Food Bank clients.

School supplies are available in August through My Neighbors Foundation. 

The Cluster Food Bank is open:

Monday mornings from 10:00 am to 12:00 pm
Wednesday evenings from 5:00 pm to 7:00 pm
Saturday mornings from 10:00 am to 12:00 pm

Sunday Park Food Pantry

Neighbors helping Neighbors

Saturday Mornings from 10:00 - noon, rain or shine!

The Brandermill Church is providing an assortment of staple items, pre-boxed and ready to go, through contactless pick-up. Cars pull into the front loop of the church parking lot and a volunteer will place a box in your trunk. Boxes contain various items, similar to those listed here:

Cold and hot cereals, Pop Tarts, breakfast bars, pancake mix and syrup, canned fruits, canned vegetables, pasta and sauce, instant potatoes, stuffing mix, rice, beans, mac and cheese, canned meats, peanut butter, soups and stews, crackers, cookies, coffee and tea, shelf-stable milk.

The Sunday Park Food Pantry serves residents in the 23112 zip code.  Proof of residency must be provided during pick-up.

We are neighbors helping neighbors.  Donations are collected daily at the church.  For contactless drop-off, items may be placed in the covered bin near the office door.

If you’d like to help pack & distribute food staples to food-insecure families living in the 23112 zip code on Friday & Saturday mornings, sign up here.

For further information, please contact Mark Carpenter.

Our Priorities Our Faith

you'll find The Salvation Army.

(301) 733-2440
HagerstownMD@uss.salvationarmy.org
JOIN US FOR WORSHIP!
Sunday School: Sunday, 9:30 AM
Worship Service: Sunday, 10:30 AM
GET DIRECTIONS
 
 
OUR LEADERSHIP

Salvation Army Officers lead Salvation Army Programs. These officers are responsible for leading by assessing needs, delivering programs, and directing ministries for our local community. From serving food to balancing the books, they function as the Executive Directors and the Pastors for the Hagerstown Corps.

 

Majors Mark & Jennifer Van Meter

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.





Commitment to Diversity

Our Vision For A Stronger Community

HRCAP connects individuals and families to educational, social, and economic programs that promote self-sufficiency, strengthen lives, and build hope—making our community a better place to live. Through our award-winning programs and proven strategies in education, training, work experience, housing, emergency assistance, and more, we address the root causes of economic hardship and remove barriers to opportunity, equipping families and communities for long-term success.We believe that when individuals and families are empowered to help themselves and support one another, they create stronger communities and a better Hampton Roads region for all.

THURMONT FOOD BANK
How do I get food?
CONTACT & MAP
ABOUT US
Resources
Other Food Banks in Frederick County

Anyone from Northern Frederick County who needs  food is Welcome!

Donations always needed!



Weather Policy:
We follow Frederick County Schools; if  they close, we will close, too. When weather improves quickly we may reopen so check this site and Facebook for updates. If it is extremely cold we may close early .
Days we're always closed:
July 4th, the Friday before ColorFest,
the Friday after Thanksgiving,
Dec. 25, 26, 31st and Jan.1st & 2nd
Thanksgiving turkeys and side dishes are given out to those registered at the food bank during regular hours on the two Tuesdays and two Fridays before Thanksgiving. 



 
Non-discrimination Policy 

The Thurmont Food Bank does not discriminate based on race, color, national origin, age, disability, religion, sex (gender identity, sexual preference,  pregnancy)

A Christian Missional Community

with the purpose to prevent and respond to addiction and homelessness in St. Mary's County

Our Mission
Get Support

For those seeking a brighter future out of addiction and homelessness, The Mission offers a safe, faith-based environment of counseling and government assistance guidance, rehabilitation, and life skills training necessary to transition single men and women from their addictions and self-limiting behaviors to brighter, independent lives of their own. 

Learn More
Give Support

Your financial contributions make a direct impact. Since our certified counselor is our only paid staff member, your financial contributions go directly to our ministry activities. Our financial records are independently audited and certified regularly in accordance to Maryland law for 501 (c) qualification. 

Learn More
Volunteer at the Mission

Are you a Person or Organization seeking to help the destitute and forgotten to a life of independence and self-sufficiency? You can and we make it easy.  Join us by volunteering for short projects, teaching life skills, providing vocational training, feeding the poor, manning our community pantry, or mentoring. Your time and talents have lasting positive impact on St. Mary’s county and enables us to spread the hope and unity of the Gospel in practical ways, by enabling those in our community who seek a fresh start.

Welcome!




﻿We are a Roman Catholic Church whose community is filled with a lively and welcoming spirit. We strive to embrace and strengthen our parish family through beautiful liturgies, engaging religious education programs for children and adults and meaningful social justice work.




Our parish consist of two churches - Immaculate Conception Church in Elkton, Maryland, and our mission Church - St. Jude Mission Church in the town of North East, Maryland. For over 175 years, the people of Immaculate Conception Parish/St. Jude Mission Church have worked and worshipped together for the greater glory of God. Our parish has a wonderful history, and many people have been a part of that heritage given to us by those who have gone before us and who left a legacy of love.




As we continue that legacy, we invite you to worship with us, grow in faith with us, celebrate with us, and possibly find your spiritual home with us.

Sunday School

@12:45pm. God welcomes us to be part of His family, and loves each and every person unconditionally. As followers of Christ, we are called to extend hospitality and love to those around us. We welcome others in the name of Jesus.

Worship & Praise Service

@2:00pm Praise the Lord everyone and welcome to GRM! As we gather for worship, let’s remember we are in the presence of Almighty God. Jesus reassures us in Matthew 18:20, “For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.” What an amazing privilege to be where God is! Let’s stand ready to worship and open our hearts, lift up our voices and together, let’s praise Him with everything we have.

Virtual Wednesday Night Bible Study

@7:00pm Though we may be go through seasons of struggle, we always have a great Saviour and Mediator.

​

His Word stands strong and He is faithful.

​

God stands by every single Word He has said. Trust in Him, build your life on His Word – even when times get tough – His truth remains so stand on that.

Women of Grace Fellowship

Monthly 3rd Thursday @7:00pm A group that offers opportunities to connect with WOMEN OF ALL AGES spiritually, emotionally, and socially.

Men of Honor Fellowship

Monthly Last Thursday @ 7:00pm A group that offers opportunities to connect with MEN OF ALL AGES spiritually, emotionally, and socially.

Master Key Prayer Line

Saturday @9:00am We are dedicated to helping each other build and maintain our prayer life. Prayer and fasting has a powerful impact on keeping us connected to God. 

Free Food Distribution & Food Pantry

Monthly 3rd Friday @9:00am

Pantry by appointment only, but clients should be prepared to show photo ID, provide birthdates and proof of residence for all members in the household.

Welcome to 
Sharp Street United Methodist Church

All Are Welcome!

SHARP STREET HAPPENINGS!

  A Bible teaching church built on the foundations of God.  Having celebrated 200 years in 2022, Sharp Steet Church endeavors to rekindle the fire that burns within each of us to worship God and serve.  All are welcome at Sharp Street Church.  We invite persons of all ages to join us.  As a catalyst to our 200th Anniversary theme “Marching Toward Our Future” we desire to grow our youth, young adults and families population.  Come and see the heart of Christ among the Sharp Street Church family.  

Click below or visit the About Us page for joining information. 

 Join us on Sundays @ 10:00 AM for a hybrid worship service -  In person or Virtual. 
If joining virtually, services are on ZOOM and FaceBook Live (Sharp Street United Methodist Church).

Worship Experience

Approximately 1 hour in length featuring traditional spirituals to contemporary praise & worship, a powerful biblically-based and practically-laced message as well as other sensory components depending upon the liturgical season or special focus in the life of the church.

Children and Youth

Sunday School is available every week beginning at 10:15 AM for all children and lasts for the duration of the church service. 

Every 4th Sunday, the worship experience is led by and geared toward our children. 

Holy Communion

United Methodists practice open communion, meaning all at worship are invited to partake. The church proclaims that the table of Holy Communion is Christ’s table. The table is open to anyone who seeks to respond to Christ’s love and seeks to lead a new life of peace and love.

Baptism

We are always excited to welcome believers into the family of God.  Through Baptism, we are cleansed from our lives of sin and become part of the community of all believers.  It is God's gift given freely to us and we make it available to all following a time of conversation about what it means and once a time has been selected that family and friends can be present to witness this joyous occasion in your life!

Each of these opportunities is a special time of spiritual refreshment as our Pastor proclaims God’s Word, our choral and congregational singing lifts our spirits, and together we experience His presence.

South Carroll Food Pantry, a Distribution Partner of the Maryland Food Bank, provides non-perishable groceries to anyone in need. After checking in, clients are invited to our pantry area where they can choose groceries to bag or box from “supermarket” style tables. Follow us on Facebook
Questions? CommunityTable@wesleyfreedom.org
COMMUNITY TABLE

Community Table (formerly “Feed My Sheep”) is a ministry of Wesley Freedom and Eldersburg area faith communities. We serve a meal three times per week that is open to the entire community.  Come sit at the table, not just to eat, but to enjoy community and meet new friends.

 

Meals served at Noon

Mondays, Thursdays and Fridays

 

Note: We do not serve meals on the following holidays when they fall on Monday, Thursday, or Friday: New Year’s Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving and the Friday after Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day. There are several other occasions, such as funerals, when we do not serve lunch.

 

Student Service Learning Opportunity

Students in 6th grade and above may volunteer at Community Table to earn community service hours each summer. 

Want to volunteer?
Thank you for wanting to volunteer with the Community Table.   Please fill out both forms and email them to CommunityTable@wesleyfreedom.org.  Once received you will be called or emailed for an interview.

Donate

Volunteer
Our Programs

Since 1965,  Monticello Area Community Action Agency (MACAA) has led the effort to eradicate poverty and improve the lives of low-income people in Central Virginia. We provide programs that benefit children, teens, and adults in our communities.

EARLY LEARNING CENTER AT HICKORY HOPE

Located in the Hickory Hope affordable housing complex, MACAA’s newest program addresses the child care gap for Southwood families by providing access to affordable, bilingual Montessori education.

Learn more
EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE

Our Emergency Assistance services aid residents of Fluvanna, Louisa, and Nelson counties with utility payments (electric, water/sewer, and gas). Our assistance is designed to be a temporary measure only. We assess needs and link individuals to resources in the region.

Learn more
FLUVANNA FOOD PANTRY

Located in Fluvanna County, our food pantry provides those experiencing food insecurity with emergency food items once per month at no cost. We stock fresh produce, diary, meats, and non-perishable foods. 

Learn more
FLUVANNA THRIFT STORE

Located in Fluvanna County, the thrift store sells gently-used women’s, men’s, and children’s apparel, accessories, shoes, houseware, and kitchenware items at an affordable price. Every donation and purchase helps support our anti-poverty work.

Learn more
HOPE HOUSE

Since 1988, Hope House has transformed the lives of dozens of families. The transitional housing program offers intensive case management for families facing homelessness in our community. We work with up to four families at a time to provide a safe home, stabilize the family, reach employment goals, attain money management skills and maintain a healthy home environment.

NELSON AFTER SCHOOL

With sites at Rockfish Elementary and Tye River Elementary, our Nelson After School program (K-5) provides safe care and engaging activities for students during the school year.

Learn more
Learn more
PROJECT DISCOVERY

Project Discovery promotes career readiness for first-generation high school students and those from low-to-medium income households in Fluvanna, Louisa, Nelson, and Albemarle counties. Students receive assistance with college applications, locating financial aid, career planning, and preparing to excel professionally, academically, and socially after graduation.

Learn more

** STEWARDSHIP ARTICLE**

BE MORE INVOLVED
Oh come, let us sing to the Lord; let us make a joyful noise to rock of our salvation!  Let us into His presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to Him with songs of praise!

Psalm 95:1-2

In this article series, “Be More Involved,” you will discover many ways in which you may be more actively engaged in serving our Lord.  There are numerous aspects of church family life in which your unique gifts and skills are needed and can be effectively utilized.  As family members, we receive from God His boundless blessings through each other as well as give back donations of our time, talents, and treasures as offerings to our Lord that support and benefit the ministries of our church family and the surrounding community.

Consider getting involved with your fellow church family members through your musical and artistic gifts.  Feel free to share your music skills with others by joining the choir or offering to provide an instrumental or vocal solo during a worship service. Express yourself artistically by displaying bulletin boards or creating flower arrangements for the sanctuary altar.  Below are some details about how to be more active in these ways:

ALTAR GUILD LEADER NEEDED

A volunteer is needed to be the point person for the altar guild. This individual would need to serve as the contact for the guild and be responsible for passing information to the guild members, training new members, seeing that altar cloth changes are made, making sure candle sticks are polished seasonally, advertising when months are vacant, and alerting the office when supplies need to be reordered. If you are willing to serve in this capacity, please contact a member of the Church Council or the church office.

—————————————————————————————————————

COMMUNION ASSISTANTS NEEDED

We are looking for volunteers who are willing to serve as communion assistants as we are preparing to return to serving communion again with traditional individual cups and the common cup. If you are willing to serve in this capacity for the communion portion of the 8am and 11am worship services, please contact the church office and indicate whether you are an attendee of the 8am or 11am worship service. Assistants for both services will be needed. The pre-packaged communion kits will still be available for those who feel more comfortable with those, but the hope is to return to the normal setting as soon as we are able. Communion assistants will be needed so please give this prayerful consideration.

___________________________________________________________________________________

Bethany’s Needs

1.      The outdoor sign committee needs a volunteer to change the sign for one month of the year.  Training will be provided

2.      Acolytes are needed for both services; training and robes will be provided.  

3.      The choir is in need of singers for all voice parts

4.      Volunteers to assist with the Food Bank distribution are needed

There are other areas in the church that need some willing workers.  Please check the volunteer wall in the fellowship hall for some suggestions.

Food Bank

Bethany participates in the Food Bank’s food drop once a month, on the third Tuesday of each month. Volunteers from the church gather from 12:30 to 5:00 PM to pack bags for those who come needing food for their families.

Olney HELP

57 years of Helping Neighbors
1969 - 2026
​Welcome to Olney HELP!
​
We have been providing food and financial assistance to local Olney, Maryland, area families since 1969. Our clients call with varied reasons for seeking aid. Many are living on fixed incomes or find their income isn’t keeping pace with rising basic expenses. Others have experienced prolonged illness or injury which leaves them temporarily unable to work. Today’s economic climate is forcing many people to request help for the first time.

Phone: 301-774-4334
Email: Help@OlneyHelp.org
Our Services Include:
Emergency Food Assistance
Emergency food assistance—we provide a 3-5 day supply of nonperishable food and access to fresh food to individuals and families requesting help.
LEARN MORE
	
Financial Assistance
Olney HELP provides limited financial aid to prevent foreclosure or eviction, to help prevent cut-off of utilities, and to purchase essential medications.
LEARN MORE
	
Referrals
​We also provide referrals to other nonprofit, state and federal agencies that may be able to assist in providing food or financial help.
LEARN MORE

ADULT MINISTRIES

Worship is just the beginning. The Christian life is not meant to be lived alone. We were created for connection – with God and with one another. Yet each of us must take our own path, one where God is leading us to grow deeper in faith and become more like Him each day. At Community Church, we are growing to be more like Christ by knowing, loving, and serving God and others. For many, a worship service is the first step on this journey of faith. Maybe you’ve been attending here for two weeks, maybe ten years. Maybe you’ve already tried a few things; maybe you’ve just come each week and worshiped quietly.

We know one of the best ways to nurture your growing relationship with Jesus Christ is to belong to a small group of Christian friends who encourage, challenges and support you as you discern and live out your life mission. We'll help you find a group best suited for you based on where you live, your interests, or your life situation. We have a variety of groups—we're positive there's one group that will be a great place for you to belong, grow and serve. Make a Difference - Become a part of something bigger than yourself. Use your gifts and experience to serve others. Develop friendships along the way while serving in our local community, the world abroad, or within the walls of Community Church.

Adult Groups and Ministries

Community is where we connect and explore the faith with others who are also on the journey. It's the place where we begin to examine Christianity, study the Bible and discover how we can know, love and serve God each day of our lives.

Altar Guild

Maintains the appearance of altar and sanctuary for worship.

God's Kitchen

Twice a year our church volunteers to prepare and serve over 200 lunches on a Saturday at Grace UM Church, 635 Church St., Salisbury, MD.

Gull Creek Ministry

The 1st Thursday of every month a team of worship leaders from the church go to Gull Creek to conduct a worship service with Holy Communion.

Pastor's Bible Study

Usually held as a study series during the day.

Power Prayers

Our intercessory prayer group welcomes all. We are a group of loving, laughing, longing people, deepening our relationship with God ~ Father, Son and Holy Spirit ~ through prayer and study. Daily, we pray for each other and lift the names in prayer of those who share their needs and burdens with us. We meet each Wednesday from 3:00pm in the Church Library.

Multimedia Ministry

The multi-media team assists our Pastors in the church's ministries and worship services through various forms of media - audio, visual, electronic and social media. Volunteers are always needed to serve the multimedia ministry in the 3 services: 8am, 9:15am and 10:30am. The Contemporary Worship Service held at 9:15 on Sunday mornings uses the multimedia ministry the most. At the 9:15am service, the sound board operator controls all sound, including the Praise 66 band members mikes and a Power Point presentation, created by a volunteer, which guides worshipers through the service. Additional volunteers are needed to cover special events, maintain the church's website, Facebook page and Twitter feed.

United Methodist Men

The United Methodist Men meet 3rd Thursday of the month at 7:00am at Church in the Community Room. All men are invited.

United Women in Faith

Meets the 3rd Tuesday of every month September thru June at the church. All women are invited.

Hospitality Team

Providing light refreshments for church reception functions.

Congregational Care

Our Congregational Care Team provides expressions of Christ's Love by assisting Community Church at Ocean Pines members, friends, and neighbors in many ways. They serve Holy Communion to Homebound and Shut-In Members, including Residents of Catered Living and The Woodlands and Gull Creek. Periodically, they Anoint with Oil the Homebound Members they visit. They make hospital, assisted living, and home visits to those suffering from illnesses or recovering from surgery. Our Team Members offer prayers. They offer comfort and services based on individual needs. They take guidance from Pastor Dale Brown and keeps him informed of our contacts.

The Woodlands

The 1st and 3rd Sundays of every month a team of worship leaders from the church go to The Woodlands to conduct a worship service with Holy Communion.

Worship Team

Ushers, greeters, communion servers, liturgists, and readers for all three services. All men, women and confirmed youth are invited to this ministry.

Berlin nursing home

2nd and 4th Wednesdays of every month a team of worship leaders from the church go to conduct a worship service with Holy Communion.

Catered Living

2nd and 4th Thursdays of every month a team of worship leaders from the church go to conduct a worship service with Holy Communion.

The Fred Bruner Memorial Library

Our library is named after the first Pastor of Community Church. Our library

About

The Nelson County Pantry is located at 9890 Thomas Nelson Highway next to St. Mary’s Catholic Church.  The Pantry is a member of the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank. We are a privately funded, non-profit organization operated entirely by volunteers and the generous contributions from many local churches, civic organizations, businesses and private individuals.

On a monthly basis the Pantry provides supplemental food to an average of 275 households, representing approximately 800 individuals. The food purchases and donations average more than 35,000 pounds each month. Students from the North Branch School as well as other student and community volunteers work diligently to stock, pack and distribute the 700 grocery boxes needed each month.  The Pantry operates with an average of 100 volunteers contributing approximately 350 hours each month.

We are forever grateful for the community support we receive. We could not survive without your help! Our amazing volunteers show up in the freezing rain, the chilling cold, the extreme heat as well as all the beautiful days. You can’t imagine how hard they work. We marvel at how everything just seems to happen. No matter what needs to be done, these wonderful people just make it happen. All they receive for their hard work is a sincere THANK YOU from those they serve as well as each other. They are the heart and soul of our organization.

Our faithful donors provide the funds to ensure those who need food do not leave empty handed. We apply for grants to cover the maintenance and recurring expenses such as utilities, insurance and upgrading equipment, so every dollar donated for food is spent on providing as much food as possible to those who need it. We are forever grateful for your support, but more importantly, so are the people who are the beneficiaries of your generosity.

May God Bless this wonderful community we are so privileged to be a part of!

Salvation Army Programs are led by Salvation Army Officers. These officers are responsible to lead by assessing needs, delivering programs and directing ministries for our local community. From serving food to balancing the books, they function as the Executive Directors and Pastors.

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

FEEDING HOWARD COUNTY
FOOD is a HUMAN RIGHT!

We believe in the importance of giving back to others in need.

With gas and grocery prices skyrocketing, hunger in our community is unprecedented. 

1 in 3 children enrolled in Howard County Schools participates in the FARM (Free and Reduced Meals Program). And more than a third of Howard County families struggle to put food on their tables. We helped FEED HOCO distribute over 3,500,000 lbs. of food to needy families in our community. FEED HOCO distributed more food to struggling families during the pandemic than the Howard County food bank. OUR SERVICE has IMPACTED tens of thousands of people.

Founded by Sanjay and Niti Srivastava in 2018 to share the richness of Indian culture. 
Rooted in the principle of Seva (serving selflessly).
 

Free Food Distributions

No ID required. No questions asked. All are welcome. Follow the FEED HOCO Facebook page for the food distribution schedule.

https://www.facebook.com/feedhoco

​

Little Free Pantry

The Little Free Pantry is a place where neighbors can exercise compassion, trust, and mutual aid.  During COVID, we installed LFPs (Little Free Pantries) throughout Howard County. ICA's Little Free Pantries continue to offer support to struggling communities 24/7. The LFP is a safety net. The concept is simple.

TAKE WHAT YOU NEED, GIVE WHAT YOU CAN.

​

We Thank Our Sponsors

If you would like to sponsor any of ICA's programs, please email us at contact@icahoward.org

INCLEMENT WEATHER

The Food Pantry goes by the Rockingham County and Harrisonburg City’s Public Schools’ decision on closures or delays. If the schools are closed, we are closed. If the schools have an hour/2-hour delay, the pantry will open at 12:00pm.

​

Daily Masses and Parish Office go by  JMU/EMU's decision on closures or delays.

​

We send notifications about closings or cancelations through Flocknote but you can also view closings here.

​

INCLEMENCIAS
DEL TIEMPO

La Despensa de Alimentos se guia por las decisiones del Condado de Rockingham y las Escuelas Públicas de la Ciudad de Harrisonburg sobre cierres o retrasos. Si las escuelas están cerradas, nosotros también lo estamos. Si las escuelas tienen un retraso de una o dos horas, la despensa abrirá las 12:00pm.


Las misas diarias y la oficina parroquial se guian por las decisiones de JMU/EMU sobre cierres o retrasos.

 

Enviamos notificaciones sobre cierres o cancelaciones a través de Flocknote, pero también puede consultar los cierres aquí.

Sunday Worship, April 12

* * * * *


Sermon: Recognizing Jesus!
Two disciples walk with the risen Jesus on the Road to Emmaus, but they don’t recognize him. They instead share their grief and sorrow at Jesus’ death. How often it may be, as modern-day disciples, that we fail to recognize the works of Jesus when it’s right in front of us. How can we sharpen our spiritual awareness?


Two Services This Sunday
9 a.m. Traditional Service. Pipe organ hymns. Click here for a bulletin.

Tidings
Weekly reflections from Pastor Rob
Life after Easter
Pastor Rob - Apr 5th, 2026

When Jesus rose from the dead, the lives of his followers took a dramatic turn.
Imagine the resurrection wiping away their doubts, their fears, their skepticism and their depression. All that replaced by surprise and joy. They’d say, “What? He is risen?? Are you serious??? Gosh, he IS risen! Risen, indeed!! Alleluia!!!”
Mary, Peter, John, Lazarus, James, Martha – and all the rest – had to rethink their lives in light of the new reality. Jesus was, indeed, the son of God, the Messiah! And it was TRUE! All that he taught: TRUE! All that he exemplified: TRUE! All that he promised: TRUE!
…

READ MORE
Hosanna! God save us!
Pastor Rob - Mar 29th, 2026

The crowd that welcomed Jesus as he rode a donkey into Jerusalem lined the street with palm branches and cried “Hosanna!” In the language of Jesus, it literally means, “God Save us!”
 
“Hosanna! God save us!” It’s a cry. It’s a prayer. It sends to the ultimate source of life, strength and love an S.O.S. – Save Our Souls! 
 
…

READ MORE
A very good 5 1/2 years!
Pastor Rob - Mar 22nd, 2026

“For everything there is a season and a time for every matter under
heaven.” -Ecclesiastes 3:1
My heart is all sweet-and-sour – brimming with love and joyful memories
yet tinged with the heartburn of sadness as I share this news: I am retiring
this spring.
No doubt this comes as a surprise to many at St. Paul’s who knew of my
intention and expectation to serve St. Paul’s for another two years, until I
…

READ MORE
Our world that God loves
Pastor Rob - Mar 1st, 2026

"For God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but may have eternal life.” -John 3:16
﻿
This passage may be the Bible’s best-known verse. It’s quoted widely, often preached on, written on greeting cards, posters and church walls, and even displayed on signs at sporting events.
 
…

READ MORE
Lent is for liberation
Pastor Rob - Feb 22nd, 2026

I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery. -Exodus 20:2, the preamble to the Ten Commandments.

Interesting how, just before giving us the Ten Commandments, God reminds us of the Almighty’s liberating work. Interesting but not surprising. God’s prime objective for us is freedom. Always. Liberation is woven throughout the Old and New Testaments. Look for it and you’ll find it everywhere.
…

Our Programs

Each of our programs is designed to support the needs of our communities. Whether it is serving one individual or a family, the Caring Cupboard is prepared to assist them as their needs arise. 

Pantries

The Caring Cupboard runs multiple pantries out of our headquarters located at 8513 Fort Smallwood Road, Pasadena, MD 21122. The first is our food and personal hygiene pantry. Food is distributed during Fill A Bag evenings and through home delivery for those without transportation. We are also located in several schools within the county for families to reach out to their counselors for assistance.  Our second pantry is the Baby Pantry, providing formula, diapers and other items for infants. The Baby Pantry is also a part of the Southern MD American Red Cross, responding to disasters with the Red Cross. 

Little Free Pantries

Similar to Little Free Libraries, the Caring Cupboard supports Little Free Pantries. Instead of books, we place food in them! We currently have 12 LFPs throughout the county, including 6 at public libraries through a partnership with Anne Arundel County Public Libraries. This is an ongoing program with new LFPs being added continually. 

Project Bloom

In an effort to assist young ladies, we place Project Bloom Baskets in our schools containing feminine hygiene items to help in their time of need. 

Project Bloom

In an effort to assist the young ladies, the Caring Cupboard has placed Project Bloom baskets containing feminine hygiene products in our participating schools to help in those times of need. 

Baby Pantry

Families can receive assistance with baby diapers, wipes and formula by bringing an ID showing they live in Anne Arundel County, on the days we are open. 

Contact
