More Dates

The Universities at Shady Grove (USG) is implementing initiatives to encourage health and wellness on campus through Mobile Market Mondays. The Mobile Market is a program supported by the Capital Area Food Bank (CAFB), which provides fresh seasonal fruits, vegetables, and other food items at ZERO COST for distribution to the campus and community. All you need to do is stop by the ground floor of the BSE in room G318, bring your grocery bags, and pick up some food. We invite you to collect food items on the 4th Monday of every month. Participants can park in Lot 2 for free, but must pull a parking ticket from the gate. We will provide patrons with a 1 hour parking voucher once they check in at the market. Patrons will need to scan the ticket they pulled when they entered, before scanning their parking voucher to depart. Please view the USG Parking Map.

Are you interested in volunteering with us? The market will run solely on volunteer labor; we welcome all volunteers. Please sign up here.

Looking for additional food resources? Please visit our Food Access page for additional food resources at USG and within our community.

For more details and ways to get involved, please contact: usg-groveressentials@umd.edu

Note: No proof of eligibility is required. Food availability is on a first-come basis and products may vary. There will be no market when the campus is closed due to inclement weather.

WOMEN'S CHOIR

Mondays at 11 a.m.

Our Women’s Choir group meets on Monday mornings at 11 a.m. for rehearsals in the Choir Suite. Reading music is helpful but not required. And being able to “carry a tune” is well, essential! Perfect attendance is not expected, though welcome. All women are invited!

 

 

Interested? Contact Ruth Szucs at szucs@fbcrichmond.org for more information.

10K SPIRIT ZONE

April 18 | 8 a.m.-noon | Along Monument Avenue

Want to be a Good Neighbor in a Great Neighborhood?

Join us along Monument Avenue on April 18, as we cheer on runners and walkers during the Monument Avenue 10K! This is a wonderful opportunity for First Baptist folks to show encouragement and hospitality to 20,000 participants passing by.

 

 

Our Spirit Zone will be filled with music from a fantastic DJ, encouraging signs, snacks for spectators, and plenty of joyful noise as we support our community and celebrate the energy of the day.

 

 

You can participate at any point between 8-11 a.m. Volunteer for an hour or stay all morning! Worried about traffic on race day? No worries! Just follow our directions to the church to avoid road closures on race day.

 

Click this event to sign up! 

 

FROM FALLOW TO FLOURISHING WOMEN'S CONFERENCE

April 25 | 8:30 a.m.-4 p.m. | Flamming Hall

Join us April 25, from 8:30 a.m.-4 p.m. for our women’s conference “Fallow to Flourishing” based on Ecclesiastes 3: There is a season for everything. The event features guest speaker and author Tabitha Deller, with music by Skyler Cumbia.
In our lives, we have to be attuned to God’s voice as we walk with Him daily to know what season we’re in and what we’re supposed to be doing. Fallow fields lead to fruitful flourishing and being breeds a better doing. Join us as we center ourselves in a time of reflection, learning, and worship.
Cost: $25 (includes light breakfast and lunch). Childcare provided by registration only. Sign up by clicking this event.
LET'S TALK ABOUT THE FUTURE WITH MATT COOK

April 26 | Combined Sunday School | 11 a.m. service

In 2016, Richmond’s First Baptist Church invited Bill Wilson from the Center for Healthy Churches (CHC) to lead us through a visioning process we called “2020 Vision.” Now, 10 years later, we’re ready to dream again.

 

 

Matt Cook is the current director of the CHC. In his work with churches across the country he’s gained a realistic sense of how things are for the church in America, but he also has a hopeful vision of what can be.

 

 

On April 26 he will lead a combined adult Sunday school class in Flamming Hall, preach during the worship service at 11 a.m. and have lunch and conversation with the deacons afterward.

 

 

Let’s talk about the future with Matt Cook. Join us!

WELCOMING YOU HOME

Welcome to Solid Rock Church Ministries! We are delighted to have you here and invite you to explore our community of faith. Whether you’re seeking a place to worship, grow spiritually, or get involved in outreach, we believe you’ll find a home with us. Join us as we pursue God’s love, strengthen our faith, and serve those around us. We look forward to connecting with you!

ABOUT OUR CHURCH

Our church is a welcoming community focused on sharing God’s love and serving others. Through worship, fellowship, and outreach, we strive to uplift lives and spread the teachings of Jesus Christ.

READ MORE >>
Redemption Center

​The Redemption Center accepts donations of unwanted clothes, furniture, toys, and more. These items are distributed to families in need within our community, helping to provide support and relief. Your generosity makes a difference!

READ MORE >>
Food Banks

Our food bank in Madison Heights, VA, offers meals and support to those in need. With compassion and dignity, we serve our community, ensuring everyone is welcomed and cared for.

READ MORE >>
Virtual Service

Join us Sunday mornings at Solid Rock Church Ministries, whether in person or online! We offer both audio and video options for our virtual services, allowing you to worship with us from anywhere. If you can't join us on location, simply tune in online to experience our inspiring messages, uplifting music, and heartfelt prayer. No matter where you are, you can still be a part of our church family and celebrate God’s love together.

G
reetings in the awesome name of Jesus Christ, the Lord! We are excited and honored that you have visited the Epworth United Methodist Chapel website. We experience God to be a Great God who is doing great things in our church, and we invite you to involve yourself in our ministries.


Epworth Chapel is a Christ-centered faith community that seeks to “Make Disciples and Transform Lives.” We are a church where everybody is welcome and where everybody is encouraged to live out their God-given purpose. We are committed to living in God’s Greatness by, living (1) the Great Commandment (Matthew 22:36-40 ) – loving God and all of God’s people, (2) the Great Commission (Matthew 28:18-20) – spreading the Gospel through vital worship, relevant discipleship, and life-giving fellowship, and (3) the Great Requirement (Micah 6:8) – doing justice, loving mercy, and walking humbly with God.


As you browse our website, we pray that you will learn more about the Epworth Chapel family, and the ministries in which you can become involved. We invite you to continue to visit our website to get updated information on upcoming events and activities designed to glorify God and build God’s kingdom. You are also welcome to join us for our Sunday worship celebrations, Bible studies, and other church events.




We praise God for the great things the Lord has done, is doing, and will do! May God richly bless you in the days ahead.


In Christ,
Rev. Dr. C. Anthony Hunt
Senior Pastor

Centreville Methodist Church Welcomes You
Loving God and Serving our Neighbors

Welcome to Centreville Methodist Church, a congregation of the Global Methodist Church. Located in Queen Anne’s County on the Eastern Shore of Maryland, we’re a congregation of Christ-followers who seek to Love God and Love People. We aim to be a disciple-making church through the teaching of God’s Word, the guidance of the Holy Spirit, and the active ministry of serving others in the name of our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ.              We have room for you!

"I rejoiced with those who said to me, 'Let us go to the house of the Lord.'"

ABOUT

Welcome to Compassion Church of the Nazarene

We are a multi generational, family friendly church located in Lynchburg, Virginia. Together, we are excited to embark on a rewarding journey of faith, service, and love.
ABOUT US

What is a Nazarene Church?

Where is Compassion located?

What are the worship services like?

What time are the services

Is there childcare available?

What options are available for children?

Can I talk to someone before visiting?

Watch Live

Tune in live every Sunday at 9:00am. Or, visit our YouTube channel to watch content from prior Sundays. 

WATCH NOW

Opportunities for the entire Family @ Compassion Church

Compassion Youth

Connect with God and others at our vibrant youth group in Lynchburg, VA! We're dedicated to helping teens in 5th through 12th grade grow in their Christian faith. Each Sunday we kick things off with a shared meal, followed by fun activities like dodgeball, basketball, board games, and fellowship time. Afterward, we engage in worship and a relevant large group message, which then transitions into small group discussions. These small groups provide a safe space for teens to connect with peers and leaders, reflecting on the message and exploring how to apply it to their lives.

Compassion Kids

At Compassion Kids, our goal is to provide Biblical teaching in an engaging, safe and nurturing environment.  We partner with families and help equip them with the resources and support needed for children to know they are loved and known by their Creator and Savior. When you bring your child to Compassion Kids you can expect that your child will be treated with care and respect. Our services are engaging, safe, fun, and nurturing. We do our very best to ensure that your child will enter a fun environment that is conducive to growth. 

Women's Ministry

Compassion Women’s Ministry is dedicated to helping women support each other, pray together, and grow in their faith through the teachings of God’s word. We focus on fostering an environment that brings women to Christ, helping them not only understand the gospel but also respond to it meaningfully and apply it in their daily lives. In this way, we create a community of faith where each woman can thrive spiritually and emotionally.

Recovery Ministry

Celebrate Recovery at Compassion offers healing through our Christ-centered, 12-step program. Focused on God’s power to change lives and providing support from others in recovery, you can find the freedom you seek! This is not a “class,” so feel free to join us whenever you want. 

If you are a student, join “The Landing,” a separate recovery ministry dedicated to jr. and sr. high students.

Young Adults

Compassion Church Loves Young Adults! We offer many different ways to not only be a part of our Church Body, but truly become part of the Compassion Family. Our young adults aged 18-35 are a valued part of our family, and we desire them to discover a meaningful relationship with God, while building lasting community.

Sunday Mornings we offer a College and Young Adult Bible Study @ 9am Located in the Cafe of the Main Worship Center Building.

Need a ride to church? Compassion has a shuttle that runs at 8:45am and 10:15am every Sunday from Liberty University’s Book Store to our Campus.

Item 1 of 5
LEARN MORE
I’m thrilled you stopped by

Whether you’ve been in church your whole life or are brand new to the idea of faith, I want you to know that you are welcome at Compassion. We have a place for you. Join us on Sunday mornings at 9:00am or 10:30 am or check us out online and be sure to reach out so we can touch base. We want to know you and hear your story. 

In Christ,

Pastor Stephen Willis | Lead Pastor

717-642-6963

We thank Phyllis & Former Commissioner Glenn Blanchard for their time and dedication.

Maryland is home to more than 750,000 people who do not have enough to eat.

Despite its status as one of the wealthiest states in the nation, Maryland communities are deeply impacted by food insecurity. While the homeless remain the most visible faces of hunger, the need for food assistance is rising among children, seniors, and working families.

With today’s stagnant wages and the steadily rising cost of living, some individuals are working full time and still struggling to put food on the table. In fact, 38% of food-insecure individuals in our service area earn too much to qualify for federal or state relief. This means that thousands of food-insecure Marylanders rely solely on the food bank and other hunger-relief agencies as they struggle to meet their basic needs.

You can make a difference. There are many ways that you can help us fight hunger in Maryland. Whether volunteering in our warehouse, donating food and funds, or letting your elected officials know how you feel about the issue, your actions are the building blocks of our hunger-relief movement.

Food assistance is available to any Frederick County resident who is in need.  No referral is needed in order to visit the food bank.  Please refer to the Maryland Food Bank website for more information.

Food Pantry

Every day, The Salvation Army helps cure hunger
by providing food items to neighbors in need.

Pantry packages include canned goods at a minimum but often include fresh bread, bakery items, meat, and produce. As an ongoing effort to meet the needs of our neighbors, The Salvation Army Food Pantry continues to operate as a drive-thru service. Individuals and families needing immediate food assistance can drive through the Food Pantry.

Hours of Operation

Monday-Wednesday: 1:00 PM-3:00 PM

Thursday: Closed

Friday: 1:00 PM-3:00 PM

 

**UPCOMING CLOSURES**

Friday, April 3, 2026

Monday, May 25, 2026

Friday, July 3, 2026

Monday, September 7, 2026

Friday, November 27, 2026

Friday, December 25, 2026

Details

Portions given will depend on the amount of stock on hand.
 

Frequency - Customers can come for a full distribution once per calendar month though USDA canned goods can be picked up weekly if needed.

Documentation - Customers will be asked to verbally provide their name, address, and income/sources during their first visit and to sign TEFAP/USDA paperwork at every visit. No ID is required for pickup.

Alternates - If you cannot pick up your food yourself, you must attend at least once each year (July-June) to sign paperwork and assign an alternate to make your pickups. Assigned alternates must bring a picture ID! Alternates can also pick up for a previously registered applicant with a written note from the applicant. Still they would need to provide a new note at each pick-up until the applicant officially assigned them as an alternate on our SDI form.

“Under Federal Law and U.S. Department of Agriculture policy, this Agency is prohibited from discriminating based on race, color, national origin, sex, age or disability.”

www.fns.usda.gov/cr/fns-nondiscrimination-statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

FIND TREASURES

Every visit to Tabitha’s House is a chance to discover something special. Whether it's a vintage gem, a cozy sweater, or the perfect piece for your home, our shelves are full of surprises donated with love by our community. When you shop here, you're not just finding treasures — you're becoming part of a story that gives back.

FUEL CHANGE

Your purchase does more than bring joy to your day. It helps power our mission to serve neighbors in need. Every dollar spent at Tabitha’s House provides support, resources, and hope to local families. When you shop with us, you're not just taking something home. You're helping someone take a step forward.

A THRIFT STORE
OF THE EPICENTER

As a strategic way to expand impact, Tabitha’s House merged with The EPICENTER, a nonprofit community center with locations in Edgewood and Aberdeen. The EPICENTER also has a proud history and reputation for serving the community and is deeply engaged with many of the families that we serve, many of whom live in the Rt 40 area. This partnership and integration into the good work that The EPICENTER is doing makes us even more effective in our mission to empower people, strengthen families, and build a sense of community and trust.

SERVICES AT SAMARITAN COMMUNITY
"YOU GAVE ME A BAG OF FOOD, AND HELPED ME WITH MY RENT PAYMENT AND BUS FARE TO JOB INTERVIEWS, AS WELL AS ASSISTING ME IN MY JOB SEARCH.  

AND, MOST OF ALL, YOU GAVE ME THE LOVE, RESPECT, AND HOPE I NEEDED TO SUCCEED!"
        - MEMBER

Each year, we proudly serve individuals and families living in Baltimore City. We believe that "the client is the program". Each person's specific needs determine the services we provide. Our eligibility criteria are nothing more than the vital needs of those who come to Samaritan Community. 

Those who initially come to us are first interviewed by our Program Director. Their needs are carefully assessed and a personalized plan is created to establish appropriate support and services for the individual and his/her family. 

Services are broken into two main categories: "Crisis Intervention Assistance" and "Empowerment". Our "Crisis Intervention Assistance" Program addresses the acute, immediate needs of our members and includes our food pantry, Clothing Shop, case management services, a computer workroom, and emergency financial assistance. Our "Empowerment" Program helps members with more-complicated, long-term problems and includes individual empowerment counseling, group support, and life enrichment activities. 

We work to provide assistance that members cannot easily access elsewhere. But, we also work within a network of established social service programs. We help members learn about their choices and teach them how to obtain additional services for themselves. 

"CRISIS INTERVENTION ASSISTANCE"
FOOD PANTRY

Many clients initially come to Samaritan Community through our food pantry. We distribute groceries in three-day supplies, or for longer, if necessary. We strive to provide as much fresh and healthy food as possible. We purchase items from The Maryland Food Bank, but also receive generous donations from Whole Foods Market Mt. Washington and other suppliers. 

When COVID hit in 2020, we transitioned our in-person pantry to deliveries directly to members’ homes. We continue this service today. In 2024, we provided roughly 300 households with 3,400 bags of fresh and healthy foods through deliveries, in-person pantry visits, and more. Through the generosity of Whole Foods Market Mt. Washington, we provide  fresh produce, wholesome breads, dairy, poultry, seafood, and shelf-stable items.

For more information on our pantry and its hours, please call 410-669-1229.

CLOTHING SHOP

Members also have access to our clothes and household goods "Shop". We provide gently-used household items and clothing (for men, women, children, and infants) free-of-charge. Learn more about donating to our "Shop". In 2024, over 200 members came to the Shop 514 times. 

An appointment for the Clothing Shop is required. To make an appointment, please call 410-669-1229.

CASE MANAGEMENT

Our programming staff work to help members through many types of difficult and often-complicated situations. Our case management services include (but are not limited to): helping members access other social services, obtain necessary documents/licenses, find employment and housing, help navigate the court system, and more. As a result of deep government cuts in the social services net, demand for our Case Management Services has dramatically increased. In 2024, our programming staff provided 1,140 sessions to help over 160 members with housing, utilities, medical care, transportation, legal issues, employment, and more. 

For more information on our case management services, please call 410-669-1229.

EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE

Our mission to provide crisis intervention includes financial assistance. Stipends to individuals and families often prevent challenges from becoming major crises. Our staff members carefully evaluate circumstances and provide financial assistance related to emergencies involving housing, including eviction prevention. Emergency financial assistance is also provided for medical care, utilities, transportation, legal help, employment, and more. 

In 2017, we began providing more significant financial assistance through our Farnham-Krieger Endowment Fund. 

Stipends help members and their families with rent, utilities, transportation, employment-related expenses, medical care, personal supplies, and more. In 2024 alone, we provided more than $250,000 to help with housing, utilities, medical care, employment, transportation, legal help, and more. 

"EMPOWERMENT" PROGRAM
INDIVIDUAL EMPOWERMENT COUNSELING

People who are struggling with long-term problems receive support tailored to their specific needs and circumstances. Members receive this service from members of our programming staff. 

While most sessions are conducted at The Samaritan Community, sessions are sometimes handled by phone or in-home if it is helpful for the member. We also utilize counseling efforts by functioning within a sys

The Food Pantry is held three times a month at the Cryer Center: second Tuesday of the month, fourth Tuesday of the month and third Saturday of the month.   

​

Leave a message at 804-758-2044 for emergency food and home repair assistance. 

VOLUNTEER
The Cryer Center Yard Sales are held once a month, on the second Saturday from 7:30 am to 11:00 am.

The Clothing Closet is open to the public on the second and fourth Saturdays from 8 am to noon.

The Clothing Closet will be open to Hands clients on the first Thursday of the month from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m.
 
Donations of furniture, household items, clothing and other items in good condition are welcome as their sale helps to support HANDS activities. Household items can be placed in one of the open trailers at the back of the Center and clothing placed in bins in the front of the building. Call 804-758-2044 and leave a message if you would like to schedule a large furniture pickup.  
DONATE

In 2023, HANDS averaged feeding 419 families a month. This includes 675,000 pounds of food though the Food Pantry, BackPackBuddy and Adopt a Senior Programs.

HANDS is a 501 (c)(3) nonprofit organization which relies upon donations and volunteers for its operations.  Donations are tax deductible and receipts are available.  

 

HANDS is able to provide services to residents of Middlesex County only through the continuing generosity of individuals, organizations and churches.

Our Lady of Lourdes Church believes strongly in faith in action!  We seek to live out the Catholic Church’s teachings on social justice and human dignity.  For additional information or to volunteer, please contact Joan Dufour. 

Ministries we support:
Rachel’s Vineyard:

Rachel’s Vineyard believes that everyone can experience the love, mercy, and forgiveness God has for them. Are you, or is somebody you know, suffering from the impact of an abortion? Hope and healing are possible! To learn about the Rachel’s Vineyard retreat ministry sponsored by our parish, please call the confidential line at 804-432-2589 or check out www.rv4hope.org.

Marty Montgomery-Jennings 804-334-7953

Maggie Carlson 804-432-2589

Matt Freeman 434-825-9661

 

Mary’s Choice RVA Mission:

Mary’s Choice RVA envisions a world in which every woman faced with an unexpected pregnancy can transform her life and make a new beginning with the support she needs in a loving, safe home in which she can give her baby the ultimate gift: life. For more information, go to https://www.maryschoicerva.org/ .

 

Food Pantry: Our Lady of Lourdes’ food pantry feeds many individuals and families each month. We distribute food on Mondays and Thursdays from 11:00am to 11:30am by appointment. We also serve those who are in emergency situations.

Learn more: See how your contributions to Human Concerns makes a difference: Sharing God’s Blessings.

 

Laura’s Closet: Laura’s closet is now open!  We accept donations Monday through Thursday from 9am to 2pm, or on the weekends during Masses. The donations may be placed in the foyer right inside the main doors in the labeled wooden boxes.

Laura’s Closet is open to the public from 9:30am-11am each Tuesday.

Do you know how Laura’s Closet got it’s name?  Click here for a history of this important ministry.

Doctor Assisted Suicide:

In prayer and in public, your voices are urgently needed to bring Gospel values to bear on vital decisions being made by those who represent you. Learn more about this issue from the USCCB by clicking here.

Pray for Life:

Is Abortion Really Healthcare? Click here to hear three arguments to this question.

Human Trafficking: Human trafficking violates the sanctity, dignity, and fundamental rights of the human person. The United Nations Protocol to Prevent, Suppress, and Punish Trafficking in Persons defines it as “the recruitment, transportation, harboring or receipt of persons by means of force, fraud or coercion…for the purpose of exploitation.” According to the U.S. State Department, human trafficking appears in “many guises”, often taking the form of commercial sexual exploitation, the prostitution of minors, debt bondage, and involuntary servitude. The United States government, and increasingly the international community, utilize the umbrella term “trafficking in persons” to define all forms of modern slavery.  For statistics, common myths, and the Catholic Response on Human Trafficking, click here.

 

Thank you for your support! ¡Gracias por su apoyo!
♥ Support Lourdes Today

MONTGOMERY COUNTY CHAPTER
		
	
WELCOME TO THE MONTGOMERY COUNTY CHAPTER OF THE SO WHAT ELSE FOOD RESCUE BANK!

From our food pantry and thrift store, to our home deliveries program, to pop-up distributions in Gaithersburg, So What Else has plenty of ways to connect you with the resources you need.

Our Montgomery County food pantry and thrift store are located at 4924 Wyaconda Rd, North Bethesda, MD 20852.
Food Pantry Schedule (BY APPOINTMENT ONLY):
Mondays: Indoor Pantry 8:30AM-3:30PM (closed 12-12:30PM)
Tuesdays: Indoor Pantry 8:30AM-4:30PM (closed 12-12:30PM
Wednesdays: Indoor Pantry 8:30AM-4:30PM (closed 12-12:30PM)
Thursdays: Indoor Pantry 8:30AM-4:30PM (closed 12-12:30PM)
Fridays: Indoor Pantry 8:00AM-2:30PM (closed 12-12:30PM)
Saturdays: Outdoor Food Pantry 7:00AM-1:00PM
Sundays: CLOSED
Thrift Store and Diaper Distribution Schedule:
Mondays-Thursdays: 10AM-4PM (closed 12-12:30PM)
Fridays-Sundays: CLOSED

If you have any questions or would like to schedule a tour, please email us at info@sowhatelse.org.

FOOD ASSISTANCE

Montgomery County Food Pantry

On Mondays through Fridays, clients with appointments can receive food from our indoor food pantry. On Saturdays, clients with appointments can receive food from our drive-up or walk-up outdoor food pantry.

Schedule a Weekday Appointment Schedule a Saturday Appointment
If you have received food assistance, please take our Client Satisfaction Survey. The survey is available in three languages:
English
Español
食物救助计划调查问卷
Weekday Appointment FAQs Saturday Appointment FAQs

HOME DELIVERIES

If you are a homebound individual and are unable to stop by our pantry or pop-up distributions due to a disability or lack of transportation, consider enrolling in our home delivery program!

You will receive one bag of food every two weeks, the quantity and variety of which is subject to change based on supplies.
This program is entirely volunteer-driven, so the days and times of your delivery are subject to change based on our volunteer capacity.
Due to high demand, you may be placed on a waitlist if you reside outside of our geographic zone. Please be patient as we take time to process your request.

To request enrollment in the home delivery program: Please email Megan Joe at mjoe@sowhatelse.org and include your name, address and phone number.


GAITHERSBURG FOOD DISTRIBUTIONS

We host pop-up food distributions in Gaithersburg. We may close due to weather, so please follow us on social media (Facebook, Instagram) for the latest news on our schedule.

Pathways Church: Thursdays from 10:00AM to 12:00PM | 200 W Diamond Ave, Gaithersburg, MD 20877
South Lake Elementary (Parking Lot): Tuesdays from 11:20AM to 1:30PM | 18201 Contour Rd, Gaithersburg, MD 20877

OTHER FOOD ASSISTANCE

If you are unable to secure an appointment with us, please see these resources for other food assistance sites:

Montgomery County Food Council’s Food Assistance Resource Map
Montgomery County Office of Food Systems Resilience’s Food Distribution Events calendar

THRIFT STORE

Our Montgomery County thrift store offers clothing, shoes, home goods, toys, books and more at no cost. No appointment is needed to visit the thrift store.

We also accept donations of clothing, shoes, home goods, electronics, small appliances, toys and games, diapers and baby wipes, cleaning supplies, toiletries and feminine hygiene products. Donations can be dropped off at our Montgomery County food pantry and thrift store at 4924 Wyaconda Road, North Bethesda, MD.


DIAPER DISTRIBUTION

We offer diapers at no cost. Diapers are available at our Montgomery County location at 4924 Wyaconda Road, North Bethesda, MD. No appointment is needed to pick up diapers.

Eligibility: Diapers are provided for children born between 2022 and 2026.
Please bring: 1) your (parent or guardian) ID, and 2) something with the child’s name and date of birth (DOB) on it (birth certificate, medical paperwork, insurance card).
You can receive 2 packs of diapers (50 diapers total) per child once a month.

DO YOU NEED HELP WITH SNAP OR MEDICARE?

So What Else is partnering with Fabric Health to provide free assistance and resources on SNAP and Medicare applications. Get your questions answered, submit your application, and stay in the know about key dates.

>>>Get started here<<<

DO YOU NEED SIGN LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE?

On Tuesdays and Thursdays from 9:30AM to 4PM at the Food Rescue Bank, So What Else will have volunteers providing sign language assistance to those in need during food pick-up appointments.

In addition to our walk-up pantries, we provide direct food support to communities across Maryland and Washington DC.

These locations are community-specific and not open to walk-ups.

Search Location
Contact Details

So What Else Food Rescue Bank: 4924 Wyaconda Rd, North Bethesda, MD 20852

Baltimore Resource Center: 424 S Pulaski St, Baltimore, MD 21223

Loaves and Fishes
End hunger in our Community

Loaves and Fishes aims to help reduce food scarcity in our community. All foods are free for those in need. 

Year round, we provide high-quality ingredients for meals, prepared box-dishes, and healthy snacks. Over holiday seasons we take care of the families around us, making sure they have goodies to celebrate the season, like a turkey for the entire family. 

Whether you are in need of food or want to donate and distribute, there is a place for you in this ministry.

24-HOUR CARE

Grassroots provides free, 24-hour mental health, substance use, crisis and homeless services support to individuals and families.

Call 410-531-6677, call/chat/text 988 or walk in anytime at 8990 Old Annapolis Road, Suite A in Columbia.

* Interpretation services are available for those who do not speak English or who are deaf or hard of hearing.

SERVICES
CRISIS LINE
988 CRISIS CHAT & TEXT
WALK-IN CRISIS ASSISTANCE
SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES
MOBILE CRISIS TEAM
EMERGENCY SHELTER
DAY RESOURCE CENTER
OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CLINIC (OMHC)
OUTREACH AND PREVENTION
MILITARY
24-HOUR CRISIS LINE

Grassroots operates a 24-hour crisis intervention and supportive counseling hotline. Individuals can call 410-531-6677 for a variety of reasons, including suicide, family and relationship problems, violent or threatening domestic situations, loneliness or depression, substance use issues, housing needs and others.

LEARN MORE
988 CHAT & TEXT

Grassroots provides 24-hour support to individuals who call 988, as well as those who contact 988 via chat and text. We support people at both a state and national level. 

LEARN MORE
WALK-IN COUNSELING

Visit us 24 hours a day at 8990 Old Annapolis Road, Suite A in Columbia. 
Grassroots provides free, 24-hour walk-in counseling for a variety of issues, including mental health, substance use, housing and other related crises. Individuals can access a full pantry of food and hygiene items.

LEARN MORE
SUBSTANCE USE SERVICES

Visit us 24 hours a day at 8990 Old Annapolis Road, Suite A in Columbia.
Grassroots supports individuals who are dealing with substance use concerns by providing free screening, assessment, counseling and referral to treatment, along with short-term withdrawal management and peer support. 

LEARN MORE
MOBILE CRISIS TEAM

Licensed clinicians from Grassroots respond 24 hours a day in person to mental health and other crises in community settings in Howard County.

LEARN MORE
EMERGENCY SHELTER

Grassroots operates Howard County’s emergency shelter at 6700 Freetown Road in Columbia. The program includes 89 total beds – 51 beds for families, 24 beds for single adults and 20 beds for the 2025-26 Cold Weather Shelter Program.

LEARN MORE
DAY RESOURCE CENTER AT LEOLA DORSEY

The Grassroots Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey provides resources, hot meals, showers and laundry facilities, as well as a food and clothing pantry, to unsheltered, homeless and low-income individuals. It is located at 10390 Guilford Road, Suite A, in Jessup, MD.

LEARN MORE
OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CLINIC

Grassroots provides free, in-person therapy and medication management appointments within 48 hours, if not sooner, to consumers who present with a mental health concern and who face a barrier to mental health treatment.

LEARN MORE
OUTREACH & PREVENTION

Grassroots supports individuals in the community with education and suicide prevention resource. The Survivors of Suicide Loss Support group is a free and confidential support group that provides a safe place for individuals to share their thoughts, feelings and experiences related to the loss of a loved one to suicide.

LEARN MORE
FREE, 24-HOUR SUPPORT FOR SERVICE MEMBERS, VETERANS AND THEIR FAMILIES

Grassroots provides free, 24-hour crisis, mental health and substance use support to service members, veterans, spouses and their families throughout Maryland. Military personnel can access immediate, compassionate and confidential care to meet their specialized needs. No matter why type of issue you might be dealing with, Grassroots is here to ensure you don’t have to do it alone.

LEARN MORE

WE MEET EACH SUNDAY AT 9:30 FOR SUNDAY SCHOOL/BIBLE STUDY AND AT 11:00 IN THE SANCTUARY FOR WORSHIP.

EACH WORSHIP SERVICE IS LIVE-STREAMED VIA YOUTUBE AND A RECORDED VERSION IS AVAILABLE VIA THIS PAGE (see URL below) AND VIA OUR FACEBOOK PAGE AS WELL AS IT IS MADE AVAILABLE ON LOCAL CABLE TV CHANNEL 13 EACH FOLLOWING SUNDAY AT 7:00.

NOTE: TO VIEW PREVIOUS SERVICES BEGINNING WITH THE MARCH22,2020 VIDEO, CLICK ON WORSHIPING TOGETHER PAGE.

Welcome to Belmont Baptist Church and our website!  Thank you for stopping by and checking out our home on the web.  We look forward to getting to know you and trust that you will find out more about Belmont Baptist on the site.  We hope you will join us in worship and in living out the gospel together in the Charlottesville community.

We are a congregation of Cooperative Baptists located in the historic Belmont Neighborhood in Charlottesville, Virginia.

Please Join Us Each Sunday for Sunday School and Worship Service:  

9:30 a.m.  – Sunday School/Bible Study Each Sunday.

11:00 a.m.  Worship Services Each Sunday In The Sanctuary.
Note: The service also is live-streamed via YouTube each Sunday. In addition, the service is available at your discretion via this current web page (see above), and it can be viewed on local TV cable channel 13 the following Sunday at 7:00 p.m.

6:30 p.m. – Wednesday Evening Crossroads Bible Study and Prayer Time in the Fellowship Hall and Via ZOOM

If transportation is needed for Sunday Morning Worship activities, please call BBC office at 434-296-7111.

Ministry Within Our Community

​

ALCOHOLICS ANONYMOUS

Alcoholics Anonymous is a fellowship of men and women who share their experience, strength and hope with each other that they may solve their common problem and help others to recover from alcoholism.  The only requirement for membership is a desire to stop drinking.  There are no dues or fees for AA membership; we are self-supporting through our own contributions.  AA is not allied with any sect, denomination, politics, organization or institution; does not wish to engage in any controversy, neither endorses nor opposes any causes.  Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics to achieve sobriety. 

 

Alcoholics Anonymous meets at Mt. Harmony Fellowship Hall on Monday and Thursday evenings at 8:00 pm.  For more information on AA, call 1-800-492-0209 or visit their website at www.marylandaa.org. 

 

 

ANGEL TREE

Each year we have an Angel Tree, coordinated by the UMW, as a mission to help those less fortunate by providing gifts at Christmas.  Included on the tree are people who have been provided to us from Social Services, the Board of Child Care, the Calvert County Nursing Center, and local families who have been brought to our attention from members of the church.  This year we will include tags on the tree for the Henderson Settlement for the purchase of gift cards.

 

Some gifts can be wrapped, others must be un-wrapped.  The tags will note what to do with your gift.  The Angel Tree usually goes up the end of October or the first of November.  Gifts are due back to the church by the first of December.  To learn more about this mission contact 410-257-2761.

 

 

BOARD OF CHILD CARE

We serve children and families who require physical, emotional, behavioral and social support.  Through residential and community based services, the Agency will provide a safe, healthy and caring environment that seeks to preserve children and families by fostering self-esteem, respect for community, a sense of purpose and life skills needed to achieve their highest potential.

 

CHURCHES COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY

The food pantry provides supplement food packages to 200 to 400 families per month and over 500 families at Thanksgiving and Christmas.  They are open to anyone in the northern part of the County without regard to race, creed, ethnic background, age or religion.  The food pantry is currently supported by nine churches of varying denominations in the northern area of Calvert County and is managed by a board of directors composed of representatives from these churches.  Mark Kromer and Nancy Adams, with Brian Doherty providing ad hoc assistance, are serving as Mt. Harmony-Lower Marlboro UMC representatives.  To learn more about this mission, or to volunteer, contact Mark Kromer at 443-964-5977 or email mnlkromer@comcast.net.

​

 

COMFORT  SYMPATHY  MEALS

Sometimes in this everyday life we take so many things for granted, and this can be true for our families!  We don’t realize when we lose someone that’s an important part of our lives and family how truly devastating that can be.  As we try to make sense of it all, it’s a good thing to know that we have such a loving and caring church family available here at Mt. Harmony/Lower Marlboro Church.  Many of us know of  loved ones lost over the past months and how very important our “sympathy/comfort” meals are and how they have touched the family members we have served.  Please know that these families are very appreciative and comforted with this gesture.  To learn more about this mission or to volunteer contact Carol Terry through the church office.

​

 

GOOD NEIGHBOR PROJECT 

The Neighbor Project, a home-grown outreach ministry at Mt. Harmony/Lower Marlboro, began in 1995 as a partnership with the Department of Social Services (DSS) and the Calvert County Office on Aging. We primarily serve senior citizens who live alone, delivering nonperishable food items and making visits to them at least five times a year.

 

We have often included members of our congregation who found themselves temporarily or indefinitely unable to get out easily. Delivering food baskets with caring visits are the part of the Neighbor Project that most people hear about in church. But there is more that we do—and more that we can do with your help—such as calling in bad weather, helping with a special need, offering a ride to go to the grocery store or doctor. To learn more about this mission or to volunteer, contact Kirsti Uunila at 410-610-1110, or email  blueheron77@comcast.net.

 

 

HARVEST FOR HUNGER

Farming4Hunger is a local Calvert County 501c3 charity started by the son of former Maryland Senator Bernie Fowler.  Farming4Hunger has teamed up with the Capital Area Food Bank and the Maryland Food Bank to grow fresh produce to serve needy families in the region, including Calvert County.  The son has negotiated with various farmers in the county who still farm for a living to lease

Crisis Assistance Response Emergency Shelter
CARES operates the only emergency shelter for women and children in the Tri-Cities area. We serve the communities of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights, Dinwiddie, Hopewell, Petersburg and Prince George.  CARES also operates a Food Pantry and Clothes Closet for low-income residents in the Tri-Cities.  If you need help and are not sure where to turn, call CARES at (804) 861-0865.
Services for Women and Children

CARES is dedicated to helping your family regain stability, dignity, and self-sufficiency. We offer emergency shelter, workshops and training, job search assistance, and networking opportunities. Our Educational Coordinator ensures that your children are enrolled in school, provided with necessary supplies, and have access to enrichment activities and tutoring to support their academic success.

Read More

Community Services

Are you struggling to make ends meet? The CARES Food Pantry and Clothes Closet offer vital resources to individuals and families who have nowhere else to turn for support.

Read More

How to Help

At CARES, we believe that community support is key to creating lasting change. There are several ways you can make a difference in the lives of those we serve:

Donate: Your generous contributions of food, clothing, household items, or monetary donations directly impact individuals and families in need.
Volunteer: Share your time and skills by volunteering at our shelter, food pantry, or service center. Your involvement helps us provide essential services to those facing crisis.
Fundraise: Organize a fundraising event or campaign to help support CARES’ mission of empowering individuals and families on their path to stability.
Advocate: Spread the word about our services and help raise awareness about homelessness and poverty in our community.

Every action, big or small, helps build a stronger, more compassionate community. Together, we can make a lasting impact.

Find Out More

We’ll Put Your Goods to Good Use!

We invite you to support our efforts by donating items from our wish list. The shelter has an ongoing need for diapers, cleaning supplies, hygiene items and gently used clothing or household items. Additionally, you can make a big impact by organizing a group effort! Scout troops, church groups, or community organizations can hold food drives or collect essential items like school supplies, hats, and gloves.

ST. VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY (HELPING THE POOR IN OUR COMMUNITY)
St. Vincent de Paul Society (Helping the Poor in our Community)

The St. Vincent de Paul Society is an international Catholic organization that serves those in need. Through its network of Parish Conferences, the Society of St. Vincent de Paul helps people while leading its members to grow spiritually through service.

Our conference at St. John was established in 1993 assisting those in need in Carroll County.  The St. John Westminster Conference meets regularly to provide for needs in a manner that promotes dignity and respect.  There are no special qualifications other than the desire to join with others in helping those in need of the love of God.  As a group we meet on the first Monday of each month at 10:30 in the Portico, September through June.

How do we serve?

Person to Person Service

Located in the Parish Center, the office is open two hours daily, Tuesday through Thursday, 10:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m..  Volunteers, known as Vincentians, meet with our neighbors who are requesting assistance with various financial needs and circumstances including evictions, medications, and utility shut offs.  We also have a food pantry that is available to all during our business hours or by appointment.

Loaves and Fishes Soup Kitchen

Our Vincentians prepare and serve a nutritious lunch every Wednesday at The Church of the Ascension, 23 N. Court Street, Westminster.  All are welcome!

Adopt-a-Family Program

For the Christmas season, through a partnership with the Salvation Army, our conference coordinates a gift distribution program.  Names of individuals and families in need are gathered and “adopted” by our parishioners by selecting a card from the Giving Trees in the Church.  Our parishioners provide  toys, clothing, and food based on the needs of the “adoptee.”

All assistance we provide is funded by donations from our parishioners, either through the Poor Box, check, online giving or food for our pantry.  If you are interested in learning more about volunteer opportunities or other ways of supporting the works of your St. Vincent de Paul Society conference, please contact Cathy Hoffman at 410-848-8693 or svdp@sjwest.org.  Your involvement in this important mission is greatly appreciated. Please pray for the works of The St. Vincent de Paul Society and those we serve.

Contact: Cathy Hoffman
Phone: 410-848-8693
Email: svdp@sjwest.org

Emergency Shelter

The Rescue Mission is open to serve 365 days a year. We welcome all who are in need and wish to seek our services.

We are excited to introduce a new system for our Emergency Shelter. This system will be focused on maximizing the potential of each guest in a way that best fits where they are, and what services they are open to.

ENTRY
Entry point for all new guests
Will be ongoing, available nightly
Includes Meals, Emergency Shelter and Fralin Free Clinic Services
ESTABLISH
For guests who are ready to take advantage of the full services offered by the Rescue Mission and our partners
Includes all the benefits of Entry plus:
Case Management
Guest Development Classes & Coaching
Chapel & Spiritual Direction
Each Establish guest will be on an individualized plan with demonstrated progress toward their personal goals and coordinated care from all Mission Services
Establish guests have the opportunity to serve on a Guest Council to provide input back to staff on needs and improvements
EMPOWER

Coming Soon!

WHAT DO I NEED TO DO TO STAY IN SHELTER?

Shelter is open from 3:00pm to 9:00am. Intakes are daily and will begin when the shelter opens.

Individuals or families wishing to stay the night in the Women and Children’s Shelter need to bring:

One large suitcase per person
Medications
Custody papers, if applicable

Individuals wishing to stay the night in Men’s Shelter are asked to bring:

One large suitcase per person
Medications

For more information about accessing our Men’s Shelter, Women’s Shelter, Family Shelter, and Respite Care, call (540) 343-7227. For more information about what you need to do if you need to stay at the Rescue Mission, check out our Frequently Asked Questions.

Food Recovery is a Beautiful Thing!
We Feed People With Food and LOVE.

At Celestial Manna, we transform surplus into salvation — feeding the hungry and comforting the weary through the love of Messiah.
Because no one should be forgotten. Every life matters to Him — and to us.

Request Food
Donate
Where Every Meal Tells a Story of Hope in Messiah.

Celestial Manna is a faith-driven nonprofit dedicated to rescuing surplus food and delivering it to individuals and families in need. Each meal provides nourishment, love, dignity, and the hope of the Lord God. Beyond food, we offer spiritual support, reminding those who feel forgotten that they are deeply valued and never alone.

Founded in Maryland, Celestial Manna now serves communities in California and Nevada, with a vision to expand nationwide. Guided by compassion and faith, we transform abundance into blessings—sharing sustenance and the lasting hope of the Lord God.

Virtual Food Drive

Even if you can’t shop for food, you can still sow seeds of hope. Every dollar you donate multiplies into meals that nourish hungry families and share the love of Messiah. Together, we can feed bodies and uplift hearts.

Donate now
GuideStar 
Recognition

Our GuideStar recognition reflects our unwavering commitment to transparency, providing current and accurate information to donors, partners, and supporters who believe in our mission.

Donate
Give Today, Change a Life

When you give, you do more than provide food — you share love, hope, and the light of Messiah with those who are struggling. Donate easily through your bank account, credit/debit card, or PayPal. Together, we can bring nourishment and new beginnings to families in need.

Volunteer
Join Us in the Fight Against Hunger

Make a lasting impact by volunteering with our food bank. Your time and compassion can help feed families and share the love of Messiah with those in need. Click below to discover how you can get involved today.

Ongoing Monthly Support
20,000+

Each month, Celestial Manna provides vital food assistance to more than 20,000 people across Metro DC, Maryland, Northern Virginia, Las Vegas, Nevada, Monterey, California, and beyond — bringing nourishment, hope, and the love of Lord God to communities in need.

Our Food RUN is 
Daily Recovery and Pick-up
Pick Up From Stores and Partners

Our dedicated volunteers visit partner stores and suppliers to collect surplus food, ensuring that every donation is handled with care and purpose to serve those in need.

Distribution Among Organizations

After careful classification, the food is delivered to trusted organizations — including churches, schools, and community centers — to reach individuals and families who need it most

Selection & Classification

Once the food is collected, our volunteers carefully sort and classify each item to ensure quality and proper distribution to families in need.

Sharing Hope Within the Community

Through our partner organizations, food is lovingly delivered to families and individuals in need, bringing not just nourishment, but a reminder of God’s provision and unwavering love.

Celestial Manna Donates Over 6 million pounds of Food Each Year

Institutions We Support 

We recognize the immense effort required to help those in need, so we partner with like-minded organizations to advance our mission. For more than 35 years, Celestial Manna has been instrumental in supplying food to battered women’s shelters, hospice centers, homeless shelters, veterans’ residences, food banks, churches, and many other vital community services.

THE MANY WAYS WE HELP

We equip individuals and families in need with essential food and household supplies—everything from non-perishable groceries and clothing to cookware and children’s toys—so no home goes without the basics. Recognizing that reliable transportation is often a hurdle to employment, we also accept and award donated vehicles to those who need them most.

Our work is made possible by the generosity of donors who believe in our mission and partner with us to transform lives.
DONATE
Food Assistance

We provide fresh, nutritious food every day at multiple community distribution sites, ensuring families and individuals have reliable access to healthy meals.

Request Food
Prayer Outreach

We stand with you in faith, offering confidential prayer support for every need and circumstance. Submit your request and our dedicated intercession team will pray earnestly for God’s comfort, healing, and guidance.

Request Prayer
Celestial Manna earned a Platinum Seal of Transparency

Celestial Manna’s Platinum Seal of Transparency—GuideStar’s highest honor—recognizes our unwavering commitment to accountability, open communication, and comprehensive impact reporting.
This designation assures donors and partners that every resource we receive is managed with integrity and maximum effectiveness.

Food Ministry

 

 

You pray for the hungry. Then you feed them. That is how prayer works.

Pope Francis

The food ministry at St. Stephen’s is inspired by the Eucharist, the central act of worship in the Christian faith.

Through the Eucharist, Christians believe that they are fed by God so that they can feed others. The food ministry at St. Stephen’s is designed to transform this belief into practice by offering ways to help people lead healthier lives that make them better stewards of God’s creation.

The food ministry at St. Stephen’s is changing lives and building community in the Richmond area. Its various facets are described below.

Food Pantry/Grocery Store
Fruit Ministry
The Farmers Market
Sunday Community Suppers
Thanksgiving Meals
St. Stephen’s Grocery Store

We provide nourishing foods to those in the community who cannot afford them using a “grocery store” or client-choice model. Parishioners bring food items to church each Sunday that are subsequently organized and assembled for stocking in our pantry. In addition to shelf-stable foods, our this ministry receives fresh produce, eggs and other food from generous farmers market vendors. Clients can select foods that are most suitable for their households, restoring a measure of dignity while reducing food waste.

Our grocery store is open Mondays from 1 p.m. until 3 p.m., year-round. Hunger does not take a vacation. When children from low-income households are at home during school vacations and holidays, they are removed from a key source of nutritious food. It’s so important that we maintain our ability to respond to the need for groceries year-round, even as many of our donors are out of town on summer vacations. (When the parish office is closed for a holiday, the food pantry distribution usually takes place the next day, Tuesday. When this is the case, regular visitors are informed the week before.)

Do you need food?

We are here to help.

Our pantry/grocery store is open on Mondays from 1 p.m. to 3 p.m. When a holiday falls on Monday, we often move the food pantry hours to Tuesday.
You do not need to bring an I.D.
St. Stephen’s Church is located at the corner of Grove Avenue and Somerset Avenue (just east of the intersection of Grove Avenue and Three Chopt Road) in the City of Richmond. The #77 Grove Avenue bus stops right by St. Stephen’s at Grove and Three Chopt.
There is a shorter wait if you come at 2 p.m. or later. We will not run out of food!
Vendors at  the Farmers Market @ St. Stephen’s are able to accept SNAP benefits and Virginia Fresh Match. The market is open Saturday mornings from 8 a.m. to noon (spring, summer, early fall) and from 9 a.m. to noon (late fall, winter).

As a nonprofit Christian organization the Westminster Rescue Mission is a community that provides clinical, physical, and spiritual resources to heal the addicted and feed the hungry. We take a holistic approach with Christ as the foundation of our work. Our two ministry programs include the Addiction Healing Center and the Mission Food Program.

Take a walk through our website to see the transformative work that's taking place in our community through these ministries, meet our team, and learn how you can partner with us to impact individuals and families - bringing hope that lasts a lifetime.

Donate
Learn about our impactful Service Ministry Programs

Tour the Westminster Rescue Mission in this awe-inspiring video. Hover over our palatial and serene campus grounds, walk through the halls where lives are being impacted each day, say hello to the faces of our incredible team members, see and hear the remarkable stories of transformation that would not be possible without your support.

Addiction Healing Center

Our Addiction Healing Center offers a CARF-accredited, programming for both Residential and Outpatient services. We offer a holistic approach to substance use disorder treatment from a client-centered perspective. Our current services include:

ASAM levels 3.1, 3.3 & 3.5 Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment
Outpatient Level 1 (both virtual and on-site) & 2.1 (IOP)
Transitional housing

Our multi-faceted, faith-based, treatment approach is tailored for each client regardless of ability to pay.

Learn More

Self referral
Mission Food Program

For those experiencing food insecurity, the Mission Food Program provides community outreach services and a food pantry for individuals and families in need of hunger relief. As a distribution partner of the Maryland Food Bank, and the largest food redistribution organization in Carroll County, we provide hunger relief through our distribution network and community outreach services in partnership with dozens of area agencies and charities.

GRASSROOTS DAY RESOURCE CENTER AT LEOLA DORSEY
Clients served at the Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey in FY 2025
1,547
10390 Guilford Road, Suite A, in Jessup, MD.

The Grassroots Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey is located at 10390 Guilford Road, Suite A, in Jessup, MD.  The Center provides hot meals, showers, laundry, referrals to social services and fellowship to individuals who are homeless, housing insecure or otherwise in need. The Center also hosts a food and clothing pantry. 

The Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey is open and provides all services Monday and Wednesday, 2-6 p.m., and Saturday, 10 a.m.-2 p.m. The Center provides Case Management Only (drop in and appointments) on Monday and Wednesday 9 a.m.-12 p.m., Tuesday 9 a.m.-1 p.m. and Thursday 9 a.m.-2 p.m. Case management services help break down barriers that keep individuals from obtaining housing. This includes helping individuals access identification documents, social services and benefits, mental health/substance use services, and other resources, as well as achieve next steps towards stable housing.

To reach the Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey, contact 301-776-9900. For emergency shelter needs, please contact the crisis line at 410-531-6677. The Day Resource Center can be accessed via the 409 bus line.

During the last fiscal year, the Day Resource Center at Leola Dorsey served 1,553 unique individuals, helping more than 100 individuals on average per day it is open.

Trinity Care Pantry

The mission of the Trinity Care Pantry is to share the love of Christ with members of our community by distributing food to those who would otherwise go without.

The pantry is open Tuesday evenings from 6:00 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. Our summer hours are slightly different with opening every other week. We will provide an updated schedule as we approach June 2026. 

Items needed that you could donate:  Hats, socks, gloves, soap, toilet paper, margarine, spaghetti sauce, tuna (and other easy to open canned meats), canned meals (like Chef Boyardee), canned soup, canned vegetables, peanut butter, jelly, breakfast cereal, canned fruit, powdered milk packets (1 quart size) 

This institution is an equal opportunity provider.

Volunteers are needed for the following tasks: 

CARE PANTRY DIRECTOR: BEVERLY HARGRAVES 

1)     Distribute food on Tuesday evenings. (Interview clients, pack grocery bags, and hand out the food.)

2)     Pick up food from Prince Frederick and from Waldorf.

3)     Help unload and sort boxes of donated food.

4)     Pick up bread from Weis.

5)     Maintain an inventory of the food in stock.

6)     Prepare bags and tables for the food distribution.

7)     Perform record keeping and reporting tasks. 

If you would like to help, please contact us by clicking here. 

 

Trinity Lutheran Church 
46707 S Shangri-La Dr.
Lexington Park, MD 20653

(301) 863-9512

We are a member congregation of The Lutheran Church—Missouri Synod.

Fralin Free Clinic
En Español
The Fralin Free Clinic’s number one priority is to serve the uninsured. We serve individuals who are currently at risk of being homeless, experiencing homelessness, and those transitioning into permanent housing in the Roanoke Valley.

We provide direct primary and preventive care.

Hours: Monday – Friday, 8:00 am – 12:00 pm, and 1:00 – 4:30 pm

Additional Hours:

Walk in Clinic, 4th Wednesday of Every Month, 4:30 – 6:30pm
Bless my Sole, 3rd Thursday of Every Month, 5:00 pm

Phone: 540-777-7671

Email: clinic@rescuemission.net

Location: 321 Tazewell Avenue SE, Roanoke, VA, 24013

Follow us on Facebook
 The Clinic is now accepting new patients!

Call 540-777-7671 to schedule an appointment today. Our number one priority is to serve the uninsured. We serve individuals who are currently at risk of being homeless, experiencing homelessness, and those transitioning into permanent housing in the Roanoke Valley. We have a community health worker on staff.

The Fralin Free Clinic, a ministry of the Roanoke Rescue Mission, operates under a volunteer Medical Director and numerous other volunteer healthcare providers. The staff and volunteers provide the care and services an individual needs to improve their quality of life.

Our providers focus on a holistic approach for each person that walks through the door.

“It is really nice to be able to come to one place and have everything done here in the same building.” ~Jack

Clinic Services include:
Primary and Preventative Care
Behavioral Health Services
Dental Care
Dermatology
Medication Assistance
Women’s Health
Medicaid Enrollment

The Fralin Free Clinic is an active member of the Virginia Association of Free and Charitable Clinics and the National Association of Free and Charitable Clinics. Additionally, we are proud to partner with the Virginia Health Care Foundation, a private-public partnership dedicated to increasing access to care for uninsured Virginians and those in underserved areas.

Interpreting services available in all languages.

 What is Medical Street Outreach?

The Fralin Free Clinic’s Medical Street Outreach program represents a vital component to effectively serving the needs of the unsheltered and underserved populations within the Roanoke community. This service brings medical care directly to the streets, offering immediate health services to those who might otherwise go without due to barriers such as lack of transportation, mental health struggles, or unawareness of available resources. Staffed by dedicated healthcare professionals and volunteers, the outreach program provides screenings, basic treatment, referrals to specialized care, and health education, all free of charge.

Who do we serve?

Our Medical Street Outreach is available to anyone who is homeless or at risk of homelessness. This is a broad definition, but includes individuals who are sleeping outside, in cars, abandoned homes, and more. If there is no medical emergency, please contact the clinic at (540) 777-7671. We encourage anyone who is in need of immediate, emergency medical care to not wait for our Street Outreach Team, but to call 911 and/or visit your local Emergency Room.

What services are available?

Services include but are not limited to: Wound care treatment, mental and physical health exams, medication management, and case management.

How can you help?

Volunteer: Know someone who has healthcare experience? We can use you on outreach! Donate: When you give to the Mission, your gifts help programs like Medical Street Outreach thrive. Advocate: Medical street outreach is a critical component to bringing down the number of people sleeping outside in the Roanoke region. This program helps us to encourage people to sleep in a safe space like the Mission where they will receive a hot meal, clean clothes, the love and support of our staff and so much more.

Download the Medical Street Outreach Flyer
VOLUNTEER

Medical and dental volunteers are needed. If you are interested in volunteering at the Clinic, please fill out this online form: Fralin Free Clinic Volunteer Application

Urgent Volunteer Needs!

We are in urgent need of volunteers who are Phlebotomy Certified, Pharmacy Technician Certified, and Dentists

 BLESS MY SOLE

This unique ministry takes place every third Thursday of the month as a means of sharing Christ’s love with people who are homeless and hurting. Volunteers wash participants’ feet, talk, and pray with them. Volunteer medical providers examine foot wounds, trim nails, and administer treatment as needed.  It is a moving experience for volunteers and patients alike.

Bless My Sole is held the third Thursday of each month.

Volunteers are needed!
Greeters/Runners – assist with greeting guests and maintain the materials and supplies
Washers – Pray for guest, bathe feet, and apply lotion and socks
Trimmers – Health professionals

FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
SMILE FOOD PANTRY

All who come to the SMILE Food Pantry are eligible to receive food once a week. We have suspended the zip code requirements for food indefinitely.

--If you live within the SMILE zip codes (Broomes Island Road and south in Calvert County) you will be issued a SMILE card when registering with proof of residency. This card will entitle you to receive food and other SMILE benefits (as available).

--If you live outside of the SMILE zip codes, you will be issued a SMILE card for Food Pantry use only.  Another form of ID will be requested for Pantry check in.

The SMILE Food Pantry offers a wide variety of foods that can help you supplement your groceries each week.

You can pick up a grocery bag of canned and packaged foods once a week by bringing a SMILE card (for registered clients) or a form of ID, to the Food Pantry. Depending on availability, SMILE also offers meat, cereal, and bread. Fresh vegetables may be offered during the summer months when we receive donations from local community gardens or other sources.

Clients usually receive the items below, but quantities are limited.

A bag of groceries that has been packed according to family size. The food provided depends on the availability of specific items. Clients usually receive canned vegetables, tomato products, soups, beans, pasta, canned chicken, tuna fish, macaroni and cheese, and other items as available.
cold or hot cereal
loaf of bread
frozen meat (approx. 1 lb per person in family) or other frozen entree
peanut butter and jelly
dessert or sweet (if available, based on generous donations from Weis Markets)

 

FOOD PANTRY HOURS

Monday:  9 to 10 am

Wednesday: 10 am to 2 pm

Thursday:  10 am to 12 pm

Friday:  10 am to 12 noon

Saturday: 9 am to 12 noon

HOW TO BECOME A SMILE CLIENT

A SMILE ID card must be presented to receive any SMILE services, including the Food Pantry, Thrift Store, and Emergency Services.
To obtain a SMILE card, you must register at the SMILE Thrift Store during its normal hours of operation:
Wednesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays: 10 am to 2 pm
Saturdays: 9 am to 12 noon

You may register for yourself and/or for a family member. When you arrive at the Thrift Store, please tell the receptionist at the front desk that you want to register for services.

What You Need to Bring to Register for SMILE services

​The following information will be required at the time of registration:

- Description or explanation of need
- Proof of residency in SMILE's service area (zip codes: 20615, 20629, 20657, 20676, 20685, and 20688). Please be prepared to show one of the following that shows your address: a copy of a mortgage bill, lease agreement, SMECO or Comcast bill, etc.
- Proof of Identification (Driver's license or other State ID)

Once registered, you will receive a SMILE ID card which is valid for 6 months. Registrations must be updated and renewed at least every 6 months. This card must be presented whenever you want to receive SMILE services at the Food Pantry, Thrift Store, or to request Emergency Services.
​

The Lord’s Table 
Gaithersburg Community Soup Kitchen, Inc.

The Lord's Table is currently serving Hot Meals weekdays from 3:30pm to 4:30pm. Check here for inclement weather updates.
 

Hours of operation     I    Guests are served Hot Meals Oct through Apr: Monday – Friday, 3:30 – 4:30p.m.

We're Hiring!  See our Job Opportunities
Purpose

Montgomery County has a persistent level of homelessness and working poor, especially during the winter months. The Gaithersburg Community Soup Kitchen, Inc., (“The Lord’s Table” or TLT) has been feeding the neediest members of our community since 1984. 

Mission

Our primary mission is to help feed the homeless and working poor, and to educate them about other social service resources available to them. Secondarily we work to educate the local citizenry about the situation regarding our vulnerable neighbors.

Volunteer Opportunities

Meal service opportunities: food prep, dining room setup and clean-up, serving, dishwashing and kitchen clean-up. 

Overview of Hot Meals program

Five days per week a FREE three course meal is served to anyone in need.

Soup

Entrée

Dessert

Children must be accompanied by an adult.

(If you are feeling any cold symptoms wear a face mask to protect staff and other diners)

Recovery Programs for Addiction

The Way Forward Residential Recovery Program is for men and women seeking to break free of the bondage of drug and alcohol addiction.

The Way Forward Program is a 12-15 month residential commitment-based program. Our approach to recovery includes both evidence-based and biblical based classes, community integration, individualized recovery plans and practical applications. We teach individuals how to “Live Recovery”.

We seek to continue the loving, compassionate, forgiving and transforming ministry of Jesus Christ
by creating an environment in which people seeking a lifestyle change from self-destructive behaviors and dysfunction find a community, encouragement, time and tools to do so.

Intakes are scheduled Monday through Thursday after pre-screening and acceptance. If individuals
are in jail or an institution we request clinical/medical charts with medication lists to be sent for review prior to making determination.

Do you think you need our services? When you reach out here is what to expect:

Prescreen – This is where we get to know you on a personal basis and see if you are a good fit for our program and to see if this program is a good fit for you.
Intake – Intake is a deeper dive into your background and a secondary review on if you fit the program.
Acceptance – This is the beginning of your recovery journey at the Rescue Mission.
What we offer
A program that is totally free of charge.
Long term recovery. Most programs are between 30 – 90 days. Ours is 12-15 months
Educational, vocational and therapeutic classes throughout the entire program. Transitional job skills training opportunities for graduates.
Our program is not a detox facility and we do not allow participants to take substance abuse related medications while in the program.
Each program participant is paired with a Peer Support Specialist/Recovery Coach.

For more information on the Recovery Program, contact us at (540) 777-7675 or  derek.fondren@rescuemission.net.

Welcome

Our church is a place where all are invited in to discover who God is and the purpose He has for all of us in His story. We think church should be one of the most engaging and exciting places on the planet—a place where people of any and all backgrounds can experience a God who is real and relevant in their lives today. And that’s exactly what we want to help you do!

About Us
Connect With Us

If you’ve been following God your entire life, you’re welcome here. If you’re new to faith, you’re welcome here. If you’re not really sure you believe in God at all, you’re welcome here. No matter where you are on the road to faith, we want to walk alongside you as you go. Our hope is that as you join us on the journey, you’ll discover a God who is active, alive, and at work in your life.

Clothing Voucher Program

Clothing vouchers are offered to shelter guests and individuals/families in the community that are in need.

If you or someone you know qualifies for social services and help is needed with clothing, please visit the Rescue Mission of Roanoke (in Reception) to sign up.

Applications for clothing vouchers are accepted Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 1:30 to 4:00pm

To apply for a clothing voucher, you will need to bring:

Current Photo ID
Social Security Card for all applying (even children)
Verification that you are receiving either SSI/SSD, Food Stamps,
Medicaid (not Medicare), WIC or TANF

If you are employed, please bring a pay stub or recent bank statement to verify income and benefits.

Each time the voucher balance is renewed, the same process must be followed to ensure proper activation, use and eligibility. 

Everyone applying for a Clothes Voucher must be present for a photo to be taken (this includes children).

 Clothing Voucher Guidelines

Once issued, Voucher Cards can be redeemed at:

The Donation Center/Voucher Shop; 421 Fourth Street, SE, Roanoke, VA 24013
Voucher Shop Hours: Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 2:00 to 4:30pm

Voucher Cards for each family member may be used to shop for clothing for the family member the card was issued to (unless a parent wishes to use their card to purchase for their child).

Eligible items include:

Clothing of all types – shirts, pants, dresses, skirts, jackets, coats, swimsuits
Accessories – ties, scarves, gloves, belts, hats, cosmetic bag, handbag or wallet
Shoes (Limit 2 pairs per visit)
Undergarments – underwear, bras, slips, socks
Adult diapers
FREE Bag (1 per year) – tote, backpack or luggage
FREE Coat (1 per year)

Adults must be present for their voucher to be used. Only parents or legal guardians may purchase children’s clothing with children’s vouchers.

We reserve the right to refuse service or terminate a voucher at any time, for any reason, at our discretion. Vouchers are not redeemable for cash and hold no monetary value.

Outreach Ministy
Many of our events are centered around serving our community. We have several reoccurring ministries that happen on a regular weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. Our weekly ministries include Food Pantry, Wednesday Night Suppers, A.A. Meetings, and Al-Anon Meetings. Our monthly ministries include Martha's Meal and the Sharing Table. We also host an annual Pancake Breakfast during Aberdeen's Christmas Street Celebration. Please contact the church office for more information about any of these ministries. 
	
	
WEDNESDAY COMMUNITY DINNERS

GRACE FOOD PANTRY
Open every Wednesday (except holidays) from 5:00 - 6:00 p.m. Each family may receive one bag of food every 30 days. 
A photo ID is required. Enter the building from the Parke Street side to the Office Entrance.
MARTHA'S MEAL
Martha's Meal is a free meal served every Thursday from 11:30 AM to 1:00 PM at Grove Presbyterian Church in Aberdeen. Six churches in the Aberdeen area take turns providing the meals. Parish Nurse Rose-Marie Lyman is also available to monitor blood pressures.
ADDICTION RESOURCES
As a community outreach of our Addiction Education Team, Grace Church hosts an Alcohol Anonymous group and an Al-Anon Support Group for family members affected by addictions. These groups are open to all interested persons. The meetings are held on Thursdays at 7:30 p.m.

All meetings are open to the public.
Additionally, our congregation supports the following organizations/missions providing services to others:
Fuel Fund, Breath of Life, Habitat for Humanity, FCCAU, New Wet Shelter, FISH, Fire Department, Meals on Wheels, Salvation Army, University of Africa, and the Heifer Project
God Is Love. You Are Loved.
"For God shows no partiality." -Romans 2:11
110 W. Bel Air Ave.,
Aberdeen, MD 21001
