Angels Helping Angels is myCHARITYof CHOICE

TRANSFORM
A LIFE TODAY
DONATE TODAY



We Raise Support for Persons Experiencing Food Insecurities!
DONATE MONEY
TO PROVIDE HELP
Donate via PayPal or Cash App ($AngelsHelpingAngels), or mail a tax-deductible check payable to Angels Helping Angels to: Angels Helping Angels, PO Box 1623 Baltimore, Maryland 21203
ADOPT A SHELF OR UTILITY
MAKE A DIFFERENCE
Adopt-A-Shelf by selecting a food shelf at the Pantry and bringing in those items on outreach days. Adopt-A-Utility by selecting a utility and cover those expenses for a month or a year -- whatever time frame matches your ability to donate.
WE’RE HERE TO HELP
LENDING A HELPING HAND
AHA’s Pantry is here to help those with acute or chronic need who live in the greater Baltimore area. We serve and distribute food on the First and Third Saturday of each month from 12 - 2 PM or by appointment. Once registered, participants receive food twice a month.
GET
INVOLVED
25%
DONATE FOOD
Goal: 100,000 pounds worth of food
Organize or participate in a special food drive. Drop off non-perishable food at New David Baptist Church located at 1401 North Milton Ave, Baltimore, Maryland 21213 on the first or third Saturday of each month from 12-2 pm. AHA’s Food Pantry cannot distribute food received in compromised packages (dented cans, crushed boxes, damaged bottles or lids, etc.) or expired food.
DONATE TODAY
12%
DONATE MONEY
Goal: $100,000

Donate via PayPal or Cash App ($AngelsHelpingAngels), or mail a tax-deductible check payable to Angels Helping Angels to:

Angels Helping Angels, Inc.
P.O. Box 1623
Baltimore, Maryland 21203

HELP US HELP OTHERS! DONATE NOW! Any amount is welcome. A gift of $100 can provide enough poultry and meat for 15 families of 3- 5 persons $25 can provide enough food to feed a family of 2-4 for one week.

DONATE TODAY

Signup To Receive Messages, Notifications, & Alerts
Signup with your email address

 SUBSCRIBE
	
VOLUNTEER AT THE PANTRY
Use your talents to help serve others.
Duties include registering and checking in guest, helping guest select food, loading, unloading, and sorting food, stocking shelves and delivering food to registered immobile or homebound guests. Duties also include assistance with food drives and special events. To volunteer, contact us at 443-756-8162 or send us an e-mail. Volunteer waivers must be completed prior to the start of your first day of volunteer service. A special thanks to our many volunteers!


HOURS
1st and 3rd Saturday
12 PM - 2 PM or By Appointment Only 
 
FOOD CAN BE DROPPED OFF BETWEEEN THE HOURS OF 12PM – 2PM ON THE FIRST AND THIRD SATURDAYS OF EACH MONTH

** You can review the updated Volunteer Waiver of Liability and COVID-19 Agreement. All volunteers are asked to review and abide by the updated Agreement as part of the registration process.
SIGNUP TODAY
 }
HOST A FOOD DRIVE
You can make a financial difference in the lives of others.
Hosting a food drive is a hands-on way for your school, office, group or place of worship to join in the fight against hunger. Drives can be held year-round and are an important resource for AHA’s Food Pantry. Hosting a drive is easy, and we will help you through the process. Please send us an e-mail to receive tips for planning a successful drive and to register your event.
CONTACT US
CHANGE
THE LIVES OF PEOPLE
“After my husband passed away in early 2018, my family and I recently moved to Baltimore in the Fall. I have myself and 3 children. After my husband’s passing, we are loss without him. Our life is a constant struggle. We don't have a lot of money for food and necessities. The Angels Helping Angels food pantry is the best thing that has happened to us since moving here. This past Thanksgiving and Christmas was rough; however, the food pantry and group of volunteers made our holiday season bearable. We are so grateful and most appreciative. I love the Angels Helping Angels team, they are always there for us when we need it.”
Sarah F.
“The Angels Helping Angels Food Pantry has helped me in my many hard times of need. The organization has been a blessing to me needing food for my family during the holiday season and when money is low. The Pantry has always been understanding and at times even dropped things at my home. I am not sure where or how things would be for me had it not been for the help of the Angels Helping Angels Food Pantry.”
Amy R.
“I need to let you know what an incredible asset the Angels Helping Angels organization is. We would not be able to keep a roof over our heads or food on the table without them. The volunteer staff is amazing, friendly, understanding, nonjudgmental and very warm. My family and I are just so grateful for all their generosity.
John F.
"This Angels Helping Angels Food Pantry is God sent. Times have been really tough, and the Pantry has helped my family. I only come when I really need

SILVER SPRING CHRISTIAN REFORMED CHURCH
 
HOME WHO ARE WE? GET INVOLVED SUPPORTED MISSIONS CONTACT MEMBERS DONATIONS
Food Pantry
Food Pantry Ministry
Despensa de alimentos

The SSCRC Food Pantry aims to reduce food insecurity in our community in an environment that encourages relationships among food pantry participants, volunteers, the congregation, and the broader community. We aim to do this in a way that recognizes that God created us as individuals and as a church to live in community and to bring the justice, mercy, and shalom of God to our community with the help of the Holy Spirit. 

Church members, other local congregations and synagogues, food pantry participants, students, and others from the community volunteer together to run this ministry.  

Contact information: 

Jennifer Renkema, MSW
Food Pantry Director

Email: foodpantry@sscrc.org 
Phone: 301.284.8401

Get Help
En Español: Despensa de alimentos
En français: Garde-manger distribution communautaire

Hours: 2nd Tuesday of each month, 3:00 - 5:00 pm 

Type of distribution: Pre packed bags for pick up

Eligibility: No appointment or referral needed. Any household in need of food is eligible. 

What’s in the bag: A typical bag has canned beans, diced tomatoes, canned fruit, dry beans, rice, pasta, and cereal. Sometimes bags include tuna, pasta sauce, coconut milk, corn, and/or pancake mix. The exact mix of food varies from month to month depending on what food is available from our partner, the Capital Area Food Bank. We also provide a variety of fresh fruits and vegetables that can be used in many ways.

Additional resources: In need of food in between our distributions? Check the following resources for information about other food distributions in Montgomery County Please call ahead to verify distribution information:

 Montgomery County Food Council Resource Map provides locations and hours of other pantries operating in this county.

Use Find Help to search for services by zip code

Call the Capital Area Food Bank Hunger Lifeline: 202-644-9807

The Montgomery County Food Resources webpage provides a calendar of food distribution events as well as other information about food assistance.

Privacy policy

Support the Pantry

Volunteer: Contact the Food Pantry director at foodpantry@sscrc.org or call or text 301-284-8401 if you are interested in volunteering at the food pantry. Student volunteers may earn SSL hours.

Donate Food: Donations of shelf stable food can be donated at any time using the drop-off bin located by the main doors of the church. For perishable items, large donations, or anything that may be unusual, please contact the Food Pantry Director. 

Financial Donations: Go to our Donate page to make a monetary donation via credit card. In the Additional Information section mention "Food Pantry" and we will ensure that the funds are used exclusively for our Food Pantry expenses. 

Food Pantry Flyer and Reports

Food Pantry Flyer (bilingual): Community Food Pantry Flyer

2024 Year End Report: Rooted in Community

2023 Year End Report: Sustainability and Renewal

2022 Year End Report: Faithfulness and Stability

Thank you to our many volunteers and supporters who have made the work of the food pantry possible!

Welcome
 
Menu
 
Events
 
Giving
 
Footer
Hereford Food Bank
 
 
The Hereford Food Bank is an all-volunteer operation and 100% of donations of both food and funds go toward fulfilling our mission to help those in need within the Hereford Zone. Due to confidentiality, our volunteers must be over 18, unless they are just packing bags or organizing shelves.

Hard times can fall on us all, but your neighbors at the Hereford Food Bank strive to provide a small safety net when you are in need.

 
OUR SERVICES:

The Hereford Food Bank provides the following free services for residents of the Hereford Zone*:

A bag of groceries per family per week containing ingredients for at least 3 full days of meals including eggs and fresh vegetables (as available) [larger families get 2 or more bags].
Detergent and hygiene items on a monthly basis or as needed.
Heating OR Housing assistance once per year for clients – a copy of the bill is required.
Referrals to appropriate social services and government agencies.
Special Holiday Feast bags at Thanksgiving, Christmas and Easter

There are no income limits or restrictions. If you need our services for a month or for a year, we are here for you. Our goal is to keep families together during hard times, feed them with food and hope, and show them compassion while maintaining their dignity.

 
OUR SERVICE AREA*:

21105 Maryland Line 21152 Sparks

21111 Monkton 21053 Freeland

21120 Parkton 21155 Upperco

21131 Phoenix 21161 White Hall

21102 Millers 21023 Butler

* Proof of residency required
 
OUR HOURS:
 
The Food Bank is open to receive clients and donations:

Tuesdays 1:00pm – 3:00pm

Thursdays 5:00pm – 7:00pm

Saturdays: 9:00am – 11:30am
 
 
HOW YOU CAN HELP:

FOOD DONATIONS:

Drop off non-perishable, unexpired food, cleaning and hygiene items at the Food Bank during regular operating hours (see above).
 
ORDER ON AMAZON
 
MONETARY DONATIONS:

Checks made payable to The Hereford Food Bank should be sent to:

The Hereford Food Bank
P.O. Box 400
Monkton, MD 21111
 
Donations may also be made electronically at
https://www.herefordumc.com/giving/
(select ‘Give Online Now’ then select ‘Hereford Food Bank’ in the dropdown box)
 
 
FOOD DRIVES:
 
Looking for student service hours or a Scout project? Organize a Food Drive at your school, church or community! Contact the Director for more information.
 
WHAT WE NEED:

BOXED AND DRIED GOODS:

scalloped/au gratin/mashed potato mixes

rice and rice mixes

noodles and pasta

CANNED BEANS , VEGETABLES & FRUIT

BREAKFAST ITEMS:

cereal/oatmeal

pancake mix/syrup

STAPLES:

peanut butter/jelly

flour/sugar/salt/spices

juice/juice boxes

CANNED MEATS:

tuna/chicken

beef stew/chili

DESSERT ITEMS:

cake/cookie/muffin mixes

jello/pudding

HYGIENE ITEMS:

Soap/deodorant/shampoo/toothpaste/shaving items

toilet paper/diapers

detergent/cleaning supplies

**All donations should be standard sizes — no bulk items or jumbo sizes.

 
CONTACT US:

Jennifer Jahromi, Director

herefordfoodbank@aol.com

Celebrating the Stories
“I’ve sincerely enjoyed the time you’ve spent during the past couple of weeks. I know it’s not always easy or convenient to personally come to my home and work with me on various strategies to assist me in my time of need. Yet you gave so generously your time, knowledge, patience and expertise to assist and speak on my behalf at such a critical time in my life. Your dedication to devote so much time, and keep me informed of a plan of action, has truly helped ease a lot of my concerns as to how I was going to continue paying such high rent. For nearly two years, it had taken a toll on me – being so stressed and many sleepless nights. I am truly grateful for your support to overcome obstacles that I alone could not have done, to rebuild my dreams to find affordable housing and have stability in my life once again.”

– SERVE Shelter client

Welcome to Babcock Presbyterian Church

We are glad you found us!  We are a congregation of the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) with a long history in the Baltimore area.  We speak a strong word of welcome!  

Do you wish to increase your understanding of God and grow in your faith?   Are you looking for a congregation that you want to be a part of?   If so, then we welcome you to Babcock Presbyterian Church in Towson, Maryland.

We open our doors to all.  Come and join us at worship on Sunday as we praise God through the preached Word, prayer, and uplifting music.  Further, please join us for Bible Study and Prayer on Wednesday evenings (see virtual Zoom info above). We look forward to meeting you.

Babcock Online
Babcock offers opportunities to join in worship and/or bible study online

Our worship services and Tower Series events are streamed live on YouTube which you can access by clicking the "Watch Via YouTube" button. Our Bible Study and Prayer Meetings can be joined via Zoom (Wednesdays at 7pm) by clicking the "Join Via Zoom" button. You may be asked for a Meeting ID/passcode on Zoom. If so, please enter the following: Meeting id: 4108253314, passcode: 21286

A Note from Our Pastor

Welcome to St. Peter the Apostle!


On behalf of the Parish Staff we invite you to explore our historic community of faith.  For two centuries, the people of St. Peter the Apostle have worked and worshipped together for the greater glory of God.  Our beloved parish has a wonderful history and many people have been a part of that heritage given to us by those who have gone before us, and we continue to work to spread the Word of our Lord.  We feel a strong connection to the dear ones who have gone before us, leaving a legacy of love.  We look forward to continuing that legacy and welcome with open arms all who wish to worship here.


St. Peter’s is an active and growing community.  For parishioners and visitors alike we invite you to worship with us, learn about the faith with us and celebrate with us.  I hope that St. Peter’s can be a spiritual home for you to find the Lord Jesus and know of his love for you.


Peace,


Fr. Chuck Wible
Pastor

Welcome to HCAA

As Harford County’s designated Community Action Agency, the HCAA’s mission is to help all Harford County individuals and families who are experiencing financial hardships in meeting their housing, food, energy/heating, and/or budget and family counseling needs. We support and encourage them on their path to self-sufficiency.

We accomplish this purpose through a diverse offering of programs and services. Current programs include:

Maryland Energy Assistance Program
Homeless Prevention
Homeless Services
Community Food Pantry
Community Case Management

We also partner with many organizations to provide additional services such as workforce preparation and development, pre-GED classes, and financial management classes.

For more information about the types of assistance we offer, please check out our Programs.

Direct Client Services are located at 1321-B Woodbridge Station Way, Edgewood, MD 21040

Get Involved with HCAA

Individuals who are deaf, hard of hearing or have speech disabilities may contact USDA through the Federal Relay Service at (800) 877-8339.

The letter must contain the complainant’s name, address, telephone number, and a written description of the alleged discriminatory action in sufficient detail to inform the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights (ASCR) about the nature and date of an alleged civil rights violation. GRACE NETWORK OF MARTINSVILLE & HENRY COUNTY
Altrusa International always there to help.
	

Grace Network is a faith-based, first-stop center for resources for families in crisis in Martinsville and Henry County. Supported by more than one hundred congregations, hundreds of individuals, civic groups, corporations, and foundations, Grace Network is where people at risk for homelessness or facing a crisis involving essential life needs can find help and a listening ear. Grace Network is here to help our neighbors in need keep a roof over their heads, their homes warm and lighted, and food on the table. Grace Network strives to give short-term assistance and to help find long-term solutions.

Dear Members, Visitors, Family & Friends,

​

On behalf of the members, family, and friends of First African Methodist Episcopal Church of Gaithersburg, we would like to take this time to greet you and extend a warm invitation to you and your family to visit and join this Christian fellowship. Our mission is to be a place where all people regardless of race, color, creed, gender, or sexual orientation connect with God, engage in ministries of service that strengthen our bonds with all members of the human family, and collectively discern how God desires for us to live in relationship with both God and those whom we are called to be in relationship. 

 

If you are new to the Gaithersburg area or are in the process of looking for a church home, prayerfully consider making FAME your spiritual residence because at FAME we “Worship, Work, Praise Our God” that manifests itself in vibrant weekly worship, dedication to our community, and love for each other. I’ll see you next Sunday at FAME.

 

Be Blessed,

Reverend Dr. Vanetta L. Brice, Pastor

Brother Kenneth Brice, First Gentleman

Opening doors into tomorrow!

​Helping families become self-sufficient through education, career services, and resources.

DONATE NOW
UPCOMING EVENTS

 

 

 

 

 

CHOSEN CDC exists to empower youth, strengthen families, and build thriving communities through leadership development, entrepreneurship, mentorship, and faith centered growth. Our upcoming events reflect the heart of our mission by creating spaces where purpose is discovered, potential is activated, and vision becomes action.

​

Stay connected and join us as we continue building leaders, cultivating entrepreneurs, and rising together as a community with purpose.

LEARN MORE

 

 

 

 

 

Join us for the 2026 Women’s History & Recognition Program, a special luncheon celebrating the impact, leadership, and achievements of women who are educating, inspiring, and shaping future generations in our community.

​

Guests will enjoy lunch while hearing from Keynote Speaker Dr. Linda Singh, CEO of Kaleidoscope Affect and former Adjutant General of the Maryland National Guard. The program will highlight remarkable women whose dedication, leadership, and service continue to move our community forward.

​

Come celebrate, connect, and be inspired as we move forward together.

Food Assistance
Food Assistance

Catholic Charities runs several types of food programs based at five of its sites: Kent County Office in Dover, Casa San Francisco in Milton, Seton Center in Princess Anne and Sussex Office in Georgetown.

HOW WE HELP

Food Cooperatives

The Food Cooperative program helps families and individuals stretch their limited incomes by providing a supplemental bag of groceries monthly. With the help of the Food Bank of Delaware and the Maryland Food Bank, club members receive nutritious, meal-ready products for a fraction of retail value.

Currently, our food distribution sites are located in Wilmington, Milton, Georgetown, Lewes, Slaughter Neck, Long Neck, Bridgeville, Roxanna, Dover and Princess Anne.

Emergency Food Pantries

Emergency food pantries are located at our Main Office, Kent Office, Sussex Office, Casa San Francisco, and Seton Center. Staffed by volunteers and stocked with donated food and USDA commodities, the pantry provides 3-4 days of food for persons who are in crisis and cannot buy food.

HOW TO GET HELP

Call the closest office to you for more information.

Main Office
2601 West 4th Street
Wilmington, DE 19805
302-655-9624

Kent County Office

Dover DE 19901

Georgetown, DE 19947

Click here to send an email for information about our Food Assistance programs.

Click here to download an application for emergency food.

Click here for a list of documents and application to complete for your first food assistance appointment.

Click here to download Acrobat Reader for mobile app.

HOW YOU CAN HELP

The food cooperatives and food pantries welcome volunteers of all ages. Volunteers bag groceries for distribution, and stock the shelves with donated goods. Call the location you want to help to see what current opportunities are available. Volunteers must apply and be interviewed prior to begin work.

Make a donation of food. Each location has the ability to store perishable and non-perishable food items. When your supermarket is having a “Buy one, get one free” sale, donate that free item to Catholic Charities. Please call ahead to make arrangements if you plan to donate perishable items such as produce or frozen meat.
Organize a food drive. Ask your family, neighbors, church, or civic group to donate nonperishable and shelf-stable food items.

Donation Suggestions:

Canned goods like spaghetti, tuna, salmon, beef stew, corned beef hash, beans, soups, vegetables and fruit
Boxes of pasta, macaroni and cheese, rice, mashed potatoes, crackers, cereal, oatmeal, and pancake mix
Jars of peanut butter and jelly
Juice, coffee, tea, hot cocoa mix, instant breakfasts
Shelf-stable milk – either dry or evaporated
Condiments: spreads, ketchup, mustard, relish, barbecue sauce, pickles and pancake syrup
Baby food, baby cereal
Grocery store gift cards

Click here to donate to our food distribution efforts online. If you prefer, mail a check to: Development Office, Catholic Diocese of Wilmington, P.O. Box 2030, Wilmington, DE 19899. Designate Food Distribution on the memo line.

Remedies and complaint filing deadlines vary by program or incident.

Persons with disabilities who require alternative means of communication for program information (e.g., Braille, large print, audiotape, American Sign Language, etc.) should contact the state or local agency that administers the program or contact USDA through the Telecommunications Relay Service at 711 (voice and TTY). Additionally, program information may be made available in languages other than English.

To file a program discrimination complaint, complete the USDA Program Discrimination Complaint Form, AD-3027, found online at How to File a Program Discrimination Complaint and at any USDA office or write a letter addressed to USDA and provide in the letter all of the information requested in the form. To request a copy of the complaint form, call (866) 632-9992. Submit your completed form or letter to USDA by:

Mail: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Office of the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights, 1400 Independe

See how 
Hunger exists in every zip code in our region.
LEARN THE FACTS ABOUT HUNGER IN OUR COMMUNITY 

Hunger stretches across the map of our area. That’s why our network of nonprofit partners does, too. Take a look to learn more about the scope of need in our region.

The Capital Area Food Bank leads our region’s efforts to provide equitable access to food and opportunity to people struggling with hunger and food insecurity. Each year, we source and distribute the food for more than 60 million meals.
How we’re helping people in need

Together with more than 400 local partners, we’re providing the food people need to thrive today. We’re also working with partners to pair food with services that can create permanent pathways out of hunger tomorrow.

Learn about our work
Providing Good Food To Help Today.
Distributing Food Where It’s Needed Most

With the help of farmers, wholesalers, restaurants, community members, and others, we source the food for more than 60 million meals each year. And thanks to our hundreds of partners in the community, we’re able to get that food to the individuals and families who need it most.

NONPROFIT DISTRIBUTION PARTNERS 
DIRECT DISTRIBUTION PROGRAMS 

400+ Food
Assistance Partners

Creating Hunger Solutions for Tomorrow.
While food is essential, we know it takes more than food to solve hunger. Employment, health, housing, and other issues can all contribute to the conditions that cause food insecurity. If we’re going to solve hunger, we have to address its root causes. Which is why we’ve developed programs and partnerships designed to help people meet their food needs today while also getting access to services that can help them build paths out of food insecurity tomorrow.
Nutrition Education

Good nutrition helps people thrive. So nutrition is at the core of our mission.

Reverend Dr. Stephen J. Russell, Jr., was born and raised in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, where he received his early education. He received a Bachelor of Arts degree in 1994 from Edinboro University of Pennsylvania, with a major in Criminal Justice and minor in African-American History. Upon entering the workforce Dr. Russell’s passion for people led him to pursue a career as a crisis intervention specialist for at risk families.

More Than a Church

Calvary Baptist Church welcomes all individuals seeking God’s love, and keep our doors open to every soul seeking to welcome Jesus into their hearts. We invite you to open your heart and allow His grace into your soul.

Our church stands as a beacon of hope in Baltimore City. We are a place of acceptance, peace and joy to all who are moved to join us. Our church family is richly diverse, with people of different ages and backgrounds coming together to worship and serve together. Get in touch to find out more or join us for a service.

​

Our Mission is to Redeem the World, Build Healthy Relationships and Provide Relevant Ministry!

Calvary Baptist Church of Baltimore is approaching its 105th Anniversary in 2026. We are excited to announce the 1st Annual Calvary Charity Golf Tournament.

On Monday, August 3, 2026, at The Woodlands Golf Course. This signature event will bring together community partners, businesses, and friends for a day of fellowship, fun, and purpose all in support of Calvary’s ongoing outreach initiatives across Baltimore City.

Over 44,000 pounds of food is distributed each month to feed more than 2,100 household members. Over the past year we served 8,279 households from our (2) Food Pantries representing 25,425 individuals.

Donate

An average of $4,700 is distributed from the benevolence fund monthly. In 2025, 264 people were assisted through the Resource Center with case management services and 175 were provided financial assistance for emergency bills through our Benevolence Fund. 

Ways to Support

It takes over 5,500 volunteer hours to operate both thrift stores and our two food pantries!  We wouldn’t be able to operate without the selfless dedication and commitment of our volunteers.

Your support will provide shelter, clothing, food, and critical assistance to those who might not have a safe or warm place to sleep, food to get through the month, or are facing eviction or job loss. About Us

The St. Camillus Food Pantry is a direct service ministry of St. Camillus Catholic Church whose goal is to promote food security for members of our local community.

 

Food security is access to safe, adequate, sufficient, easy-to-use, and culturally appropriate food throughout the year. In the fiscal year ending June 30, 2025, we made 22,280 food distributions to our neighbors. 

 

We're supported completely by an all-volunteer staff. We continue to see increasing numbers of families reaching out to us for assistance.

 

All monetary donations are used exclusively to purchase food and distribution supplies to feed the hungry in our community. Please help us meet the needs of others! DONATE HERE.
 

Our Location

​

Demand for food is great and the pantry is as busy as ever! If you need food assistance, please come see us.

‍CAPITOL HEIGHTS, MD (December 10, 2025) United Communities Against Poverty, Inc. (UCAP), Prince George’s County’s leading anti-poverty agency and Maryland’s designated community action organization, announced that it has reached $70,000, 70% of its $100,000 goal, in support of its Emergency Rental and Mortgage Assistance Fund.

UCAP Hosts “Walk in Their Shoes” 5K Walk to Raise Awareness and Financial Assistance to Prevent Homelessness

Capitol Heights, MD (November 3, 2025) — United Communities Against Poverty (UCAP), the State of Maryland’s designated Community Action Agency for Prince George’s County, is bringing the community together again for its annual Walk in Their Shoes 5K event. This purposeful event—dedicated to raising awareness, driving empathy, and strengthening support for families experiencing homelessness—will begin at 8:00 AM on Saturday, November 22, 2025, at Bladensburg Waterfront Park.

Montgomery County Corps
Montgomery County Corps 

The Salvation Army Montgomery County Corps opened its doors nearly three decades ago offering help to those most in need. Last year, hundreds of people facing hunger and eviction received the Emergency Assistance needed to stay in their homes, keep the lights on, and put food on the table. In addition to feeding empty stomachs, the Corps feeds hungry hearts. Music education, a women's outreach group, and youth programs help to develop the potential of each individual that comes through these doors.

 Community Programs
Social Services Monday through Friday from 9am to 4:30pm (Closed for lunch: 12:00-1:00pm) Walk-ins are not accepted. Please call (301)515-5354, Ext. 0 for an appointment.
Food Distribution on Fridays from 1:00pm until the total supply is distributed.
Youth Music Lessons and Children’s Youth Program on Wednesdays at 4:30pm

Sunday School at 11:30 am
Prayer and Worship on Thursdays at 6:00 pm

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

Community Food Pantry
Our Food Pantry is open to anyone in the greater Roanoke Valley area Monday mornings from 9:30 AM until 12:00 PM, excluding holidays.  We provide non-perishable staple goods.
Please call us at 540-343-7685 the morning that you are available to pick up food; you may call as early as 9:00 AM.
We will gather some basic information over the phone and schedule a time for you to pick up food between 9:30 AM and 12:00 PM the same day.
You must be able to pick up food; we do not deliver.
We do not ask for any documentation of need.
One person from each household (address) may pick up from the Food Pantry once every two months.

We will consider our church, community, and world in all of our decisions. Our actions will be infused with meaning and consideration for those we serve.

Clara's House Food Pantry

Clara’s House Food Pantry is dedicated to Felicia Champion’s mother, Clara Lee Venson. Together, they had a vision to help the less fortunate in the community. In November 2001, the Champions saw a need to feed the poor in Fauquier and surrounding areas. They renovated an old house on the CTI property and called it Clara’s Faith House Food Pantry.

Since its inception Clara’s Faith House Food Pantry has fed thousands of people. The food pantry provides food for the hungry in Fauquier, Culpeper, Rappahannock, Orange, Madison and surrounding counties. The food is purchased and donated from the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank and other donation sources. Clara’s Faith House Food Pantry solicits and stores food products donated by supermarkets, wholesalers, USDA food, food processors and local farmers. Many people and businesses in the community enjoy making donations to the food pantry as well. Donated food is redistributed to the less fortunate and nearly half of the food recipients are children. When a child does not eat properly, they lose concentration, are less productive and suffer from malnutrition. Last year CTI fed over 2,473 people through this outreach.

This institution is an equal opportunity provider.

10499 Jericho Rd.

Preventing Homelessness

Community Touch Inc. is a Service Provider that offers Emergency Housing Assistance with government grant funding. Prevention funds are provided to families and individuals who meet the requirements of the program and are facing homelessness in 14 days or less. The program is designed to divert clients from homelessness and if necessary, provide financial assistance for back rent to keep them stably housed. Case management is provided on a monthly basis to help clients retain housing stability. This could include budget and housing counseling, developing a housing service plan or referrals to needed resources.

To qualify for these services each individual must start with the Foothills Housing Network - Rappahannock-Rapidan Regional Commission intake process by calling (540) 724-6630.

Contact the Foothills Housing Network - Rappahannock-Rapidan Regional Commission to begin the intake process by calling (540) 724-6630.

Victory Transitional Housing

The Victory Transitional Housing is a one-year program that offers transitional housing to homeless men, women and children who have a broad range of needs. The program helps individuals achieve and sustain a higher quality of life through resources and various training opportunities. This program provides an opportunity for homeless individuals to save money for a future residence, gain information and resources, and acquire access to services that they need. Individuals meet with assigned financial mentors every week and participate in structured programs where they learn new skills and are connected with resources to meet their needs.
The Victory Transitional Program offers specialized services and referrals to individuals based on their needs (see examples below). Every resident develops goals with his/her assigned mentor and establishes a service plan for success. In the case of job loss, each individual is assisted in identifying and obtaining stable income. Last year, this program housed 359 homeless people.

Every resident is provided a case manager and financial mentor to assist with the following:

Housing Service Plan
Goal Setting
Life Skills Sessions
Money Management
Debt Reduction
Savings Plan
Job Search Services
Community Resource Referrals

CTI staff empower those they serve to live a life of self-sufficiency during and after they leave the program. This is accomplished through promoting productive activities, as well as by providing encouragement, education and support. The Victory Transitional Housing program collaborates with other agencies in the community in order to assist individuals in obtaining the most effective, beneficial services available.

Contact Us

10499 Jericho Rd.

Bealeton, VA 22712

Victory Transitional Housing Office Hours:

(Closed on weekends)

Rapid Re-Housing

Community Touch Inc. helps provide homeless families and individuals the financial assistance to move into permanent housing with rapid re-housing funds. This assistance can be for a security deposit and first month’s rent to help them secure housing. Case management is provided on a monthly basis to help clients stay in stabl

Dear First Lutheran Family and Friends,                       

Alleluia! Christ is risen. He is risen indeed. Alleluia!

This is the "Easter Acclimation" and these words proclaim both our faith in AND our hope in the resurrection of our Lord, Jesus Christ! 

Easter is more than just one day in the year when we gather to celebrate our Lord's resurrection. It is actually a seven-week season running from Easter Sunday to Pentecost (May 24th) and includes the Feast of Ascension (May 14th). The season recalls that when Jesus rose from the dead, He remained with His disciples for 40 days until His ascension to heaven the week before the Festival of Pentecost – 50 days after Easter.  During this time, Jesus – still fully God and fully man – appeared to; lived, walked, ate, and slept with; and taught His disciples as He did before His death. He truly was raised to new life ... He wasn't a ghost or spirit (John 20:19-31).

During the Easter season we celebrate Jesus' decisive victory over sin and death. We celebrate that fact that His victory over sin and death is also our victory. As we live now as children of God in a fallen world, He has called us to continue His mission to seek and save the lost through the good news of the gospel. Then, one day, He will return to raise all who believe to new and eternal life in the new creation. 

Therefore, throughout this season and until that day of His return, we proclaim in our worship and our lives, "Alleluia! Christ is risen. He is risen indeed. Alleluia!"

In our Risen Christ’s Love,
Pastor Jim

"Five Hundred Fifty Words-Or Less" Pastor's Blog

Thrift Stores

When you shop at our thrift stores or donate you can make a difference in the lives of countless people in your community

WHY SHOP AT A SALVATION ARMY Thrift STORE?

From home goods to electronics to children’s clothing and vacuum cleaners, our thrift stores offer deeply discounted, like-new items in a clean and organized shopping environment. The best part? You can score great deals and support The Salvation Army Potomac Division at the same time! All sales directly fund local, life-giving programs and services.

(540) 341-8385
India.Morris@uss.salvationarmy.org
Hours of Operation
Hours of Operation: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Monday - Friday
Hours of Operation: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday
Get Directions


SHOP. DONATE. MAKE A DIFFERENCE.

 

 

WHY DONATE TO A SALVATION ARMY Thrift STORE? 

Every item donated has the potential to make a life-changing difference! When you bring a bag with clothes, appliances and gently used items to your local thrift store, we process and sort these items, then add them to our shelves and clothing racks for purchase. Every dollar generated goes directly to funding our programs and services that support your local community. 

 

By shopping at or donating to a Salvation Army Thrift Store, you bring hope to those in need in our community.

Neighbors Helping Neighbors
Introduction

We know some of our neighbors need extra help as they age in place.

We also know that Bowie has many able-bodied retirees, civic organization members, and young people who are looking for ways to give back to their community.

The Community Services Department facilitates a program to match these two groups. We call the program “Neighbors Helping Neighbors.” This program is designed to bridge the gap between elderly (65+) and disabled residents and adults and organizations looking for ways to reach out and help someone in need. This is strictly a volunteer program. More information is included in the application forms.

Program
Snow Shoveling

Volunteers will assist with shoveling snow from driveways and sidewalks.

Light Yard Work

Volunteers may assist with light yard work - raking leaves, trimming shrubbery, gardening, mowing the lawn, or shoveling snow.  

Grocery and Pharmacy Pick Up

Our other focus is on essential needs  - grocery shopping and pharmacy pick up requests.

PROJECT PLASE IS RELOCATING!

During the weeks from Dec 9-20, we will close our Administrative Offices and Temporary Residence at 3601 Old Frederick Road to our temporary location: 2636 Wilkens Avenue, Baltimore. Please be sure to call your case manager before arriving for your appointment to confirm the meeting location. Community programs such as the food pantry|clothing closet|diapers will resume in 2025 at our new location. 410-837-1400 | info@projectplase.org

OUR MISSION

Project PLASE (People Lacking Ample Shelter and Employment) addresses homelessness in Baltimore by providing temporary housing, permanent housing and supportive services to homeless adults and families. We serve the most vulnerable and underserved, including persons with mental illness, HIV/AIDS, addiction, developmental disabilities, and ex-offenders, etc. We treat, restore and rehabilitate the whole person. We empower each individual to function at the highest level possible.

OUR PHILOSOPHY

Our philosophy is to stand with and serve marginalized and underprivileged individuals and families who lack access to resources in the larger community. We help persons in need through advocating for and accessing resources to meet our client's needs.

OUR VISION

To eliminate homelessness.

THE WAYS WE HELP OUR COMMUNITY
Permanent Housing Programs

Our Permanent Housing Programs enable veterans, families, and individuals to become as financially, medically, and socially independent as possible.

Learn More
Food Pantry Services 

As good neighbors, Project PLASE offers nutritional food to our community, as well as our clients. Access to nutritional food is one of the road blocks to a balanced home.

Temporary Housing Program

Our Temporary Housing Program combines case management, safe and accommodating housing, medical assistance and counseling to help clients move into permanent housing.

Learn More
Free Clothing 

The Community Clothing Closet is created by your donations, that enable our clients and community members the opportunity to have fresh, decent clothing for any life occasion.

Every dollar helps fund our programs, feed our clients, and help those struggling to retain their home. 

Help us give the gift of life after homelessness by donating today!

	
Donate
Look at our Wish List + donate your house  items?

We've compiled a few wish lists of items our clients need when either moving into the shelter or, when moving into their new home. Please consider helping in any way.

LIVING LEGENDS FOUNDATION
HELP US DEFEAT FOOD INSECURITY IN OUR COMMUNITY
We've adopted ending food insecurity in Maryland as a permanent charitable endeavor.  We currently feed an average of 1000 families monthly, and more than 700 families during the Thanksgiving holiday.
LEARN MORE
ABOUT US
OUR MISSION
The Living Legends Awards for Service to Humanity, Inc., serve at the intersection of the Arts and Social Justice. We harness the universal language of music to encourage all to become active participants in the human symphony. Under the direction of minority conductors, focused on music of minority composers, the Living Legends Awards Singers, our resident artist, grace the concert stage sharing with audiences powerful messages in song. We meet the needs of our community by serving others and inspiring everyone to serve in their own way. Through the use of creative expression we shine a light on those around us who seek to build the best in humanity. We strive to inspire all to give with joy, unite in our humanity, and express themselves creatively.

Our Mission
To diminish poverty and enable self-sufficiency for all Howard County individuals, families, and children in need.
Get Help
We are here to support you! By layering our five critical services of early childhood education, energy assistance, food assistance, housing assistance, and weatherization assistance, we guide a family from crisis to stability and ultimately to self-sufficiency. Get to Know Us
HELPING PEOPLE. CHANGING LIVES.

The Community Action Council of Howard County (CAC), is Howard County’s designated anti-poverty organization for over 60 years. We are a non-profit 501(C)(3) Community service organization designed to meet community needs. CAC is one of our nation’s 1,000+ Community Action Agencies (CAAs) dedicated to fighting poverty by helping people become economically self-sufficient. We are a dynamic organization that utilizes the Two-Generation (2Gen) approach to integrate our five core services of Early Childhood Education, Energy Assistance, Food Assistance, Housing Assistance, and Weatherization Assistance to create a holistic approach to empower families on their path to social and economic stability.

Services

hold spot

If you are new to our Food Pantry, we will ask you for an id to register you as a client. We ask for family, personal and contact information for our data base, so that you can receive the proper amount of food (small vs. large family) and are able to participate in our related ministries such as backpacks/school supplies and Christmas gifts for children in your household. We never give out your personal information, but for some grants we apply for, we have to provide summary information about the client population. We do not use income for eligibility. If you visit because your family needs food, we provide food. For your convenience the new client registration form is provided here for download in word format and pdf format. If possible, please fill it out and bring it with you when you come. We are located at 8823 Dayton Avenue, North Beach, MD 20714.

Our pantry is open every Tuesday 4:30 pm – 6:30 pm and Saturdays from 10-12 pm. If you are unable to come during regular opening hours, please call the pantry at 410-286-7086 to make arrangements to pick up food at an alternate time. We provide perishable and non-perishable food, meat, and a bonus bag with household items. We try to provide as much choice as possible beyond the basic perishable items that are pre-packed; we ask what kind of produce you want, what kind of meat you want, and whether you would like each extra food item.  Produce and bonus bags are only given to a client household once per month. You are welcome to come to the Pantry whenever you need food; we will provide non-perishable food and meat for your family each time you visit. We often have extra food items to provide as well, based on generous food donations.

If you are in need of temporary financial assistance such as for gas, prescriptions, utilities, or rent, please call the Pantry at 410-286-7086 and leave a message with your name, phone number, and the financial need. You will be contacted within a day to discuss your need, and a board member will ask you for information, and we will let you know how we can assist.

We want you to know that the Ladies of Charity Food Pantry will do its very best to always be open, to serve our clients, during our normal business hours, regardless of the weather. We DO NOT follow the Calvert County public School closings. If by chance we have to be closed, you will be notified on our Facebook page and a recording on our pantry phone 410-286-7086.

Mission Statement

Mission and Goals
“He who comes to me will never go hungry, and who believes in me will never be thirsty
John 6:35”

“I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.” Philippians 4:13

MISSION STATEMENT

The Bea Gaddy Family Centers is a community based nonprofit organization that assists and provides food and other items to our neighbors throughout the Greater Baltimore Metropolitan Area and throughout Maryland; we use your donations to help the lives of many.
Our focus is and always will be to maintain the legacy of the late Dr. Bea Gaddy, our founding volunteer. The volunteers at the Bea Gaddy Family Centers are dedicated women and men who sincerely have a passion for helping others. They reflect the spirit of love and desire to work together for good of our city and state.

GOALS

1. Promote and maintain the vision Bea Gaddy
2. Assist our most vulnerable citizens- children and senior citizens
3. Strive to stand-in-the gap for those in need
4. Strengthen relationships of those that need help with those that can give help
5. Maintain the dignity of those that come to our doors

“The people coming to our doors for help, may not have food, clothing or money but, they do have their dignity. How dare we be the ones that take that away from them.”
Bea Gaddy

Proclaiming Grace Outreach
Food  Pantry

1/4
Hours of Operation & Location

​

The Pantry is open the 1st and 3rd Fridays of each month from 1 p.m.-6 p.m (and by appointment). 

 

We serve  individuals and families in all of New Kent County and upper James City County who are experiencing food insecurity or need assistance, and are excited to now offer the “Shop Model.”   Our clients are able to come inside the pantry, grab a cart and “shop” for the items that best suit their needs. We offer not only canned goods, boxed food items, and meats, but we also offer paper products and cleaning supplies. As available, we offer fresh produce and pet foods.  

​

All first-time visitors are asked to complete a brief application so we can better understand needs and continue serving our community well. Everyone who comes will receive food on their first visit, regardless of where they live. For subsequent visits, clients are required to provide proof of residency to confirm they live within our service area. This may be in the form of a bill mailed to their home that includes their name. If living outside our service area, we work to refer the client to a food pantry operation in their area for the future.​

​

We are currently located at 20051 Tabernacle Rd., Barhamsville, VA 23011

behind Tabernacle CCC.

Purpose

There is growing need in our community for those struggling to provide for themselves and their families.

 

Open since July of 2009, we serve families living slightly above and below the poverty lines. We also provide food to families and individuals who have lost their jobs, are seasonally employed, or need assistance during these times of hardship.

How Can I Help?
​Food Products

You may always donate non-perishable canned goods, boxed items, paper products and pet items. We have three options for donating food:

Drop off at our pantry on Mondays and Wednesdays between 10 a.m. and 1 p.m.

Leave in the bin at Grace Village Thrift on Saturdays (15730 Pocahontas Trail, Lanexa)

Contact the PGO Office at (747) 741-5070

​

Financially

As good stewards of the gifts we receive, we are able to buy items in bulk through our affiliation with Feed More in Richmond, VA.  ​

 

​Send your tax deductible donations for the purchase of food or to contribute to our Building Fund to:

PGO

P.O. Box 224

Barhamsville, VA 23011.

​

Volunteer 

We are always in need of volunteers to assist with the pick up and distribution of food. Volunteers help greet, bag and carry the groceries to the cars of our clients.

 

To volunteer, call the PGO Office at (757)-741-5070.

We always need:

 

Foods to help with dietary needs (low or no sodium, gluten free, etc.)

Jelly (small jars)

Corn Bread Mix (Jiffy, etc.)

Rice

Flour

Jello

Sugar

Pasta and Sauce

Cereal

Condiments

Paper Towels

Toilet Tissue

Dishwashing  Detergent

Laundry Detergent
 

Contact Us for eligibility and more information.

Proclaiming Grace Outreach 2026

Helping Our Neighbors In Need

Lake Christian Ministries is a comprehensive center that provides services that address critical basic needs and help build pathways out of poverty. These services support a vision of “creating a poverty-free Smith Mountain Lake community where each individual is valued, is connected and lives in hope.”

Services

FOOD ASSISTANCE

CRISIS FINANCIAL AID

CLOTHES, FURNITURE & HOME GOODS

NEW TOMORROWS PROGRAM

CHILDREN’S PROGRAMS

Donations make a difference 

Our organization is supported by donations. From monetary donations to donations of food, clothing, furniture and in-kind services, there are many ways to make a difference.  Each hungry child who receives food, family that avoids homelessness, or individual who secures a job, is made possible because of your donation. See the ways you can impact the lives of our neighbors in need.

DONATE
Volunteers are the heart of our services

Volunteers are the heart and soul of our work at Lake Christian Ministries. 95% of all direct service is provided by our volunteers. If you have a passion for helping others, consider volunteering at Lake Christian Ministries. Check out the many volunteer opportunities.

LET'S REBIRTH OUR COMMUNITY
Diversity is the  Symptom, lack of Inclusion is the Illness, by Devika S.
Rebirth Resource & Support Center
​

Rebirth is a local 501 (c)(3) nonprofit organization Based in Salisbury, MD, created in 2002.

We were founded on the idea to empower, sustain & enrich our local communities in the U.S & abroad.

​

OUR Mission 

To advocate for the rights & facilitate resources to  the vulnerable & immigrant workers in the Delmarva Peninsula.

​

​

OUR VISION

To Improve life for the vulnerable and immigrant workers.

​

​

​

​

​

​

                       

​

With your support we become one step closer to serving the community as a whole. Join us, and support us in anyway that you can.
Our goals: Strengthening, Empowering and Protecting the legal rights of the immigrant workers, refugees, including the most vulnerable in our community, on the path toward better social adjustment.
Advocating for fair treatment, policies and working at both the individual and community levels. We will achieve that through education, communication, community outreach and development.
Let's help grow the least among us, from the ground up, with an helping hand.

EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES

 

Welcome

... to the Reisterstown United Methodist Church Food Pantry!!  We are a ministry of the Reisterstown United Methodist Church serving food to families in need.  Our all-volunteer staff is ready to help you.

The RUMC Food Pantry is a partner with Maryland Food Bank and we work closely with the Community Crisis Center, Westminster Rescue Mission, and the Alpha Pregnancy Center.  We operate solely by volunteers and via donations made to the Food Pantry.  We are always looking for new partners.

 We Serve

The RUMC Food Pantry serves the residents of Reisterstown, Owings Mills, Glyndon and Finksburg.  If you live in those communities then you are welcome to stop by once a month for a free bag of food.  Please note that ID is required to show residence in these areas.  We also have a "swap shop" that makes clothing and other donated items available if you have a need.

Hours We Are Open

We are open:

Saturday mornings from 8:30 am to 10:30 am 

Find Us

We are located in the basement of the Dale Chambers Community Center located at 308 Main Street in Reisterstown.  Please come around back and take the ramp down by the brick wall.

There's limited parking behind the Food Pantry so if the lot is full, please use the main lot behind the church.

Help Us

The Food Pantry is always in need of additional help as the need for food in our community continues to grow.  We welcome connections with local businesses through food drives, financial donations, and volunteers.

We are always looking for donations of the following items:

Cereal and oatmeal	Canned vegetables
Canned fruit and applesauce	Spaghetti and sauce
Macaroni and cheese	Soup and crackers
Peanut butter and jelly	Tuna and canned salmon
Rice and mashed potatoes	Mayonnaise, ketchup, vegetable oil
toothpaste, deodorant, shampoo	laundry detergent, liquid dish soap

The Food Pantry can always use plastic grocery bags as well. You can drop off your empty bags where you drop off your food donations.

If you would like to volunteer to help in the Food Pantry, please copy and paste this link into your web browser to sign up.

 http://www.timetosignup.com/rumcfoodpantry

Please contact the church office at 410-833-5440 if you would like to help with a food drive.

GIVE ONLINE

Financial donations are always welcome too.  You can donate securely online by clicking here.  Praise God and thank you for your support!

Serving Madison County, VA Since 1982.
OUR MISSION -
Welcome to the Madison Emergency Services Association (MESA) website. For over 40 years, MESA has been a beacon of hope and support for the residents of Madison County, Virginia. Founded in 1982, our privately funded and run organization has been dedicated to providing assistance to those in need within our community.​
CONTACT US -

Office: 540-948-4427

Email: info@mesamadisonva.org

927 Orange Road,

Madison, VA 22731

Our mission is to serve as a resource for our Madison County neighbors in need, and our vision is to build community by empowering neighbors. We uphold the values of Community, Compassion, Dependability, Dignity, Empathy, and Responsiveness.​

We offer assistance primarily in two areas: Financial and Food. Financial assistance is provided on an as-needed, case-by-case basis and can include help with electric bills, rent/mortgage payments, fuel, medical/dental expenses, vehicle repair, and other life needs. Food and personal hygiene items can be provided through our Food Pantry.

MESA's programs and services are provided by a small dedicated staff and passionate volunteers, funded exclusively through donations and grants. The generosity and participation of the community are what makes it possible for MESA to continue its mission.

​We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, (including gender expression) sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, family/parental status, income derived from public assistance programs, political beliefs, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity, in any program of activity funded by USDA Rural Development.

​We invite you to explore our website to learn more about our services, how to get involved, and how to support our mission. Together, we can make a difference in the lives of our neighbors in need.

Come Visit Us At The Pantry!

We happily operate as a resource for ALL of Madison County, from Etlan and Wolftown to Leon and Locust Grove. There’s no corner of Madison County that we don’t serve. Whether you’re in need of food or personal hygiene items from the pantry or would like to drop off donations in person, swing by and say ‘hey!’

Pantry Hours -
Tuesdays: 10:00 A.M. - 1:00 P.M.
Wednesdays: 10:00 A.M. - 1:00 P.M. 
Wednesday Evenings: 6:00 - 7:30 P.M.
Saturdays: 10:00 A.M. - 12:00 P.M.

Interested in learning more about our Client Services? View our Emergency Services Page or fill out our digital Client Services Form below to get started. You may also pick up a paper Client Services Form from the Food Pantry during our regular Hours of Operation.

About Us

 

Our Work:

Elkton Area United Services  is a comprehensive social service agency serving Eastern Rockingham County since 1972. Elkton Area United Services strives to develop programs and services where needs exist and tries to meet the needs of an ever changing community. Through its programs Elkton Area United Services provides assistance with housing, electricity, heat, food, GED classes, school supplies, books, transportation, aid to stranded travelers, senior citizens activities and joyful holiday season. The organization is supported primarily from community donations, thrift store proceeds and a United Way of Harrisonburg and Rockingham County Community Impact Grant.

Organization History:

Elkton Area United Services was established, in March of 1972, as an outgrowth of the Elkton Ministerial Association, under the leadership of Holy Infant Catholic Churches’ Father Charles Ryan.  Funded with a $1000 loan from the Elkton Ministerial Association and a $500 donation from the Catholic Diocese of Richmond, the agency initially provided leadership and funding for five projects:

Christmas Cheer Program
Senior Citizens Program
Literacy Program
Travelers’ Aid
The Clothing Store

In 1973, a Food Pantry was established to fill temporary food needs and in 1987 expanded to better meet the nutritional needs of the community by the distribution of USDA foods on a regular basis.  To complement the expansion of this program, a weekly Meals at Heart Program started to serve the nutritional and social needs of the homebound.

The 1980s saw the beginning of many changes necessary for the agency to keep pace with the growing needs of the Elkton community,

In 1985, Elkton Area United Services became a grant agency with the United Way of Harrisonburg and Rockingham County and received $500.  Today, Elkton Area United Services is a full partner agency and in 2016 received $15,000.

In 1986, the Elkton Area United Services board approved the first salaried position; a store manager. Today, Elkton Area United Services has five part-time salaried employees, an Executive Director, a Client Services Manager, Food Pantry Coordinator, a Thrift Store Manager, and a Volunteer Coordinator.

Place to Belong

You are not an outcast nor a misfit; you are God's beloved child adopted by grace.  As you claim this identity, you find yourself in good company. By God’s invitation we are a part of God’s family. We belong to God and we belong together.

​

Our prayer is that every person connected to Wesley Freedom Church will experience belonging in our church family and in small groups or serving communities where friendships and faith can be deepened.  If you are seeking a place of belonging, hope, and purpose we hope to welcome you soon.

Bedford Christian Ministries

Serving Our Neighbors in Need for 41 years

About Our Services at Bedford Christian Ministries
Food Pantry

Our food pantry is open Monday, Tuesday and Friday mornings from 9AM to 12 Noon.  USDA, purchased and donated food items are available.  Please bring picture ID and proof of income and/or benefits.

Clothing and Household Items

Our clothing and household departments are open on Monday and Tuesday mornings from 9AM to 12 Noon.  Gently worn clothing and new and donated household items are available.

Emergency Financial Aid

Emergency Financial assistance is available to help with electric and water bills, past due rent, electric, water and rent deposits, fuel oil and propane, and limited vision care.  By appointment only.  Please call to schedule.

Non-Discrimination Statement

In accordance with federal civil rights law and U.S. Dept. of Agriculture civil rights regulations and policies, this institution is prohibited from discriminating on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, age or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity.

Brief History

In early 1984, area ministers came together to plan for a centralized non-profit agency to serve community members in need of food, clothing and financial assistance.  The result was the founding of Bedford Christian Ministries in December 1984.  With a budget of $2,000, BCM opened for business in a small house (now demolished) on Washington St. near Main Street UMC.  In August 1995, we moved to our current location at 217 W. Washington St.

Our Inspiration

In the nearly 30 years since Johnson"s Furniture Store became the Bedford Christian Services Building, the Holy Spirit has inspired and guided the hundreds of volunteers who have served thousands of their neighbors through Bedford Christian Ministries, The Shepherd's Table and the Bedford Free Clinic.  It is their love of their neighbors that transformed a furniture store into one of the many rooms in God's house.

Sharing Stories Through the Lens: A Photo Gallery by Bedford Christian Ministries
Hello
Volunteers Welcome

Bedford Christian Ministries is an all-volunteer non-profit charity seeking to do God's work by serving members of the Bedford community who are in need of assistance.  We are always seeking new volunteers in all areas of service.

Are You A Veteran In Need?

Are you a veteran in need? Do you know a veteran in need? Please fill out this intake form so that AHI can provide them with the support they need!

Volunteer!

AHI welcomes all volunteers! There are so many valuable skills you have that could be shared with our veterans, both in house and remotely. Please fill out this form to find out how you can help!

Stock our pantry!

AHI will be opening its Food Pantry very soon! You can help us stock it and feed hungry veterans. Your donation is tax-deductible and will be put to good use. Donations can be dropped off Monday through Saturday from 9 am – 7 pm (at 820 Chesapeake Street, SE in Washington, DC). Thank you!

92-year-old D-Day Veteran Gives Students Living History Lesson

Volunteers and youth groups across the country visit The Veterans Service Center, which is operated by Access Housing, Inc., to perform acts of service. This time, they gathered the opportunity to speak to John Miller, a veteran that was a part of the D-Day invasion. The children learned more than they could have imagined by Miller, and so can you!

Hunger In Our Area

Persistent hunger is one of the surprises of our economic recovery.  In our area 1 in 10 people struggle with hunger, and 1 in six are children.  Finding it difficult to make ends meet our clients have to make tough choices.

64 % choose between paying for housing or food
72 % choose between paying for utilities or food
68 % choose between paying for medicine or food

Hope Distributed serves more than 3,800 individuals a month and distributes over 1,300,000 pounds of food a year.

OUR FOOD DROP SCHEDULE

Stay up to date with our upcoming food drop dates. Use this calendar to see when and where we’ll be distributing meals to families in need. Mark your calendar and help us spread hope!


REDEFINING HUNGER RELIEF WITH A MOBILE FOOD BANK

As a pioneering food bank in the Baltimore Metro Area, Leading By Feeding is committed to turning scarcity into abundance. Our mobile food bank travels throughout the area, reaching those who may not have access to healthy food options. Through our innovative approach, we provide fresh produce and essential items directly to those in need. The food bank's impact is amplified through collaboration with volunteers and partner organizations. Support our mission to bring nourishment to every corner of Baltimore!

EMPOWERING AREAS WITH A MOBILE FOOD PANTRY


Our partnerships with businesses fuel innovation and extend our impact exponentially. By aligning company values with our mission, businesses become active participants in the fight against hunger. Corporate collaborations translate into targeted food relief, strategic growth, and shared success in shaping thriving communities. Discover the advantages of partnering with us in making a sustainable difference today!


Give Hope 
FOOD BANK AT THE HEART OF COMMUNITY SUPPORT

Our Food Bank is a cornerstone of philanthropic efforts, extending lifelines to those in need. We source and distribute vital food supplies, bridging the gap between abundance and scarcity. With robust collaborations in place, the bank bolsters emergency shelters and mentoring programs, ensuring a comprehensive support system for vulnerable individuals. Every donation and volunteer effort fortifies our mission to build healthier, resilient communities. Your involvement can spark change, join us!

Donate Now 
EXPLORE OUR GALLERY OF GIVING AND GROWTH

Take a look through our gallery to explore the smiles, milestones, and memories that define our story. Each image and video reflects the power of community, resilience, and hope. See the difference we’re making through the eyes of those we've helped and the volunteers who make it all possible.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Want more information? We've provided answers to some common inquiries below.


WHY IS LEADING BY FEEDING SO POPULAR?

Leading By Feeding has become a beacon of hope in the Baltimore area due to its commitment to fighting hunger through its innovative food bank initiatives. Our food bank focuses on providing essential nutritional support to underserved communities, including low-income families, seniors, and individuals facing food insecurity. With our weekly Free Mobile Community Markets, we ensure that fresh, healthy food reaches those in need without the typical barriers found in food deserts. The food bank model we employ does more than distribute meals; it fosters community awareness and promotes self-sufficiency. Additionally, our food bank's efforts extend globally, aiding areas like India with much-needed food relief. By combining local outreach with global awareness, Leading By Feeding has positioned itself a

I know that some believe that they are beyond the hope of salvation.  Beyond having their lives changed.  Many people believe that their sins are too many to be forgiven and that all hope of having a relationship with God is vanished.  But this message is for you and anyone who may be caught in bondage under this untruth.

"God so loved the world, that He gave HIs only begotten Son, what whosoever believeth in Him should not perish, but have everlasting life" John 3:16.




The good news for you is in the word "whosoever"that means you, me and anyone who is willing to believe in Jesus Christ as the only begotten Son of God and accept His work on the cross as payment for your sin.  The verse says who shall have eternal life, God's gift is extended to everyone.  




Remember the key word is "whosoever" that means murderers, thieves, robbers, alcoholics, drug abusers, adulterers, fornicators, homosexuals, child abusers and liars too, in other words any and everybody can receive salvation.




Romans 10:9 "...that if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved."




Accept Christ as Lord TODAY and receive God's gift of ETERNAL SALVATION -- TODAY.

THE BIG QUESTION

THE BIG QUESTION - Message 




We all go through life searching for answers to questions that we cannot answer.  Some of these questions are centered around the end.  We want to know "how old will we we be when we leave this earth?"  We are concerned about when will the world end and is there really a heaven and hell?




In Mark 9:46 and 48, hell is described as a place where "their worms does not die and the fire is not quenched."  Jesus describes hell as a place of outer darkness and a furnace of fire where there will be wailing, weeping and gnashing of teeth (Matthew 8:12; 13:42; 50; 22:13; 24:51; 25:30; and Luke 13:28)




Hell is real - it does exist.  Your question may be, does or would a God so loving and kind really send people to a place so terrible?  My answer is not that God will send people, but because some chose not to accept God's free gift of salvation through Jesus Christ, then people choose hell.




Accept Christ as Lord TODAY and receive God's gift of ETERNAL SALVATION -- TODAY.

WELCOME TO DESTINY DRIVEN

Welcome to Destiny Driven, a beacon of hope and catalyst for positive change! As a nonprofit organization, we are passionately committed to empowering communities through education and resources, striving to pave the way for a brighter and more promising future for all. 

Join us on this impactful journey towards success

ABOUT DESTINY DRIVEN GLOBAL
ABOUT US

 At DESTINY DRIVEN Empowering our community through compassionate action is what Drives us. Our nonprofit is dedicated to addressing the most pressing social, economic, and environmental challenges. With a commitment to equity, inclusivity, and sustainability, we provide essential services, advocate for systemic change, and foster collaborative partnerships to create lasting impact. Guided by our values of integrity, empathy, and accountability, we strive to build a future where all individuals have access to opportunities for growth, justice, and well-being." 

At DESTINY DRIVEN, our mission is clear: to provide access to Basic and essentials of life; Food, Education and Health. These are vital essentials that uplift and empower individuals and families in our communities. We firmly believe that education is the key to unlocking one's full potential, and by extending a helping hand, we can collectively shape a world where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. 

Our commitment goes beyond words; it's a call to action. Our goal is to see a Community where every individual is have access to what makes life easy. We equip our Community with the essentials resources they need to succeed. 

Join us today and become a driving force for a future filled with hope, opportunity, and empowerment. Together, we are Destiny Driven.

WELCOME TO CENTRAL UNITED METHODIST CHURCH!

We have now returned to 11:00 Sunday morning worship services.   In addition, our services are broadcast on Facebook.  We hope you will join us either in person or at the link shown under the Worship tab above.  

Our vision statement:

Central UMC welcomes all people to a growing relationship with Jesus Christ to love and serve God’s people.

Mission statement:

We will be Christ followers by fully participating in God-centered worship
Actively learning the Word in reading and studying
Grow in spirit through generous love for the least, the lost and the last
Characterized by moving out in compassionate witness and service to others, in the power of the Holy Spirit to demonstrate and make visible God’s kingdom on earth as it is in heaven and to issue a gracious invitation for all to enter into new life in Christ.
Founded in 1797, we are the oldest continuing Methodist Congregation in Staunton and Augusta County.

Weekly Services:
Adult Bible Study 9:30 AM both In-Person and Via Zoom. Contact Pastor Un to join in.
Agape Fellowship Time 10:30 AM
Sunday Morning Worship Service 11:00 AM

We are Central United Methodist Church, located on the corners of Lewis & Beverley Streets in the beautiful Downtown Historic District of Staunton, VA.

ABOUT THE CULPEPER FOOD CLOSET

The Culpeper Food Closet provides food and personal care items to Culpeper families, individuals, and seniors in need of assistance. An outreach ministry of St. Stephen’s Episcopal Church, it began in 1984 and operates as a full community effort, through the dedicated work of over 80 volunteers from our area. Food and monetary donations are received from local businesses, organizations, churches, and generous individuals. These donations may provide over 3,500 meals weekly for families and individuals, in the town and county of Culpeper. Daytime clients first contact Sam’s Place to complete a food voucher. Sam’s Place (219 S. Davis St. / 540-727-1055) is a part of Culpeper Resource Center, where individuals and families can access information and referrals to community partners. Clients may receive custom packed groceries twice a month during the day.


Many clients do not need help on a regular basis. They may have an unforeseen hardship such as illness, job loss, even a car breakdown. Families are offered hope and encouragement, with three to four days of meals, which covers three meals a day, for each qualified household member. Personal care items are also available.

Si necesitas comida, les invitamos a visitar el Food Closet de Culpeper, abierto de lunes a viernes de 9am a 12pm.

Primero, visite Sam’s Place, localizado en 219 E. Davis St., para completar la forma verde y luego visite el Food Closet de Culpeper, localizado en 120 N. Commerce St., donde uno de nuestros amigable voluntarios le preparará provisiones y artículos de cuidado personal selecionada para usted y su familia.

El Food Closet de Culpeper también abre los jueves de 4pm a 6pm. Los jueves por la noche, puede venir directamente al Food Closet de Culpeper y completar una forma naranja de información.

NOTA: Familias pueden venir dos veces al mes durante el día (con la forma verde de Sam’s place) y un jueves (directamente a Food Closet) por la noche.

HOURS:

Monday – Friday | 9 AM – 12 PM
Thursdays | 4 PM – 6 PM
(If school is closed, the Food Closet will be closed for the first two days of weather-related closings.)

Thursday evening vouchers (orange) are created on-site. Clients may use this voucher once a month.


We want you to know that our volunteers are ready to help, but we cannot do it alone. You can make a difference.
Donate to the Food Closet Now.

We continue to care for our community during this ever changing environment. Please be assured that all staff and volunteers practice the most current CDC health guidelines, which includes hand washing and surface cleaning.

If you, or someone you know, is in need of food or personal items, please call us or Sam’s Place.

Contact the Food Closet

Mail: The Culpeper Food Closet
P.O. Box 343
Culpeper, VA 22701

Address: 120 N. Commerce Street
Culpeper, VA 22701
Phone: 540-825-1177

Email: culpeperfoodcloset1@gmail.com
FB: Food Closet on Facebook

OUR LEADERSHIP

Salvation Army Programs are led by Salvation Army Officers. These officers are responsible for leading by assessing needs, delivering programs, and directing ministries for our local community. From serving food to balancing the books, they function as the Executive Directors and the Pastors.

 

Lieutenants Douglas & Sharon Ingold 

 

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

Welcome to the Islamic Center of Maryland

The Islamic Center of Maryland (ICM) is a 501-C3 non-profit organization with an operational philosophy based on the Holy Qur’an and the teachings of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Muslim families who visit ICM work toward fulfilling their religious obligations and striving for spiritual, intellectual, social and economic well-being. The Center strives to build a working relationship with people of all faiths to promote understanding, tolerance, and activism that benefits all. For more information about services and programs, please visit the Center, subscribe to our weekly newsletter or submit a Contact Us request

HELPING HANDS FOOD PANTRY

"For I was hungry and you gave me something to eat, I was thirsty and you gave me something to drink, I was a stranger and you invited me in..." (Matthew 25:35)

Helping Hands Food Pantry needs more hands! We are looking for workers on Wednesday and Friday morning. If you are interested, please contact the pantry via email or call the pantry at (410) 592-2226.

The Helping Hands Food Pantry, located in the lower level of Salem House, began operations in 1992. It is staffed by volunteers from several local churches - including Fork United Methodist, Grace Community, Franklinville Presbyterian, St. John's Episcopal Church, St. Stephen Roman Catholic, St. Paul's Lutheran, and Salem United Methodist.

 

Currently, the pantry is open Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 10 a.m -12 p.m.. In addition to regular food distribution, additional bags of food are given for Thanksgiving and Christmas.

​

For fiscal year ending June 30, 2022, Helping Hands was able to provide assistance to 577 families (1503 adults and 627 children) thanks to generous support from the Maryland Food Bank (monthly) as well as businesses, schools, churches, and individuals.

Wilmot donation Dec 21

Wilmot Modular Structures, Inc. gives a generous donation to the Helping Hands Food Pantry in December 2021.

1/3

If you are in need of services from Helping Hands Food Pantry, please call (410) 592-2226 and leave a message.  Someone will return your call.

​

If you would like to make a donation to Helping Hands, checks are payable to Helping Hands Food Pantry and can be mailed care of Salem United Methodist Church.

OUR MISSION

We are called to be active in God's blessing of the world through our commitment to welcoming one another,  growing in grace, serving our neighbors and caring for  all God's people.

SALEM UNITED METHODIST CHURCH

Rev. Dr. Stacey E. Nickerson, Pastor
