(A Grateful Neighbor Served By Our Baby Essentials Program)
Our Values

Collaboration: We are committed to cross-sector collaboration, bringing together diverse community stakeholders to collectively identify and develop sustainable solutions to the complex challenges facing our city.

All People: We value every community member and are committed to serving each person with dignity, kindness, and respect- regardless of religion, race, ethnicity, national origin, gender, age, or sexual orientation.

Relationships: We believe healthy relationships are essential for happiness, growth, and resilience. We are committed to investing the time necessary to know our neighbors and connect them to the resources and relationships we all need to flourish.

Engagement: We believe everyone has something to offer. We are committed to engaging the strengths and resources of the neighbors we serve. We rely on community volunteers to get the job done.

The Hope Center Is Made Possible Because Of The Generous Support Of Community Neighbors And Our Excellent Partners.

“I do not want to send them away hungry...”     

Please leave non-perishable donations at one of our food collection sites at the Middleburg Library or in the donation box outside the Seven Loaves entrance.

Stats | 2024 Report

438

Total households served

1,539

Total people served

Total pounds of food distributed

Our Mission

To respectfully serve nutritional groceries to ANYONE in need. We provide food to those facing food insecurity in Middleburg and surrounding counties. We serve all people regardless of place of residence, income, faith, race, gender, sexual identity, disability, or other criteria. Clients may receive food once a week.

Learn More About Us

Volunteer

We laughingly call each other "LOAFERS", but loafing is the last thing Seven Loaves Volunteers do!

Instead,  we're driving to the Blue Ridge Area Food Bank in Winchester each week  and returning to the Pantry with 1,000 pounds of non-perishable food  products to unload and put in the storage hold. We're working the Pantry  on distribution days, welcoming and assisting Patrons, packing bags,  restocking shelves and helping to clean up afterwards. We're picking up  hundreds of pounds of donated bakery and refrigerated food products from  multiple grocery stores and markets three times a week, and we're delivering  fresh produce from two Farmers' Markets to the Pantry each weekend.

Our mission is to enhance the quality of life for residents in Central Maryland.  We accomplish our mission through community service programs and community outreach initiatives that promote improvement in the quality of lives of youth and their families.

Over the past 5 years, in conjunction with our community partners, we have provided book scholarships, middle and high school male and female mentoring, tobacco and sexually transmitted infections education, summer youth enrichment, school supplies and food to over 10,000 youth and 2,000 families in Central Maryland.

We Need Your Support. Make a Tax Deductible Donation Today

Click Donate button below to Donate

 
Operation Timothy
Operation Timothy is a Christian-based mentoring program that will equip young men from grades (5-12) to be productive in their personal life, community life, and work life with the assistance of positive role models.

Virtuous Steps
Virtuous Steps mentorship program is for teen girls ages 11-18 years of age and is open to the public. The program starts the 1st Sunday in October and ends the 1st Sunday in June. Our life skills lessons are on the 2nd and 4th Sunday of the month,

LCDC Community Youth Center
The benefits of the LCDC community youth center are job creation, a healthier community, stronger families, education and economic empowerment, safe haven, crime prevention, and a comprehensive approach to the needs of the community.

Food Pantry
The Food Pantry offers healthy and nutritious meal solutions to families in need. Our loving staff not only serves food but also offers prayer and encouragement.

Ministerial Staff
What is the A.M.E. Zion Church?
Read More
About the A.M.E. Zion Church 

The African Methodist Episcopal Zion Church, often called the "Freedom Church," is rooted in a deep history of resilience and faith. We believe in God’s grace and the power of the Holy Spirit, striving to transform lives and bring believers closer to unity with God through Jesus Christ. Founded by those who endured the hardships of enslavement, our church became a beacon of hope and justice, grounded in the teachings of Jesus to bring good news, freedom, and healing to all.

​

For a more detailed history and beliefs, please visit the Official AME Zion Church website and the AME Zion Mid-Atlantic Conference website.

392 Cypress St, PO Box 187

Millington, MD 21651




Contact: Faye Everett

443-480-005

info@millingtoncrumptonfoodpantry.org

The Millington-Crumpton Food Pantry is an outreach ministry of the Millington-Crumpton Charge, comprised  of Asbury UMC, Crumpton UMC, and Double Creek UMC.

The food pantry is open every Monday from 9-12, except when major holidays fall on that Monday, and is a TEFAP pantry which means clients must provide a photo ID and proof of assistance, i.e., SNAP, medical assistance, fuel assistance, etc.. Emergency food is available for those who just need a little help and don’t qualify for the regular pantry.

We serve clients in upper Kent and Queen Anne’s counties, and lower Cecil County.

LINK Against Hunger	
HOMEDONATEVOLUNTEERGET HELPABOUT USWHAT WE DOFAQsMore
	
Welcome to LINK, Inc.
The all-volunteer staff at LINK provide food assistance to people in need in the Herndon and Sterling, Virginia communities.


Get Help

How to request food assistance or financial support in Norther Virginia

Donate

We welcome both financial contributions and food donations

Volunteer

Would you or your organization like to help sort, package, or deliver food?

Food Deliveries

SIGN UP TO DELIVER FOOD

LINK delivers Tuesday through Fridays (between 4 pm – 8 pm) and Saturdays (between 1 pm – 4 pm) to families in Herndon and Sterling. Regrettably, we no longer deliver to Ashburn.

Mobile Food Pantry

SIGN UP FOR MOBILE PANTRY

The Capital Area Food Bank supplies food to the Mobile Food Pantry. Volunteers are needed on the second Tuesday of each month at Christ the Redeemer Church from 9am until the food is distributed. The start time varies based on the delivery arrival.

Holiday Program

VIEW MORE ON HOLIDAY PROGRAM

The 2025 Program took place on December 6th and 13th.  We served over 800 families and over 5,700 individuals.




We thank you for your generosity and your selfless time and effort to make our Holiday Program such a blessing to so many.

School Food Rescue

School Food Rescue is starting for the new school year. More information soon.




We rescue food from area schools in Fairfax and Loudoun County. Volunteers are needed to transport food from the schools to pantries or other locations. 

Store Food Rescue

SIGN UP FOR STORE FOOD RESCUE

Looking for a quick volunteer opportunity? LINK rescues food from local restaurants and stores, delivering delicious items from Auntie Anne's Pretzel, KFC, Target, Trader Joe's, Wawa, Bojangles Chicken, Great Harvest, and frozen proteins from Cornerstone. Our clients love it!

Simple Gesture

SIGN UP FOR SIMPLE GESTURE

We need volunteers to help unload food that is donated to LINK by "A Simple Gesture" program. The program is every other month on Saturdays from 9:30 am - 11 am at Christ the Redeemer Church. 

GRATITUDE TO OUR RECENT DONORS

Neighborhood Resource Center of Greater Fulton
 
NRC’s Core Program Areas

NRC was founded to build relationships, advocate for positive change, share resources, and develop skills to enhance residents’ lives in Greater Fulton and east Henrico County through our programs and partnerships. We are dedicated to these programs that are vital to building capacity in Fulton and east Henrico:

EDUCATION

Workshops and Technology Education

NUTRITION
Food and Outreach, Kitchen and Gardens

FINANCIAL OPPORTUNITY CENTER 
Financial Training, Job Placement, Resume Building, and Apprenticeship Programs

COMMUNITY ORGANIZING 
Outreach, Organizing, and Advocacy for Greater Fulton

Response to COVID-19: Our building is open with masking and social distancing requirements.

NRC Fulton is a 501(c)3 non-profit educational, cultural and nutrition center in Richmond, Virginia’s east end. Our mission is to build relationships, share resources, and develop skills to enhance lives in Greater Fulton and east Henrico through our Core Programs.

The Suffolk Christian Fellowship Center seeks to bridge the service gap for under-resourced and food insecure communities in Suffolk and Western Tidewater Virginia.
Bridging The Gap

“If it wasn’t for this place, I would not be able to pay bills, let alone feed the children. It’s a big help to be able to come more than once a week.”

A quote from A. A. -a 79 year old grandmother taking care of her 4 grandchildren who shared that she is seven dollars over the poverty line. 

Sharing food & bringing hope!

"The organization was founded with the aim of 'Lending a Helping Hand' to those facing hardship. Its mission focused on providing nutritious meals, ministerial and counseling support, clothing, and household items whenever available."

Founded in 1986, My Brother's Pantry is a joint endeavor of 12 area faith communities, dedicated to supporting  the food-insecure on the Broadneck Peninsula of Anne Arundel County, Maryland.  We are a healthy "family-choice" pantry, and our building can be found on the campus of College Parkway Baptist Church in Arnold, MD.
Providing healthy options to the Broadneck community

🍊 Choose Your Groceries
Our Pantry is open to anyone from anywhere (on a one-time basis). Community members who meet certain eligibility requirements can come by for continuing monthly assistance.

Just drop by The Pantry during our announced hours to find out more and select your groceries!
MORE
	
🍅 Make a Donation
My Brother's Pantry is a volunteer-run nonprofit organization, and we count on your support to achieve our mission. Consider a gift of grocery items or cash.
MORE
	
🍐 This Month's Hours
In April we will be open:

  🌷  Thursday, April 16 (3-5pm)
  🌷  Friday, April 17 (3-5pm)
  🌷  Saturday, April 18 (9-11am)

If your schedule allows, there is usually no waiting during our Friday and Saturday hours. Thanks to Mariners' Church for organizing our activities this month.
CALL
.Our building is located on the campus of College Parkway Baptist Church (301 College Parkway, Arnold, MD).
My Brother’s Pantry provides services based on need - without regard to religion, race, color, ancestry, national origin, age, physical characteristics, marital status, disability, gender, sexual orientation, or political beliefs.

Additional Food & Nutrition Programs

Supporting Frederick families year-round with nutritious meals.

The City of Frederick’s Department of Housing & Human Services (HHS) offers several food and nutrition assistance programs in partnership with federal, state, and local partners to help children and families access healthy meals year-round.

Summer Food Service Program

Funded by the U.S. Department of Agriculture, the Summer Food Service Program provides a free lunchtime meal to children age 18 and younger, targeting lower-income children who receive free or reduced-price meals during the school year. Nutritious meals are served Monday through Friday during June, July, and August at local parks and youth centers.

Nondiscrimination Statement

Helping Others

​

Through a variety of feeding programs and other hands-on activities, we bring our hearts and energy to love God’s people.  Our efforts help our neighbors and reach neighbors we’ve never met who live throughout the world.  

Helping Trinity Members
Food Pantry and Financial Assistance

Trinity offers food assistance and financial assistance to those in need. When we say, "Come as you are," we mean it! Lost your job? Disabled? Suffered an injury and can't work? Can't pay a utility bill? Short on food this month? Trinity aims to hold you in your time of need.

 

Going on over 30 years, our "Tuesday Night Outreach" program is completely volunteer based and runs on the generosity of our parishioners and those who we've once helped. 

​

If you are in need of financial assistance or wish to volunteer in this ministry, call Trinity at (540) 373-2996 and leave a message or email us at fxbgcare@gmail.com.

Welcome to the Franciscan Center

Answering the call. Adapting to the need. In the heart of the city since 1968.

PLATED TICKETS & INFO
Make A Difference

Your gift at any level makes a big difference to the working poor, indigent and homeless Baltimoreans we serve every day, at the Center and where they live.

DONATE NOW
Our New Social Enterprise:  Opening the B&O Cafe!
VISIT THE B&O CAFE
Helping Baltimore

The mission of the Franciscan Center is to provide emergency assistance and supportive outreach to persons who are economically disadvantaged in an effort to assist them in realizing their self-worth and dignity as people of God.

Nutrition

A healthy, good tasting meal is a critical foundation for those struggling to make their way. Culinary Services oversees a lunch (M-F) and dinner (T, W) program that feeds 400 to 600 people per day. Meals are made from scratch using fresh ingredients, some provided through partnerships with farms in the city and the region. An emergency food pantry also helps clients to make ends meet when they find themselves in difficult times.

Main Kitchen
Connection

For many indigent, homeless and working poor, the next biggest challenge after finding something to eat is knowing where to get help. Responsive Services provides intervention, advocacy, and resource information. Every service that clients need – from applying for benefits to securing housing to lining up a job – requires some type of government-issued identification, so this is a critical first step. Also available to clients: case management and counseling, eviction prevention and mail service, dental care, eyeglasses and more.

Responsive Services
Education

A steady job can provide an enormous boost to a person’s sense of dignity and hope. Empowerment Services focuses on the building blocks of successful employment: counseling, computer training, job-readiness classes, work attire and network building.

The Dignity Plates Training Academy provides basic culinary instruction to prepare students for entry-level culinary jobs, offers assistance to overcome barriers, and provides an example of passion for the culinary arts.

Training Academy
And You

Volunteers and donors are force multipliers. Working side by side with staff and contributing when and what they can, they enable the Franciscan Center to offer thousands of Baltimoreans in need a continuum of care that includes food, clothing, counseling, health care, housing, and training in the culinary arts and technology.

How You Can Help
Capital Campaign
Renaissance campaign set to create “An Environment Worthy Of Our Guests”

It’s been a quarter century and nearly four million meals since the last major renovation of the Franciscan Center. Time and hard use—nearly 20 percent of those meals were served just in the two years of Covid lockdowns—has taken its toll.

About the Bayside Community Food Pantry

The Bayside Community Food Pantry provides food to hungry people in the Chesapeake Beach and North Beach community. Our goal is to help the hungry and build relationships, sharing the love of Christ in a warm and welcoming environment. 

​

Come out for a short Bible Study Time before the Food Pantry Officially Starts:  9:15am

 
Location & Hours of Operation:
Saturdays, 9:30 am-11 am 
Bayside Baptist Church
3009 Chesapeake Beach Road
Chesapeake Beach, MD  20732
 
Food collection baskets are located in the Bayside Welcome Center. We continue to need weekly donations of food items and financial donations to replenish the Pantry, as the cupboards run bare some weeks because of tremendous demand. 
 
The Bayside Food Pantry is run by volunteers.  If you would like more information on how you can become a part of this ministry, please contact the church office.

Our Mission
To distribute food to our neighbors in need by mobilizing our giving community and volunteers.

In Fairfax County, more than 105,000 people are experiencing food insecurity. Many of our neighbors are struggling to balance the rising costs of food, housing, healthcare, and other essentials.

For 30 years, Food for Others has served as a vital part of Northern Virginia’s safety net – providing food for families who are struggling to make ends meet. Together, we can build a community where everyone has access to the food assistance they need to thrive.

WJLA-TV joined Food for Others in April to shed light on challenges that lie ahead due to USDA budget cuts. PET FOOD PANTRY
The MCHS Pet Food Pantry is here to help!

Dog food, cat food & cat litter is available to those in our community who need a helping hand through hard times.


The pantry operates solely on generous donations. Inventory is based on what has been received throughout the week.

﻿Item availability cannot be guaranteed. Litter, cat, and dog food may be limited or unavailable at times.

Pet Food Pantry Hours

The pantry is opens at 11 am on Wednesdays

(except major holidays) and remains open while supplies last.




Please ring the doorbell for assistance when you arrive.  Parking and our front door are located at the back of the building.  We’re also a short walk from the Rockville Metro station.


Donate Pet Food to Help

Your donations keep our pantry going!  




We are able to accept donations of unopened, unexpired, non-prescription, cat and dog food for our pet food pantry. 




Our pantry always needs a wide variety of food:

Canned cat food (kitten & adult)
Dry cat food (kitten & adult)
Dry dog food (puppy & adult)
Canned dog food (puppy & adult)
Cat litter
Cat treats
Dog treats

Please note; smaller bags of dry food are easier for us to store, handle, and distribute than very large ones. 




Donations may be dropped off every day but holidays between 11 a.m. and 5 p.m. Look for the table at the front door. Thank you for your generous support!


SERVICES

Cat and Dog Rescue Adoptions, Spaying/Neutering, Vaccination Clinics, Micro-chipping, Outreach Programs, Pet Food Pantry, and more!

More Info
Our Mission
The Presbyterian Community Center addresses homelessness and poverty of underserved families in southeast Roanoke by providing emergency financial aid, food access, after-school education, and spiritual development through direct services and community partnerships.

Our vision is a vibrant southeast Roanoke, free of poverty. Established in 1967, the PCC also provides services to at-risk residents of northeast Roanoke and eastern Roanoke County, including the Town of Vinton.

WE MUST MAKE
 THE BEST LIFE,
REGARDLESS OF OUR CIRCUMSTANCES

A Letter From Our Founders

​

Our dreams of giving back to a community that molded us into the women we are today has finally come to fruition and we could not be more proud. Even though our grandmother is no longer with us, we feel closer to her through the establishment of Catherine’s Family and Youth Services. Our grandmother instilled the importance of being humble and helping those in need at a very early age. It is our honor to continue her humanitarianism to a community that she held close to her heart. It is our hope that we are able to touch as many people in need that our grandmother touched during her life. Catherine’s Family and Youth Services promises to leave no stone unturned and no one is exempt from receiving services. We look forward to everyone joining us throughout this journey in offering the gift of empowerment, education and encouragement. 

 

Sincerely,

​

Valarie V. Matthews

Executive Director/Co-Founder

​

Shannon S. Morgan

Co-Founder/Board of Director

Get to Know Us

The Millennium World Outreach is a Life-giving church, we are a community of men and women who have accepted the life-giving message of the Gospel of Jesus Christ.  We seek to be empowered by God's Spirit, and enjoy the company of other fellow believers and seekers in worship,  praise and fellowship.
    We believe that man's highest duty and greatest privilege is to worship God and enjoy Him forever.  Worship brings us into God's presence and as such becomes the fountain of our life and service to Christ.
    We believe that the atmosphere of our church should be charaterized by love, acceptance and forgiveness. It should be a safe place for people to gather to seek the deeper things of Christ.  It should be a place where the love of Jesus is demonstrated through the love of his people.
    We believe that the Word of God is truth-that it is God's gracious revelation of Himself and His will to mankind.  Therefore, we will be people of the Bible, who will read, study, share and accept it's principles for our lives.  We understand that culture changes, but the Word of God will stand forever.
    We believe that church should not just be a crowd.  It should be a community and at the core of this community there should be dedicated diciples-Christ followers who are committed to the habits of Godly living and active Christian service.

Prayer Request
.

We’re big on worship, fellowship, growing, learning and serving in the community. The Bible says that we are "ambassadors for Christ." That's 2 Corinthians 5:20. That means we are representing the Kingdom of God to the people around us and in our community and wherever we go. Knowing that we're representing the Kingdom of God helps us to be alert for opportunities to make a positive difference in peoples' lives and to allow the Spirit of God to use us whenever and wherever He wants.

Being in the Body of Christ isn’t a spectator sport or a spiritual hobby. We don’t gather just to sing songs, listen to people talk and throw a few dollars into the offering plate. For us, it’s about knowing the Lord, growing in Him. Being involved with His people, making friends, getting plugged in, becoming closer to God and becoming a better person than you ever thought possible. Many of our people come from various places, often with careers that constantly move them and their families around. Because of that, they never get the opportunity to establish real roots. We'd like to help you and your family get involved quickly and give you opportunities to use your giftings to make a true impact in peoples' lives.

Here are some areas you may want to get involved with...

Food Bank. Feeding the hungry was, and is, an important part of Jesus' ministry. He didn't just feed people who were poor. He fed any and all who were hungry. We feel the same way. If a person eats, they qualify to receive food from our food pantry ministry.

With the increase of food prices, there are more and more people struggling to feed themselves and their families. God has blessed us with relationships with several stores and corporations that generously provide food for our food bank ministry. We are thrilled to be able to provide meat, breads, desserts, veggies to the people of Western Charles County at our weekly food pantry.

Due to the generosity of various donors and businesses, we are not only able to provide food for individuals and families, but also we supply to other local food pantries, churches and soup kitchens.

The food bank is open every Tuesday beginning at 4 pm. Setup begins at 2 pm. This may be a perfect way for you to get involved in helping others to be able to eat healthily and plentifully. 

 

The Worship Team. Make a joyful noise to the Lord! If you play an instrument reasonably well or sing, this may be an opportunity for you to be blessed and be a blessing to others!

Maybe you have sound engineer experience, and you LOVE helping singers and musicians sound their best, to optimize the worship experience of the congregation.

Using your gifts and talents is a great way to worship the Lord and encourage other people at the same time. The Worship Team is an important part of our Sunday worship services. God loves music and we love music!

We are always on the lookout for dedicated singers, musicians and sound people who want to use their talents to glorify the Lord and to edify His people.

You may not be a singer, but you play an instrument or two... or a few! Our Worship Band would love to meet you and let you audition. The Worship Team practices on Friday nights and comes in at 10 am on Sunday morning for a further practice, prior to the 11 am service.

 

Married Couples Ministry. Marriages are under attack and stressed more than ever. Temptation is everywhere, and no marriage escapes tests and trials. We don't want our couples' marriages to become casualties! We do all we can to support our couples and families. Rev. Hooker and Linda Hooker oversee the Married Couples Ministry. This group meets periodically for dinners, events and whatever they prayerfully choose to do! It's a good time to get out just as a couple, away from the kids, away from the never ending chores and tasks at home, and enjoy each other's company and fellowship. All of our couples who attend and work with this ministry love it and look forward to the next get together!

 

Men of Favor. We want our me
n to be strong in the Lord, mature and high-functioning men of God. We're in a culture that exhibits poor impulse control and self-centered lifestyles. Too many men are struggling with the unrealistic expectations of our culture, and feel like they just cant measure up. Then, there's financial pressure, all kinds of temptations and personal challenges and struggles. Many men live with a sense of inadequacy and failure. Men of Favor is led by Rev. Hooker. We meet monthly for prayer, teaching that's pertinent to men, fellowship and, of course... food! These meetings are usually right after the 11 o'clock service on Sundays. It's a great time for men and teenage boys to gather together and gr

Emergency Assistance
New River Community Action’s Emergency Assistance Program

The Emergency Assistance Program (EAP) offers a variety of temporary assistance for residents or transients who are 200% poverty income or below and experiencing financial crises.  

Low-income people frequently face high utility bills, a decrease in income, disabilities, chronic health issues, a significant amount of medical debt, housing costs greater than 50 percent of income, or homelessness.  

EAP helps with food, rent, mortgage, utility, and heating fuel. Some area offices can assist with gasoline to medical appointments. 

To receive Emergency Assistance, applicants must be experiencing a crisis beyond their control. Assistance is provided on a first come first serve basis after all documentation has been received. Call the office in your locality to schedule an appointment. 

Required documentation includes: 

Photo identification card, 
Social Security numbers for all household members, 
Proof of crisis (bills, etc.), 
Proof of income ((pay stubs for the last 4 week period, statement from employer, or income tax statements), 
Additional documents may be required depending upon the situation.

A direct deposit bank statement is not acceptable proof of income. The entire household’s income is considered for eligibility and must not total more than 200% of federal poverty income guidelines.

To receive assistance, the household must have exhausted their own personal resources and accessed other forms of assistance, such as the Department of Social Services Heating Assistance or Crisis Program, etc. when available.

Application for NRCA Emergency Assistance/ Housing Program

Provide information for your local New River Valley office to determine eligibility. 

New River Community Action Online Request for Services

New River Community Action partners with the Montgomery County Emergency Assistance Program (MCEAP) and the Pulaski County Emergency Needs Task Force (PCENTF) to administer the Emergency Assistance Program. Assistance services provided in Montgomery and Pulaski counties are determined by MCEAP and PCENTF policies and could differ from the guidelines described below. 

For rent or mortgage payments, a partial payment may be made, and the household may be required to pay the remainder of the payment. If the household is unable to pay a portion of the bill, the landlord must sign a paper stating they are willing to accept a partial payment; the assistance must allow the household to remain on the property for at least an additional 30 days. 

Utility assistance requires a termination notice or must be disconnected. For heating fuel assistance, the household’s fuel tank must be out of heating fuel or approximately 10% remaining. 

Households may receive food monthly from the CCA food pantry. A clothing voucher to local thrift store may be provided; extra consideration may be given for special situations. 

For people who are homeless, referrals are made to the New River Family Shelter, To Our House, or Women’s Resource Center and others as appropriate. Transportation to the shelters may be considered if available.  

For emergency housing assistance, call Central Intake during business hours Monday – Friday at 540-639-3159. On weekends and holidays contact the on-call number 540-320-7028. 

NRCA partners with numerous human services organizations; referrals are received from and made to other organizations as appropriate. 

Donations from the community are used for direct services, while federal and state Community Services Block Grant (CSBG) plus local government funds to CCA are used for office space, staff, and related expenses. 

Contact the office in your locality to schedule an appointment for rent, mortgage, or utility assistance. Walk-ins for food assistance are welcome in all localities.

Our Mission
Delmarva Community Services, Inc. is a multi-service, non-profit agency dedicated to helping people who wish to maintain dignity, and an independent lifestyle within their community on the Delmarva Peninsula.

At DCS we believe all people should be treated with respect. The services we provide should always embrace the highest integrity and quality. As advocates for our consumers, we assist all individuals towards self-sufficiency and independence.

We do this through a process that emphasizes communication, planning, and teamwork that recognizes and seizes opportunities that will benefit our consumers while realizing we are accountable to the public we serve and stewards of government and community resources.
Burke & Herbert Bank Donates to Delmarva Community Services
The Burke & Herbert Bank Foundation recently donated to support Delmarva Community Services, Inc. (DCS) in Cambridge to help further the vital role DCS plays for local families, children, students, and seniors.
“Delmarva Community Services’ programs, including the county-wide food pantry, senior activities, delivered meals, homeless services, and youth empowerment initiatives, make a lasting impact on our community.  At Burke & Herbert Bank, we believe in always being invested in enriching the communities we serve, and together we’re helping build a stronger, more vibrant community,” comments Eastern Shore Market President Heather Bacher of Burke & Herbert Bank.
According to Andy Hollis, “The demand for our services in Dorchester County continues to grow. Having Burke & Herbert Bank as a community partner will help us continue to make strides in meeting these growing needs,” states Andy Hollis, President/CEO of DCS.
Delmarva Community Services Inc. (DCS) is a multi-service organization founded in 1974 that serves individuals with developmental disabilities, seniors, and individuals living in poverty and provides childcare and transportation services.
Caption: Pictured left to right are Senior Vice President/Senior Credit Officer Billie Jo Newman, Burke & Herbert Bank; Andy Hollis, President & CEO of Delmarva Community Services, Inc.; and Eastern Shore Market President Heather Bacher, Burke & Herbert Bank. 
	
​Delmarva Community Services Appoints New Director of Sprouts Early Childhood Learning Center
Delmarva Community Services, Inc. (DCS) has recently appointed Petrae Simpson as its new Director of Childcare for Sprouts Early Childhood Learning Center @ Chesapeake Grove in Cambridge.  Simpson previously worked as director of Bright Beginnings Child Care Center in Stevensville, Maryland.
“Petrae is a passionate and experienced childcare professional with a strong early childhood education and daycare management background who came to us highly recommended. We are thrilled she is joining our team as she is dedicated to creating a nurturing and engaging environment where children can develop in their early years,” states Andy Hollis, President/CEO of DCS.
With extensive experience in licensing regulations, staff development, and curriculum implementation, Simpson understands the importance of research-based teaching strategies.  She is a proponent of incorporating the Frog Street Curriculum into the Center’s program. This comprehensive curriculum is designed to support children from infancy through pre-kindergarten, focusing on intentional, developmentally appropriate learning experiences that foster critical thinking, creativity, and school readiness.
According to Simpson, Frog Street’s emphasis on social-emotional development, language acquisition, and STEAM-based activities aligns with her own philosophy of holistic learning. By implementing engaging, hands-on experiences, she believes that every child can receive the foundational skills needed for lifelong success.
“I am committed to maintaining high-quality standards – including building strong relationships with families, creating an inclusive and welcoming environment, and mentoring educators to continuously grow in their roles. I am driven by a

As a community connector for crisis support, CCHASM partners with trusted churches and agencies across Central Virginia to link individuals and families to food, utility relief, and a path forward.

Food Assistance

We work with 15 regional pantry partners to provide nonperishable groceries and essential items to individuals and families facing a crisis.

Utility Assistance

Welcome

Breath of God Lutheran Church is a young, multi-cultural, progressive Christian community in Southeast Baltimore. We see the Holy Spirit doing great things in these neighborhoods: working to bring love, justice, hope, transformation, and new life. Our hope, prayer, and goal are to be a key part of that holy work among our neighbors of all faiths and backgrounds. A worship service of Holy Communion takes place each Sunday at 10:30am.

We welcome you to our website, and invite you to look around. If you have questions, comments, thoughts, or prayer requests, we would love to hear from you.

Soplo de Dios Iglesia Luterana es una comunidad Cristiana joven y progresiva en el sureste de Baltimore. Veimos el Espíritu Santo haciendo grandes cosas en nuestros barrios: trabajando de crear amor, justicia, esperanza, transformación, y nueva vida. Nuestra esperanza, nuestra oración, y nuestra meta son participar completamente en este trabajo santo con nuestros vecinos diversos.

Bienvenidos a nuestro sitio, y gracias por su interés. Si tiene preguntas, pensamientos, o necesidades, no falta de enviar un mensaje o llamar al pastor.

Worship

I was glad when they said unto me “Let us go into the house of the Lord.” (Psalm 122:1)


At the center of Christian life is worship, the gathering of God pople to pray and sing together, hear God’s Word, be washed in the water of baptism, and be fed at God’s table. Worship ought to be rooted in the patterns and practices Christians have shared for 2,000 years, while drawing on music and on style from the diverse cultures of our neighborhood. All are invited and welcome to worship and to receive Holy Communion. 

Our regular 10:30am Sunday community worship opportunity takes place simultaneously in the sanctuary and via our Zoom channel and YouTube. In warmer weather (April through November), Stoop Worship and Lively Worship both usually take place outdoors.

__________________

Holy Week with Breath of God Lutheran Church

Palm Sunday Family Worship, 9am, March 29th

Palm Sunday Community Worship, 10:30am, March 29th

Maundy Thursday Worship, 7pm, April 2nd

Good Friday Worship, 7pm, April 3rd

Easter Sunrise Worship, 6:30am, April 5th

Easter Family Worship, 9am, April 5th, in the park

Easter Community Worship, 10:30am, April 5th

______________

Weekly Sunday worship, with Holy Communion, takes place at 10:30am. Nuestro culto cada Domingo es en Ingles y en Español. . Our Prayground area is available and ready to be a resource to families with young children.

Morning Prayer, 9am, Tuesday and Friday each week, on Zoom and Facebook Live.

"Lively" Worship designed for kids, birth to 11 years old, and their adults, takes place normally every two weeks, in the sanctuary during cold weather, and via Zoom. Check the calendar for the next upcoming dates.

Mid-week prayer services take place on Wednesdays at 7pm in December while we prepare for Christmas. Please double check the church calendar for specifics.

Stoop Worship, the first Saturday of the month, 8pm, May through October, with Holy Communion, takes place on the front porch of the church. Check the calendar for the next upcoming dates.

Sermon Archive

Along with other Lutheran Christians in the ELCA, we believe in a three-fold engagement with the Word of God. That is to say, the Word of God is, first and foremost, alive in Jesus Christ. Secondarily, the Word of God is proclaimed in preaching, study, song, and prayer, inasmuch as it points toward God's love for us in Jesus Christ. Thirdly, the Word of God is written in Holy Scripture inasmuch as it helps us to understand God's love for us in Jesus Christ, which informs our proclamation of the Word. You can listen to a selection of our Sunday sermons here.

Worship Service Archive

You can review and engage with our worship services beginning in mid-March 2020 through our YouTube channel. You can also use that same channel to participate in worship live every Sunday morning at 10:30am.

Worship Leadership Sign-Up

We have many opportunities to participate in worship leadership here at Breath of God. You can e-mail Pastor Madeline to learn more about opportunities to lead, and to get on the schedule.

Serve

“Teacher, which commandment in the law is the greatest?” Jesus said to him, “‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind.’ This is the greatest and first commandment. And a second is like it: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’” (Matthew 22:36-39)

Together we can Improve Lives

Welcome to CARIBBEAN HELP CENTER ORG. We are a non-profit organization (501C3) determined to better our community, one person at a time. We serve the greater Washington metropolitan area as we are conveniently located in Silver Spring, MD. We have over 29 years of experience and provide a variety of services for low-income individuals and families. Over the past fiscal year CHC supported more than 2,174 clients.

Start with a little
ABOUT CHC

The Greater Washington Metropolitan Area is the home to a high concentration of unassisted immigrants. Over the past fiscal year, the Caribbean Help Center has provided support to more than 2174 patrons. CHC continues to reach out to the community to provide assistance to even more low income families and individuals.

Founded in 1995 by the Rev. Evans Faustin , the Caribbean Help Center is a thriving 501C3 non-profit organization working to help people open doors of opportunity that they were unable to before. With a staff of four, nine board members and eight zealous volunteers, the center is determined to change people’s lives. If you need assistance with education, employment, information and referrals. Interpretation, food assistance, immigration, public services, and / or finding health care the Caribbean Help Center is willing and able to push you forward. Since we are a non-profit organization, we depend on financial donations among others to keep this dream alive. Donating to the Caribbean Help Center is not only a monetary gift to the organization but an investment into the community. Do not hesitate to be a part of our organization.

AFFORDABLE HEALTH CARE

When a lot of adults and children began to fall sick at regular intervals in our community in the USA, people did not know where to turn to. Most of them cannot afford the expenses of hospitalization and medication. Caribbean Help Center provides affordable health care.

Read More
“PUBLIC SERVICES”

The population we serve continues to struggle in this tough economy. Clients often don’t understand about the social services for which they are eligible and don’t know how to navigate the system to obtain them.

Read More
FOOD DISTRIBUTION

While many may question the connection between hungry and illiteracy, there is an undeniable link. To eradicate hunger and illiteracy Caribbean Help Center provides food assistance for hungry people. We believe that beyond just being fed, it is imperative that adults and children get nutrition and wholesome meals.

Read More
CHC PROGRAMS

The mission of the Caribbean Help Center is to provide assistance and support to people in need located in the United States and the Caribbean. Over the past 29 years the Caribbean Help Center has provided various services to help people create better lives for themselves and improve their communities by offering relief to their problems and the promise of brighter days to come. The CHC’s purpose is to help individuals and families access academic, economic and civic opportunity that enhances their ability to strengthen their neighborhoods, succeed at school and work, raise healthy families and become engaged members of their communities.

THANKSGIVING
Our Thanksgiving Tradition

Each November, CCHASM provides over 3,500 households and more than 10,000 residents of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights, Dinwiddie, Hopewell, Petersburg, and Prince George with a full Thanksgiving meal. Each registered household receives a full bag of nonperishable Thanksgiving items and a $20 grocery gift card to purchase a turkey or fresh ingredients.

 

We work with schools, churches, social service agencies, volunteers, and corporate sponsors to coordinate distribution through a combination of pickup events, home deliveries, and partner agency outreach.

We have completed Thanksgiving registration.

Anyone who has left us a voicemail, we are working those requests now. If you have requested delivery, we will be contacting you to verify you meet requirements.  

Pickup & Delivery Options
Drive Thru

Held at Chesterfield County Fairgrounds on the Saturday before Thanksgiving

Home Delivery

For seniors and individuals without transportation

Pre-Pickup

Through schools, social workers, mental health providers, and local agencies

Lorton Community Action Center

The Lorton Community Action Center (LCAC) has been serving the Lorton community since 1975.

LCAC seeks to provide low-income individuals, senior citizens, and families residing in Fort Belvoir, Lorton, Newington, and other portions of southeast Fairfax County with access to basic needs and the opportunity to empower themselves through LCAC’s self-sufficiency programs.

LCAC strives to connect people with other programs in the area that will have a positive impact on a family’s long-term “health.”

YOU BELONG HERE

Picture this - most of us are looking for the same things: a place where we actually belong, where people see us, where we can grow, and where we can do something that matters. That’s what Bridgeway is about - not just a church service on Sundays. It’s a family that looks like the world around us: different colors, cultures, and stories all worshipping together, moving together in unity and love in the same direction - towards Christ.

Here, you’ll find worship that lifts you up, teaching that connects scripture to real life, and spaces for kids and teens that they’ll actually want to be part of. You’ll also see people rolling up their sleeves and working for the Kingdom - whether it’s through the Community Cupboard food pantry, serving neighbors, or just walking with each other through tough seasons.

MORE ABOUT US
YOU ARE WELCOME
ONE MULTICULTURAL CHURCH
MULTIPLE MARYLAND LOCATIONS

Led by Founding and Senior Pastor, author on race relations, Dr. David Anderson. Bridgeway is one church in multiple locations. We have shared leadership, same spirit, and unique campuses.

KIDS ZONE

In Children’s Church, children between the ages of 5 to 12 years old are invited to glorify God through a time of communal prayer and worship. Children’s Church also includes small group time that creates a place for Children to grow spiritually through Biblical learning and caring relationships.

Learn More
YOUTH ZONE

In Youth Meet-Up, we will help every young adult (ages of 13 - 21 year old) come to know their purpose and destiny in life and walk in it. Using the Bible as out textbook we bring the Biblical Encounters to our youth by using real life situations to help them have a higher level of understanding and spiritual development.

Learn More
ADULT GROUPS

We regularly offer industry-relevant training programs and workshops. Our certified instructors have many years of experience in their specialist area and enjoy sharing their expert knowledge with their students.

Learn More


MEET OUR PASTOR

A man of faith, determination, and integrity, Dr. A. Bryan Claxton is a practical faith teachers, and has a heart for families.

 

 

 

LEARN MORE
About Us 
Get to know more about what makes us different.

In the fall of 1968 a prophetic word came to Reverend and Mrs. Claxton through a man of God saying, “Make haste and establish a house of liberty; a house of refuge for God’s people; for there are many in this city in houses of bondage to pharaohs behind stain-glass windows”. So the Chapel of the Risen Savior church was founded by Pastor & Mrs. Aaron B. Claxton in the living room of 3707 Woodbine Avenue in Northwest Baltimore. The church began with their six children, one senior, Mother Florence B. Williams and two other members. Sixty children were gathered from the neighborhood for a summer program and the church was established.

 

In 1985, the church moved to its present location, 5401 Frankford Avenue and the name changed to

New Creation Christian Church. Elder A. Bryan Claxton, the eldest son and Sheila Claxton his wife were elevated to the pastorate of the church in 1991. Apostle Aaron and Dr. Deborah were called to a global ministry and are currently traveling the nations preaching and teaching the gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ with signs following.

 

“Pastor Bryan” retired from his government job to become fulltime Pastor in 1996. In 2002 Pastor A. Bryan Claxton was consecrated to the office of Bishop. The church is currently in an expansion project, following a mandate from the Lord to “Expand for the Harvest”.



WE'D LOVE TO SEE YOU

 

Join us for a Sunday service, a virtual bible study or one of our upcoming events, we would love to meet you! 


 

PLAN A VISIT

About

New Creation is a local non-denominational church with a global Kingdom perspective. We are intimate enough to get to know you and your family, and large enough to impact the Greater Baltimore community, the nation and the world.

Feeding Greene Mission
To see that no one goes to bed hungry in our community.

​Since 1995, Feeding Greene has been providing supplemental food assistance to area families in need. Currently, we are serving over 900 families who receive over 120,000 pounds of food each month. Feeding Greene is a 501(c)3 non-profit volunteer driven organization.
Feeding Greene is so thankful for this donation that will go to support our Backpack Program which has grown significantly since last year. Families with children are really feeling the pinch in our community and are reaching out like never before! Pictured here are some of the Post Grad Students from our local William Monroe High School, Director Rhonda Oliver, and Coordinator of our Backpack Program Jen Wattier, all of whom will see first hand the impact of this generous donation. This kind of support is so important to small, rural nonprofits. Many thanks to Dunkin Joy for making this possible!
AND 
​We are always SO GRATEFUL for the support we receive from Twice Is Nice Upscale Resale Boutique! From receiving grants to help our seniors receive nutritious food, to “mentions” on social media, to encouragement for our mission, Twice Is Nice supports our local community in amazing ways! Take a look at the grant recipient list for 2025!  Shop at TIN this season and support their goals as they support us!! Thank you, thank you, thank you TIN!

Our Mission
Food Sunday’s mission is to provide emergency supplemental food weekly in a way that enhances human dignity.
Our Founder
In 1982 Dominic Jollie encouraged the religious community to collect food at their Sunday services for distribution through a county wide food bank network that was incorporated as “Food Sunday”.

He stated: “To know hunger exists and to do nothing is the shame and bane of human life”.

What We Do

Martin’s House & Barn provides food support for Caroline County residents and others in need at its 14374 Benedictine Lane location in Ridgely, MD.

 

Recipients must wear a mask during the distribution and should remain in their vehicles while a staff person brings to them a selection of food and bags of perishable and nonperishable groceries and household items. 

 

Food distribution hours are Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays from 8:30 to 11:30 a.m., and Wednesdays from 6:00 to 7:30 p.m. 

 

Martin's House & Barn's Thrift Shop, also located in the Barn, offers quality clothing and household items and is open and accepting donations during the following hours:

Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday: 8:30 – 11:30 am

Wednesday: 6:00 - 7:30 pm

The First Saturday of Each Month: 8:00 am – 12 noon

 

Martin’s House & Barn provides an array of services through a single point of entry with a dignified approach to address immediate and long-term needs on a four-acre rural complex.

 

Family Shelter

Our mission is to help meet basic human needs.

The Family Shelter in Martin’s House provides a safe haven for families struggling with homelessness.

Food Pantry

Our vision is a community without homelessness, hunger, and poverty.

The Food Pantry is located in the Barn and supported by local businesses, farms, caring donors, and the Maryland Food Bank. Regular programming during Food Pantry hours allows clients to access information on community services, health information, educational opportunities, and life skills classes.

Thrift Store

We provide clothing, furniture, and household items to help local families in need.

Martin's House & Barn's Thrift Shop, also located in the Barn, offers quality clothing and household items and is now open and accepting donations:

Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday: 8:30 – 11:30 am

Wednesday: 6:00 - 7:30 pm

The First Saturday of Each Month: 8:00 am – 12 noon

Please Note: Donations are accepted every day by leaving in our drop-off box at the rear of the Thrift Store, or by calling for an appt. to drop them off during operating hours.

Emergency supplies remain available by appointment.

Homelessness Prevention Services

Poverty is not a choice. We show compassion and respect for all our clients as they struggle with challenging situations and realities.

Martin's House & Barn's Homelessness Prevention Services focuses on a “Housing First” approach to help community clients avoid eviction and shelter clients to quickly get into their own housing. Case management helps clients determine obstacles, make a plan and navigate available sources.

Non-perishable donations can be dropped off at the back of the Pantry, which can be accessed via the church parking lot on Mechanic Street.

CAMP HOPE

CAMP HOPE, Inc. was an independent organization that served Allegany County Maryland and the surrounding areas.  But, CAMP HOPE was so much more than that!   For 48 years, over 400 youth and adults from various churches and denominations repaired many, many homes.  Church teams each served one week while staying together in the dorms of Frostburg State University.  Daily routine included meals together, working at home sites, prayer/reflection/songs, worship, group activities, more meals together, and truck loads of FUN!  Everyone involved with Camp Hope (youth volunteer, adult volunteer, homeowner) was blessed as God's unconditional love was shared and celebrated through service, fellowship, witness, and song.       

​

Frostburg United Methodist Church started CAMP HOPE in 1977, and unfortunately, came to an end after two weeks of extraordinary service in 2025. Our church hosted daily breakfasts and several evening services each week, and provided its own work teams for years.  

 

If you are interested in sharing memories of your experience with Camp Hope with Frostburg UMC, please contact:

 

Ron Tenaglio via our Office Administrator:

office@frostburgumc.org

 

​

PENNIES INTO POTATOES

The Society of Saint Andrew is modeled after Jesus and feeds those who are hungry every day.  Volunteers collect potatoes by gleaning fields (going back after harvest and collecting what is left).  They also collect potatoes that have been rejected by commercial markets due to slight imperfections such as size or shape.  The potatoes are perfectly edible and donated by the farmers.  The potatoes are free and funds are only needed for packaging and distribution.  This means that a pound of potatoes can be given to those in need for approximately a mere cents per pound.

 

Our church contributes through a program called Pennies Into Potatoes.  All pennies in the offering plate each Sunday go to this project.  Jars are also in the entry way for collection.  Volunteers are always needed to sort and bag potatoes when the local food bank is in need. 

 

More information can be found on the End Hunger website.

ST. MARY'S EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH

Join Our Community of Faith

 In-person worship services Sundays at 8:45 am

About St. Mary's Evangelical Lutheran Church

 St. Mary's is an assembly of Christian believers who gather together in the community of Silver Run. We are a collection of people with different needs and concerns, yet we are called to a unity which grows from joining together in worship of God,
​the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.




Each of us has different God-given talents which we are called to use to witness to our faith in God's love revealed through Jesus Christ. We are called and empowered by the Holy Spirit to invite others to share in our faith in Christ and to welcome them into the life of our congregation.

MEET OUR PASTOR
Rev. Samuel F. Chamelin

He has been a local church pastor in Carroll County for 19 years. He is a 2014 graduate of Gettysburg Seminary, where he concentrated in Town and Country Church ministry. He grew up on a dairy farm and paid for his wife’s engagement ring by milking cows. He was the founder of The Keep & Till in Carroll County, MD, a Christian community founded at the nexus of radical (“deep rooted”) spirituality, food justice, and environmental regeneration. He served as the Sustainability Coordinator for United Lutheran Seminary. He is a Re:Generate Fellow with Wake Forest School of Divinity. He has attended the Collegeville Institute for spiritual writers. He is a devoted 4-H leader and volunteer, and his children are all involved in livestock projects – swine, sheep, and dairy cows/goats. He is a former college baseball and roller hockey player, and sees no problem with a pastor laying a good hit in a hockey game!

History
Did you know?

St. Mary's Evangelical Lutheran Church is the only church in the Delaware-Maryland Synod named for a woman and is one of the synod's oldest churches.




On May 31, 2012, we celebrated our 250th anniversary.

We began as a Union Church of two congregations, Lutheran and Reformed. A log building was erected on a 15-acre lot by the members. The log building sufficed for almost 60 years.


After much pondering and planning, a newer church, again a union church, was erected in 1821, across the street from the present Reformed (United Church of Christ) church. By this time much of the virgin forest area of Maryland was depleted, and timber was thought to be scarce, so the new structure was made of bricks.

The brick church was finally completed and dedicated in 1822. Pastor Yoder, writing in 1912, described the church as follows:

The Sanctuary

 "The sanctuary had then an up-to-date appearance, and was the revelation of the general status of the people of the community. It was a high, square brick building, and seemed to have been started with no positive knowledge as to where the last course of brick would be laid heavenward. The building never had a chimney. It was heated by stoves, and the pipes pierced the ceiling overhead, depositing all the smoke and soot in the upper story. As they had always burned wood, what marvel it was that the church was never burned down by escaping sparks. The furniture consisted of straight-back benches and elders' amen corner, the almost perpendicular galleries on three sides but above all, and most interesting was the wine-glass pulpit, placed half way between the galleries and floors, on small round pillars, with the winding stair-way leading thereto and the large, round sounding board suspended within a few feet from the top by a stout iron rod, made to represent a sun-flower stalk with its leaves and flowers hanging from the side. The circular pulpit and sounding board were formerly used in all churches. An old father of St. Mary's, now deceased, once said that the sounding board was placed there for the purpose of dropping on the head of the minister who preached too long or did not preach the truth."

The Union of these two denominations had lasted for about one hundred and thirty years, but the time had come for separation. Eventually the Lutherans sold and the Reformed bought the union interest in the old brick union church. In 1893, the Reformed people dedicated a church at the top of the hill, across the road from the site of the discarded brick church which was torn down and sold as rubble. The "land in square about the old church" became a hitching ground, and is now a parking lot. On a corner lot at the bottom of the hill, along the main highway leading between Gettysburg and Baltimore, the Lutherans consecrated a site for their edifice.

The church was built of rocks - graystone. It is trimmed with brownstone from Hummelstown, PA.

From a newspaper clipping, quoted, in part: "One of the most interesting and important events in the history of St. Mary's Evangelical Lutheran Church of Si

Montgomery County Corps
Montgomery County Corps 

The Salvation Army Montgomery County Corps opened its doors nearly three decades ago offering help to those most in need. Last year, hundreds of people facing hunger and eviction received the Emergency Assistance needed to stay in their homes, keep the lights on, and put food on the table. In addition to feeding empty stomachs, the Corps feeds hungry hearts. Music education, a women's outreach group, and youth programs help to develop the potential of each individual that comes through these doors.

 Community Programs
Social Services Monday through Friday from 9am to 4:30pm (Closed for lunch: 12:00-1:00pm) Walk-ins are not accepted. Please call (301)515-5354, Ext. 0 for an appointment.
Food Distribution on Fridays from 1:00pm until the total supply is distributed.
Youth Music Lessons and Children’s Youth Program on Wednesdays at 4:30pm

Prayer and Worship on Thursdays at 6:00 pm

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

COPE FOOD PANTRY

COPE Food Pantry
McAllister is home to the area’s COPE Food Pantry. The Food Pantry is open every Thursday from 8:30 am until noon. Someone can be designated to pick up food for you but you MUST WRITE A LETTER and send with that person stating their name, date they are picking up your food, and signed by you the recipient registered with COPE. Unfortunately deliveries are not available. If you need a delivery, please contact Alleghany Highlands Community Services Board at 540-965-2135 OR the local Alleghany-Covington Department of Social Services at 540-444-7100. NO PROOF OF INCOME IS REQUIRED BY COPE. Curb-side service only.






COPE Food Pantry is participating in this year’s 2026 Give Local Alleghany Highlands event on May 19, 2026. This event is hosted by the Alleghany Foundation and the Alleghany Highlands Chamber of Commerce. Please see the below link for details on this event and consider giving!

Bethesda-Chevy Chase Meals on Wheels delivers meals to persons in the Bethesda/Chevy Chase areas of Montgomery County who are unable to prepare food on their own
FIND OUT IF YOU ARE IN OUR SERVICE AREA
About

B‑CC Meals on Wheels volunteers have been delivering two nutritious meals each weekday morning to “shut-ins” in the Bethesda-Chevy Chase area since 1973.  B‑CC Meals on Wheels is a 501(c)(3) organization, and donations to the organization are tax-deductible.  We operate from the North Chevy Chase Christian Church at 8814 Kensington Parkway, North Chevy Chase, MD  20815. Meals are delivered Monday through Friday, and the office is generally staffed every weekday between 8:00 a.m. and 10:30 a.m. 

 

See FAQ for more information on delivery during holidays and weather cancellations.

AN ONGOING MISSION IS THE MAIN ST UNITED METHODIST CHURCH
FOOD PANTRY
SERVING FAMILIES IN NEED

The Pantry operates from Monday through Thursday, 9:30 to 11:00 a.m.  
Purchasing additional food becomes more and more necessary each month.

WE WELCOME ALL VOLUNTEERS

Main Street continues to support this ministry with generous donations of money, supplemental food, untold hours of volunteer time and immeasurable amounts of energy expended in loading, unloading and packing pounds and pounds of food. You can help by volunteering in the food pantry each week, donating food or household items like toilet paper and diapers and financially through the Church Office.

If you are in need, contact the Church Office or stop by when the Food Pantry is open.

NON-DISCRIMINATION STATEMENT

In accordance with federal civil rights law and U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) civil rights regulations and policies, this institution is prohibited from discriminating on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, age, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity.

Program information may be made available in languages other than English. Persons with disabilities who require alternative means of communication to obtain program information (e.g., Braille, large print, audiotape, American Sign Language), should contact the responsible state or local agency that administers the program or USDA’s TARGET Center at (202) 720-2600 (voice and TTY) or contact USDA through the Federal Relay Service at (800) 877-8339.

FILING A DISCRIMINATION COMPLAINT

To file a program discrimination complaint, a Complainant should complete a Form AD-3027, USDA Program Discrimination Complaint Form which can be obtained online at: https://www.usda.gov/sites/default/files/documents/ad-3027.pdf, from any USDA office, by calling (866) 632-9992, or by writing a letter addressed to USDA. The letter must contain the complainant’s name, address, telephone number, and a written description of the alleged discriminatory action in sufficient detail to inform the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights (ASCR) about the nature and date of an alleged civil rights violation. The completed AD-3027 form or letter must be submitted to USDA by:

Mail:
U.S. Department of Agriculture

SW Washington, D.C. 20250-9410

or

Together, we’re improving our communities

The Council of Community Services envisions a future in which all people have equitable pathways to health, housing, and resources.

OUR SERVICES
87k
individuals served through health, housing, and information and referral services across the Commonwealth.
$1M
raised for the community through GIVE Roanoke, the community's local day of giving.
70%
of housing services clients achieved successful housing outcomes, securing hope and stability.
We're dedicated to improving health, ending homelessness, and increasing access to resources.

By assessing the needs of our community, we can develop effective plans to improve available programs and resources, as well as establish new ones to benefit those in need.
ABOUT US

Helping our community with four needs-driven service areas

With focuses on housing, health programs, information and referral services, and capacity building services, our programs help provide equitable access to health and human services.

Housing

For many in Frederick County our pets are family.  No one is immune to hardship, and for those families facing crisis, the Frederick County Humane Society is here to help their companions.  

Frederick County Humane Society is not a shelter, we do not do adoptions.  FCHS works with Frederick County residents to provide needed assistance to keep their pets healthy and happy in their home.

Courageous Faith
a series through Joshua

Sundays at 9am & 10:30am In person & Online
11:30am Broadcast on CW Richmond

Join Pastor Josh each week, starting Sunday, January 11, for an incredible journey through the Book of Joshua. God’s people stood at the edge of their promised land, facing fortified cities, giant enemies, and impossible odds. They didn’t need a better strategy, but “Courageous Faith”! Over the coming weeks, we’ll discover how to move forward when paths seem impossible, find grace in unlikely places, take confident steps into God’s will, and experience victory through His power rather than our own. This isn’t just ancient history – it’s YOUR story. God is calling you to move beyond your comfort zone and overcome your obstacles through His power.

Joshua 1:9 says, “Be strong and courageous! Do not be afraid or discouraged. For the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.”

Don’t miss this sermon series! Invite a friend and discover together what it means to live with COURAGEOUS FAITH!

HOME DONATE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOOD PANTRY 
	
St. William of York Outreach is a non-profit organization which provides emergency financial assistance and food to families in Stafford County, VA. We are staffed solely by volunteers who donate their time to operate the Thrift Store. The profits from the thrift store sales and donations are used to provide financial assistance and food to the members of our Stafford County community in the greatest need.

OUR MISSION

So What Else’s mission is to improve the lives of children and families living in underserved communities in Maryland and Washington, DC by providing food security, access to basic necessities, and high-quality out-of-school time programs in literacy, athletics, and the visual and performing arts while inspiring young people to give back to the community by creating volunteer opportunities for citizens of all ages.

Approximately

125 Million

meals provided since March 2020

	

Over

3,150

students provided with free out of school time programming annually

	

More than

30,000

volunteer hours hosted every year

Help us provide emergency food support and free out-of-school time programming for thousands of children and families in Maryland and Washington, DC.

The Eastport Community Food Pantry

Our food pantry distributions will be at 11 am on the third Saturday of each month:

 

Remaining 2025 Dates:  20, October 25, November 15, December 20.

Eastport United Methodist hosts the community food pantry outside in the parking lot on the third Saturday of each month at 11 am.

 

Supplies are distributed on a "first-come first-served" basis as long as supplies last. Volunteers should arrive around 10 am.

Our food pantry distributions providing much needed staple foods, frozen meats, and health and hygiene items are a huge success and a lot of fun.

 

We have so many food donations from the Anne Arundel County Food Bank as well as donations from friends and neighbors. GROW Annapolis brings the freshest produce from their community gardens.

​

We have several valuable community partnerships that have come together to provide relief to our neighbors and have been instrumental in donating money, all kinds of food, and other essentials for the food pantry.

​

Interested in volunteering? One does not have to be a member of the church to join in the fun. This is open to all. Join us at 10 am to help set up and we usually wrap up around 12:30 pm. We are grateful for the 50+ volunteers from all over the county who make this happen!

Want to Contribute?

The most efficient way for us to collect food donations is through the Anne Arundel County Food Bank, however, if you wish to contribute financially to the community food pantry, you can give online at eastportumc.org/give

Give to the Food Pantry
Wondering what food pantry day is like?

Here are a number of videos celebrating the work that we do at the food pantry. While some of them are dated, they are still representative of the work we do to this day:

Pet Pantries of Talbot County

Right now across the US it is estimated that 30 MILLION pets are food insecure. Our goal is to help those animals living in homes on the midshore struggling to feed themselves and their pets.

Individuals experiencing economic difficulties, especially those with no close family, are particularly in need of their pet’s unconditional love and companionship during difficult times. Our hope, with a little assistance we will be able to keep these pets and their family’s together.

Thanks to the wonderful generosity of the community and donations our pantry remains open.

The process to participate is simple.

Owners must agree not to obtain new pets or breed their pets while in use of the program. 
Owners must agree to abide by all state, county and town laws as they apply to animals.

For new users of the pantry, we ask that you allow 5-10 minutes to register you in the program on your first visit. The Pantry is open during business hours.

Interested in being a donor to the pantry or a business interested in being a drop-off location for food collection?
Contact Patty today at patty@talbothumane.org !

Open Hands of Maryland 
*****Please do not fill out this registration form more than one time. If you have shopped at the trailer you are already registered here. PLEASE DO NOT FILL IT OUT AGAIN.   If you want to update info just let us know when you visit next******


Welcome to our food pantry.  If you are in need of food, please come visit us at the trailer at the back right of SPEP Church property at 110 Ritchie Hwy Pasadena.  This is a neighbor choice pantry of primarily fresh foods, produce, protein & bread.  You pick your own food.


We are affiliated with the Maryland Food Bank and serve residences of all counties in Maryland EXCEPT for PG and Montgomery County.  If you live in either of those counties please go to Capital Area Food Bank's website to find a pantry in your area.  https://www.capitalareafoodbank.org/ 


One shopper per household may come in to shop one time a week.  Unfortunately, we are not able to allow children to accompany you into the trailer. Please bring reusable grocery bags if you have them. 

We are open 
Tuesdays    10:00 am - 10:50 am    by appointment only
Tuesdays    11:00 am - 11:30 am    walk in - no appointment needed
Thursdays    7:00 pm - 7:40 pm       by appointment only 
Fridays          3:00 pm - 3:40 pm       by appointment only
Saturdays     9:00 am - 9:40 am       by appointment only
Saturdays    10:00 am - 11:00 am   walk in - no appointment needed 

Feel free to pick what day works best for you on any given week, but you may only come one time a week.  For your first visit, you must come in during one of our walk in times. Numbered tickets are handed out to those that will be shopping as a walk in during that days appointment time.  Let us know during your first visit if you would like information on how to make appointments in the future.  

Fill out the form below prior to your first visit.  This only needs to be filled out one time.  Click on the "Next" button at the bottom of this message to fill our our registration form.  

Questions?  Reach us at contact@openhandsofmd.org

WE HELP ALL PEOPLE IN THE HAMPTON ROADS COMMUNITY
Jewish Family Service is a premier social service agency, helping families and individuals through life’s transitions since 1946. Our commitment to preserving the well-being of every person, regardless of race, religion, or financial circumstance is upheld by committed and caring staff members, volunteers, and Board of Directors.
JFS STATEMENT OF DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION

The core of JFS’ mission is to help “repair the world through acts of loving kindness” and to do our part in creating a more just and equitable world.

This philosophy is reflected in our practice of service delivery to clients, and the hiring, retention, and promotion of staff. JFS is an equal opportunity employer dedicated to a policy of non‑discrimination. Ensuring that we are always recruiting, retaining, and promoting a diverse mix of colleagues who are representative of the diversity in our local communities enriches us with a broad range of ideas and experiences. This diversity, combined with the values of Jewish culture, traditions and beliefs creates a strong foundation for a more equitable and inclusive world.

OUR IMPACT STORIES
“This year was hard for me. I lost my brother and had to take a pay cut at work that has put me in debt. My therapy with JFS has changed my life. Handling grief is constant, but I know I was able to get through it because of my therapist’s help.”


– Linda
“My mom suffered a terrible fall that required months of rehab. I live out of state and had no idea how to handle getting her to all the rehab appointments she needed. JFS transportation services made sure she was on time to every appointment, and I felt so good knowing someone reliable was making sure she was getting there and getting home.”


– Rachel
“When I was between jobs I couldn’t buy Chanukah gifts for my kids, I called JFS, and they let me make a few requests that my kids asked for. I didn’t know what to expect but was blown away by the gifts they had for my kids. Chanukah was so special, and I am unbelievably thankful that my kids didn’t have to go without this year.”


– Aaron
“I knew I needed help with my mom, but I just didn’t know where to start. I called one of the care managers or Senior Advisors and they helped guide me and gave me some opportunities to explore with them. I know I will continue to use their services.”


– Seth
“I didn’t know where to turn when my husband and I were struggling to pay bills and feed our family. JFS Food Pantry not only provided food for my family that lasted us until my next pay day but also let me maintain my kosher practices with many kosher items to pick from on the pantry shelves! I can’t say thank you enough!” 
– Leah
 
“Thank you from the bottom of my heart for your generosity. My children were able to celebrate Chanukah this year and it couldn’t have happened without JFS. With your help, they received “everything” they wanted. And with that in mind, so did I.”


– Annie, single mother of three
“When my husband had a stroke and was forced to live in a nursing home, the JFS transportation service enabled me to visit him three times a week. Without this service, I would have only been able to see him once in a while as I cannot drive. This service has truly transformed our quality of life in a very challenging time.”


– Zell, 85 years old
“When my father got sick, JFS was able to meet all of his medical needs and provide counseling to our entire family as well. No other provider could have helped us in such a holistic and empathetic way.”


– David
“When I lost my job, JFS was there for me and for my children. Nothing can describe how grateful I am for the help we received. In two years my family went from having what we needed to not knowing where we would live or what we would eat. We were hungry and our clothes were too small…”


– Beth, single mother, unemployed 14 months
“Thank you for always being there for me. The gift cards and the rent assistance during this COVID-19 has made this emotional roller coaster more bearable.”


– Arlene
