ABOUT
SERVICES
NEXT STEPS
MINISTRIES
GIVE
CONNECT

A place where you can belong & grow.

VISIT SUNDAY
SUNDAY SERVICES | 9AM & 11AM

6201 Solomons Island Rd, Huntingtown, MD

DISCOVER LIFE WITH PURPOSE

CONNECT

CONNECT TO GOD

A deep relationship with God doesn’t have to be complicated.

BUILD

BUILD GENUINE RELATIONSHIPS

Make real friendships and experience true community.

GIVE

MAKE A DIFFERENCE

Be part of something bigger than yourself. Give back and serve.

REDISCOVER SUNDAY MORNING

Many people have had church experiences that leave them feeling uninspired at best—and defeated or judged at worst.

We’ve been there too. That’s why every moment of Chesapeake’s worship services is designed to help you encounter God and experience His grace. Our music is energetic and moving, and we teach the Bible simply, clearly, and unapologetically.

Church shouldn’t be boring, confusing, or alienating. In fact, we believe it should be the most hope-fueled hour of your week.

LEARN MORE
A PLACE WHERE YOUR WHOLE FAMILY CAN FIND PURPOSE
KIDS

Peake Kids helps children grow in Christ through developmentally age-appropriate biblical teaching and engaging activities they look forward to each week.

LEARN MORE

YOUTH

We help teens navigate one of the toughest seasons of life through real relationships, relevant teaching, service, and lots of fun.

LEARN MORE

ADULTS

God has a purpose for your life. Getting connected with others through involvement at Chesapeake will help you live it.

LEARN MORE

YOUR QUESTIONS ARE WELCOME HERE
You’re not alone. You belong here, just as you are.
We’ll help your whole family grow. Your kids and teens will never say church is boring.
We’ll walk with you. God created you for a purpose. Discover it and live it out.
We’re human. We’ll mess up from time to time. Together, we’re learning how to better love people and follow Jesus.

WE’RE GLAD YOU ARE HERE

FIND YOUR PLACE AT YOUR OWN PACE
VISIT ON SUNDAY

For most people, the best first step is checking out a Sunday service. You’ll feel warmly welcomed, but we promise not to call you out or embarrass you or do anything awkward.

GET CONNECTED

In our Starting Point class, you’ll meet new people and get connected by serving on one of our teams.

GO ALL IN

As a fully devoted follower of Jesus Christ, you’ll find your purpose, you’ll feel accepted and loved by God, and you’ll have a firm foundation for tackling life’s challenges.

Find your place & purpose.
VISIT SUNDAY

SUNDAY SERVICES: 9AM & 11AM

HUNTINGTOWN, MD 20639

The Catholic Community of St. Ursula Church and School warmly welcomes anyone who is new to our area or ​anyone who is looking for a spiritual home. 
​
In our mission, we are called to be disciples of Jesus Christ through Eucharistic worship, prayer and service.
It is our vision, that all members of our Catholic Community become intentional disciples of Jesus Christ.  

​We are a community of faith, worship, service, and formation, and with open hearts we invite all our brothers and sisters into a living and saving friendship with the Lord Jesus Christ in the communion of His One, Holy, Catholic,and Apostolic Church.

Please complete our registration form electronically by visiting the Parish Life page HERE, or if you prefer, you may download the REGISTRATION FORM, and leave it in the collection basket, mail it to the office, or bring it by during office hours to introduce yourself! 
What is the OCIA?
OCIA stands for the Order of Christian Initiation of Adults and is the process through which adults join or complete their initiation into the Catholic Church. Please contact Mrs. Bernie Miller at bernadine.miller@archbalt.org for more information!

OUR CORE VALUES

St. Mark’s is a community of believers who express our love for God by welcoming everyone, serving our community, and celebrating our Episcopal worship.

We Love God

St. Mark's is a community of people who believe in the power of God's love as expressed through God's son, Jesus Christ.

We Love Each Other

Our love for God compels us to love and welcome others with open arms – regardless of age, ethnicity, sexual orientation or social status.

We Love Our Community

Our love for God compels us to serve our community by being active in service to those in need.

We Love Our Faith

We celebrate our love for God, for each other and for our community through Episcopal worship that keeps us grounded in our faith.

PROVIDING LIFE-CHANGING RESOURCES
Why We Exist

Many of those we serve may have experienced a crisis that has brought them to a place of financial insecurity, such as the loss of a job or the death of a spouse. Others live in chronic or generational poverty, having never been taught another way of life – away to find that bridge they need to break the cycle. That is why we employ a holistic approach, to empower people to recognize their worth and their potential and live the full lives for which they were created, as shown in our motto: Help. Healing. Hope.

We are most well-known for our help, providing food and clothing for those in need.​ We want to do more by offering an opportunity for healing…  restoring lives by helping people develop new skills and utilize resources to build their own bridge from their present circumstances toward a better lifestyle. In all we do we seek to build personal relationships. We want our neighbors to know that they are loved and that they can find the hope that everyone needs to live a full and purposeful life.

Y FRESH MART: RELIABLE ACCESS TO HEALTHY FOOD
Breadcrumb
Home  About Us  Reliable Access to Healthy Food
NOURISHING OUR COMMUNITY

Healthy living starts with access to healthy food and feeling a part of a caring community where people value, respect and support one another. 

The Y provides healthy food to our community through Y family center community food pantries called Y Fresh Mart. Food is also distributed through various Y Head Start locations and our Y Community School food pantries. 

We are deeply appreciative of the generous partners and funders who support this critical community work: Wellpoint, Maryland Food Bank, McCormick Flavor For Life, Caring Cupboard, Anne Arundel County Food Bank, Quest Diagnostics, Sprouts Farmers Market, University of Maryland Medical Center, Morgan State University and Westminster Rescue Mission. 

Y FRESH MART LOCATIONS AND HOURS

Our Y Fresh Mart locations are OPEN TO ALL—even if you are not a Y member. We simply ask that visits to Y Fresh Mart are limited to once a week. This allows greater access for all.

Y FRESH MART LOCATIONS


The Michelle Becote-Jackson Y Fresh Mart at the Y in Druid Hill
1609 Druid Hill Ave. | Baltimore, MD 21217

Mondays: 10am - 1pm
Tuesdays: 3pm - 6pm
Saturdays: 11am - 2pm




Y Fresh Mart at the Y in Parkville
8910 Waltham Woods Rd. | Parkville, MD 21234

Tuesdays: 4pm - 7pm
Saturdays: 10am - 1pm




Y Fresh Mart at the Y in Pasadena
26 Magothy Beach Rd. | Pasadena, MD 21122

Wednesdays: 3pm - 6pm
Saturdays: 10am - 1pm




Y Fresh Mart at the Hill Y in Westminster

1719 Sykesville Rd | Westminster, MD 21157

Mondays: 3pm - 6pm
Saturdays: 10am - 1pm

Y FRESH MART ITEMS

We primarily carry basic shelf-stable food at the pantry:

canned vegetables
canned fruit
canned entrees
dry goods 

We also try to have a selection of frozen meats and fresh produce. When possible, we offer halal and kosher meats as well, though we cannot guarantee their availability. Some basic hygiene and cleaning products may also be available.

YES! WE ACCEPT DONATIONS

We happily accept food donations and greatly appreciate your kindness. We are always in need of:

• canned proteins (ex. fish, chicken, lamb)
• ready-to-eat meals (ex. Spaghetti O's, Ravioli, soups)
• canned fruit
• breakfast items (ex. cereal, oatmeal, pancake mix)
• peanut butter

We currently have plenty of canned vegetables and beans.

Before donating, please make sure your donations are not expired and are in their original packaging. If you would not serve it to a guest in your home, please do not include it in your donation to Fresh Mart. Thank you for generosity!

VOLUNTEERS ARE WELCOME

We love volunteers! You do not need to be a Y member to volunteer. The minimum age requirement is age 16, however younger volunteers are welcome if they are accompanied by a parent or guardian.

Volunteer tasks may include:

sorting food
organizing pantries
assisting neighbors when Y Fresh Mart is open
reading nutritional labels for dietary restrictions

If you would like to be a Y Fresh Mart volunteer, please click below to get started. Once you find an opportunity that you like, click "register" to create your Y Volunteer Account and sign up. If you already have a volunteer account, just login.

VOLUNTEER AT Y FRESH MART

Note: We recommend using a laptop or desktop computer when signing up to volunteer. While our volunteer website is accessible from a mobile device, the online experience is more user-friendly from a computer.

MORE OPTIONS

If you would like to help give more children and families access to healthy food, please click below.

MAKE A TAX-DEDUCTIBLE GIFT

 

If you would like to find out more about this work, please CONTACT US.

A Small Town Church

Here at BOC, we are committed to embodying the love and generosity of Jesus. We believe that serving our community and those around us is one of the most effective ways to accomplish this mission. That's why we offer a range of services, including free clothing, a food pantry, and showers for homeless members of our community.

We make sure our building is open as often as possible by filling our weeks with as many groups and ministries as possible. Feel free to check our schedule below and stop in for a visit! Whether you are a seasoned church goer or looking to start coming we are so very grateful that you are considering BOC. 

Weekly Schedule:

Monday: Men’s Group at 6pm

Tuesday: Women’s Group (Bi-Weekly) at 6pm

Wednesday: Bible Study & Youth at 6:30pm

Sunday: Service at 10am with Sunday School for all ages to follow

What we do to make a difference in the lives of others. One person at a time…..
How We Help….
Community Food Pantry

The RCDC Community Food Distribution program consists of the food pantry and nutritional education programs geared toward serving low-income individuals and families in need. This program is staffed by a group of dedicated volunteers. Our pantry is the only pantry in the local area that provides fresh produce on a consistent weekly basis. Clients are served from throughout the Maryland, DC and Virginia area and can self-refer based on need.

To schedule an appointment for food, please

Text 240-628-9154 or Call 301-625-2561, Ext 1

Holiday Giving Project

The Holiday Giving Project has been serving Montgomery County families in need for over 30 years, and RCDC has been a partner for 15 years. This program is a collaborative group of agencies working together to assist those unable to purchase the traditional American trimmings during the Thanksgiving and December Holidays. Families are referred to this program through social service agencies and school counselors.

Emergency Safety Net Services

Emergency efforts to secure that basis human need: Shelter

Temporary Homelessness Prevention: Assistance provided to temporarily provide housing for up to one week when The Crisis Center or Shelters are unable to place. Referral Required.

Eviction Prevention: Assistance provided based on social service agency referral and court-ordered eviction

Utility Cut-off Prevention/Reconnection: Assistance provided based on social service agency referral to prevent cut-offs and/or reconnections of utility services

Requests for Rental Assistance or utility payment assistance must come from DHHS or another social service provider.

The Rainbow Community Development Center

2120-A Industrial Parkway

Silver Spring, MD 20904

Office: (301) 625-2561 

For Food Recovery/Corporate Food Donations, please call (240) 639-2293

Neighbors Helping Neighbors
CCAP has over 50 years of experience organizing community-led support and providing access to food, clothing, and financial aid in Winchester and Frederick counties, Virginia.
Give Help
Get Help
What does CCAP do?

Food Assistance

We maintain a high-volume food pantry providing fresh and nutritious proteins, produce, and dairy to ensure food security for families in Winchester and Frederick counties. Check out our neighborhood Freedges, 24/7 accessible, community stocked refrigerators.

Find out more…

Food Assistance Open Hours:

Mon, Wed, Thu: 9 am – 12:30 pm

Friday: 9 am – 11:30 am

Second Saturday of each month: 10 am – 12:30 pm

The Unique Boutique

We operate a donations-based boutique aimed at providing professional attire and job-specific gear like steel-toed boots or slip-resistant shoes. We also have access to bikes to cover transportation needs and stock essential baby care items including diapers and clothing.

Find out more…

Clothing and Gear Boutique Open Hours:

Mon, Tue, Thu: 9 am – 11:30 am

Financial Aid

CCAP may be able to provide financial assistance with rent, utilities, and car repairs. We also offer self‑sufficiency programs designed to help remove barriers to employment and access to other resources.




Find out more…

Financial Aid Open Hours:

By appointment

Donate Now
Read More
“It’s far easier to prevent homelessness, than to fix it”

– Andrea Cosans – CCAP Executive Director

Guided by our faith in God and love of our community, CCAP is committed to helping our neighbors to meet basic needs with food, clothing, and financial resources.

Our neighbors can always count on the compassion and generosity of the community in times of need and will have meaningful opportunities to lift themselves out of difficult life situations.

Fauquier Community Food Bank and Thrift Store

If you need assistance, you have come to the right place. Our food bank provides USDA Commodities, Senior Commodity boxes, pantry services, some personal supplies, and firewood when available to registered clients in Fauquier County. During the 2016-2017 fiscal year our food bank served 7,841 families which consisted of 6,457 children, 10,115 adults and 3,536 seniors.

​

Not only do we provide food, we also have information on the other Social Services in the county and we will help you get connected. You can also learn more about Community Services available in Fauquier County through the Fauquier County Resource Guide.

Here is what you need to know about our Food Bank

USDA food is available at this pantry to Virginia residents and is an equal opportunity foodbank. For USDA food only you do not need to show ID, or proof of residency.


We serve Fauquier County residents only. Our hours are Monday thru Friday from 12 pm until 4 pm for client services. Donations of food are welcome Monday thru Friday from 9 am until 4:00 pm If you live in another county, we can refer you to your local food bank.

You will need to bring the following documents:

Proof of Income

Proof of residency

Photo ID.(ID is needed for all family members)

Proof of Benefits (such as SNAP.. only if your family receives benefits)
NOTE: We serve our clients based on income and your family does not need
to receive public assistance to come to the Food Bank.

You can pick up pantry food twice per month. Bread and produce can be picked up daily when available. A volunteer will assist you in choosing food.

 

We are located at 249 East Shirley Avenue in Warrenton. This is the road that goes past Walmart. We are near Taylor Middle School between Warrenton Auto Services and Highline Motors. If requested, the Circuit Rider bus will stop at our front gate.

 

This institution is an equal opportunity provider!

Donate

We accept donations of food, money, and through our Thrift Store, household goods (furniture, kitchenware, clothing, jewelry, and so on). See our donation hours below at the bottom of the page.

There is a story about a man who left this earth and was taken on a tour of the Inner realms. He was shown a room where he saw a large group of hungry people trying to eat dinner, 
because the spoons that they were trying to eat with were longer than their arms, they remained frustrated. "This" his guide told him, "is hell." "That's terible!" exclaimed the man; 
"Please show me heaven!" "Very well," agreed the guide, and on they went. When they opended Heaven's door, the man was perplexed to see what looked very much like the same scene:
there was a group of people with spoons longer than their arms. As he looked more closely, however, he saw happy faces and full tummies, for there was one important difference:
the people in Heaven had learned to feed each other. Anonymous

Welcome to Church of the Ascension in Lexington Park -- a Rising Church for the Risen Christ.  We're one of two campuses of Resurrection Parish. Our other location is St. George's in Valley Lee, and we partner with All Saints, Oakley Parish in Avenue.  As the Episcopal Church in Lexington Park, we're proud to inclusively proclaim Jesus' good news in worship and witness.



All are always welcome at Ascension, Lexington Park!


The Rev'd Greg Syler

OUR MISSION
Our Mission

One Mission Cambridge seeks to provide vital physical resources to those living in poverty in Dorchester County, while also demonstrating the uplifting power of faith. We believe in the life-changing word of God and wish to provide spiritual guidance to those who may need it. 




Above all, we open our arms to all who require a helping hand. Our mission center located at 614 Race St. includes a food pantry open three days a week, navigational resources and guides, hearty and healthy community meals, temporary daytime shelter during our opening hours, and one-on-one counseling. We offer prayer, fellowship, and spiritual guidance upon request. 




Neighbors helping neighbors. Friends assisting friends. Churches serving together. That is our dream at One Mission Cambridge. 







Over 51,000 pounds of food was distributed through the food pantry in 2025.

Local Health Fair - April 11th

Our food pantry and resource center is open to the public on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays from 1pm to 5pm.

The first Monday evening of every month, we provide a healthy and hearty community dinner. This begins promptly at 4pm in the event space located at the back of our building.

CityBeat Dream Center
Changing The Heartbeat Of Baltimore And Beyond

CityBeatDream Center aims to change the heartbeat of Baltimore by loving, restoring, and empowering communities through outreach & residential programs.




Inspired by Pastor Matthew Barnett’s heart to "find a hurt and heal it, find a need and fill it," Pastor Jared Michael began to imagine a similar organization right here in Baltimore - a beacon of light that would help bring hope to the people of Baltimore.

Donate
Say Hello To The Dream Center Team
Dream Center Staff

This team of Pastors & Leaders is committed to changing the heartbeat of Baltimore City.

Founder
Jared Michael




Executive Director of the Dream Center
Yvonne Rodriguez




Campus Pastor
Alec Underwood




Dream Kids Pastor
Kelly Underwood




Bread of Life Director
Steve Kahn




The Core Three
Loving

We first prioritize love to every person we encounter. The love of Jesus can be shown in may ways and we will continue to be his hands and feet as we love each person we serve!

Restoring

We believe Jesus loves people too much to leave them as they are. So it is one of our core convictions to provide resources to people so they can find freedom in Jesus!

Empowering

We understand the greatest privilege we have is seeing people become all God intended them to be. It is our job not to have people rely fully on us but to help them build a strong foundation in Christ so they can be released into their destiny!

Outreach Programs

Our outreach programs exist to build consistency and personal relationships with individuals in our city. Our hope is to first and foremost be a friend, and then to help meet any tangible or spiritual needs.

WHO DO WE SERVE?

We serve the amazing people of the New River Valley in their time of need. The New River Valley includes the counties of Giles, Floyd, Montgomery, Pulaski, and the City of Radford.

 

We do not require an appointment for first time visitors. If you plan to come during our days and hours of operation (Wednesdays and Fridays 12N-4PM), our volunteers are trained and ready to offer a helping hand to anyone in need.

 

HOURS OF OPERATIONS:

First through Fourth weeks of a month: Wednesdays and Fridays from 12N-4PM
Third Saturday of each month from 9AM-12N

Closed Fifth Wednesday and/or Friday of a month, if applicable.

​Call 540-251-5646 for an appointment

​​

ANOTHER BRICK IN THE WALK

Helping People, Meeting Needs and Building Community

​

Order your brick ONLINE

​

Give Now
THE AGAPE CENTER NRV
WHAT IS THE AGAPE CENTER NRV?

We are a multi-church, mentorship-based outreach center focused on serving people in the New River Valley when they need a hand up. We are a God-directed ministry with the goal of attending to the felt needs of individuals and families, while also addressing their spiritual needs in a manner that gives God the glory.

GET INVOLVED

God is doing some amazing things through the Agape Center NRV! This is a community effort and we need all the supportive hands and feet we can get. There is a place at the Agape table for you.

VOLUNTEER

From sorting food, to data entry, to praying with clients, we have a place for you to serve.

BECOME A PARTNER CHURCH

We rely on our partnerships with local churches to do all that we do through prayer, volunteer, and financial support.

BECOME A PARTNER BUSINESS

We are actively growing our Partner Businesses Program. Join us and help make a difference in our community.

DONATE

Become a financial partner and help us continue to meet the needs in our valley.

CONTACT US

We are now at our permanent location. You can contact us to schedule an appointment during our Hours of Operation.

​​

HOURS OF OPERATION: 

First through Fourth weeks of a month: Wednesdays and Fridays from 12N-4PM
Closed fifth week of a month, if applicable.​

Call 540-251-5646 to schedule an appointment 

Physical Address: 1175 Cambria Street, NE, Christiansburg, VA  24073

Mailing Address: PO Box 1281, Christiansburg 24068

info@agapecenternrv.org

Phone: 540-251-5646

Agape Center NRV is USDA approved and is an Equal Opportunity Provider. For more information, see the links below:

How We Serve Our Community
 

​

FACES supports two distribution locations. Choose the location that's right for you: 

 

Farmville FACES at 482 Commerce Road 

County Line FACES at 12129 County Line Road, Keysville, in the Abilene community.

​

Farmville FACES distributes food through FACES Marketplace on Thursday 4-6 pm,  as well as Saturday morning drive through, 8-10:30 am.

​

County Line FACES distributes food on Saturday morning, with drive through 8-10:30 am.

​

FACES sponsors a Backpack Program providing weekend meals for school children.

​

FACES supplies food for the homeless in partnership with STEPS, Inc.

​

FACES partners with other community organizations for the betterment of our community.

​​​

​

***In accordance with federal civil rights law and U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) civil rights regulations and policies, FACES is prohibited from discriminating on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, age, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity.

​

 

Exciting news! FACES neighbor choice pantry is now open! Neighbors who are registered with FACES now have the option of coming inside to shop for their weekly food. Choices include fresh produce, canned foods, assorted meats and frozen items, personal or household goods, bread, eggs and milk when available. 

​

Shopping is by appointment and appointments are made at the first visit. Come in, register, shop as a walk-in, and receive an appointment for future visits.​

​

Studies show that food pantries have less waste and neighbors are happier when they can select their own food. There is also a health benefit to being able to select foods that are appropriate for specific medical dietary needs. 

​

Marketplace is open Thursday 4-6 pm, at  Farmville FACES - 482 Commerce Road.

Open Doors Food Pantry (ODFP)

On the third Saturday of every month the Open Doors Food Pantry distributes food to community members in need from 9:00am - 10am . Our distribution is drive-thru style with volunteers loading the trunks of the cars as they pass through the back parking lot of the church.

Each month we provide a full cart of groceries (an assortment of canned and boxed food supplemented with fresh produce or frozen meats as available) to more than 100 local families. The ODFP receives food and support from the Maryland and Howard County Food Banks, Mt. Zion UMC, the Columbia Town Center Rotary, the Boy & Girl Scouts, the Clarksville Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, Catholic Charities, Pepsi Corporation, local businesses and individuals. 

Our Facebook page provides updates of delays or closings due to inclement weather or other events, announcements of upcoming distribution dates, and our volunteer sign up link is posted the week before distribution.

Those wishing to volunteer on distribution day must be 16 years of age or older, or 12-15 years old accompanied by an adult or guardian. Please arrive 30 minutes before the start time and sign-in at the volunteer table in the back parking lot. Volunteers must wear closed-toed and closed-heel footwear (no sandals, clogs or flip-flops) and clothing appropriate for the weather as some duties may require working outdoors. Please bring necessary forms for community service hours if desired.

For more information please contact Carol Hill: opendoorsfoodpantry@gmail.com

Winter Homeless Shelter

For the last 25 years (except 2021 due to COVID-19), Mt. Zion UMC has hosted a week-long cold weather shelter, providing overnight accommodations, a hot dinner and breakfast, a bag lunch, and other services for 20 to 30 homeless men. Some years, we have provided the same services for 6 to 10 women. 

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Rod Barr: rodkbarr@gmail.com

Sandwiches for DC Homeless

For almost 30 years, Mt. Zion volunteers have made sandwiches each month for Martha’s Table, which provides a daily meal to more than 1,000 homeless persons in Washington, DC. To date, Mt. Zion has provided Martha’s Table with at least 400,000 meat and cheese sandwiches.

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Rod Barr: rodkbarr@gmail.com

Camp Hope/ Appalachian Service Project (ASP)

Each year (except 2020/2021 due to COVID-19) Mt. Zion has sent a team of 25 youth and adults to Camp Hope in Frostburg, MD, for a week to repair the homes of Western Maryland homeowners. When the 2021 Camp Hope program was again cancelled, a youth/adult team was sent to Wise, VA to repair the homes of low-income residents in Southwest, Virginia. 

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Sarah Blough: sarahblough@comcast.net

Salvation Army Howard County Thrift Shop

Mt. Zion provides volunteers who work on a regular schedule at the Thrift Shop and also provides financial support of its operation.

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Margaret Rose: gcrose@aol.com

Ames Church Support

Mt. Zion has provided support to Ames UMC, an inner-city Baltimore church, through winter clothing drives, provision of school packs and supplies, and funds for emergency repair to the leaking church roof. The Open Doors Food Pantry has supplied Ames with Thanksgiving turkeys and fresh produce. 

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Fred Towner: fdtowner@comcast.net

View the Thanksgiving Basket Distribution Event

Ames Memorial Back to School Bash, August 27, 2022 and August 18, 2024

Volunteers-in-Mission (VIM) Disaster Relief Trips

Mt. Zion teams have made many week-long Volunteers-in-Mission rebuilding trips to North Carolina, Mississippi, New York, Georgia, and Illinois after hurricanes and floods. We completed the construction of a new home in Pass Christian, MS, for a family who lost their home in Hurricane Katrina, for which Mt. Zion raised $55,000 and provided 85 man-weeks of labor. 

For more information or to volunteer, please contact Rod Barr: rodkbarr@gmail.com

UMCOR Disaster Relief

The Mt. Zion missions budget regularly provides support for United Methodist Committee on disaster

Southwest Emergency Services (SWES) is a Christian-based organization in Arbutus, Maryland that provides the following:
Perishable and non-perishable food

Personal care items such as bar soap, shampoo, toothpaste, toothbrushes, feminine products, etc. are welcome.

* To qualify for financial assistance, the individual needs to live in zip 21227 or in 21229 (Baltimore County) on the Arbutus side of Wilkins Ave.  

Alternatively, the individual can belong to one of the member churches that sponsor SWES. See the list here.

Regardless of address, no one requesting food on a one-time basis will be turned away.

Drive Thru Food Distribution 

The Community Crisis Center, Inc. (CCC) is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) human service organization. Our mission is to address hunger and hardship in our community by providing our neighbors with supplemental food and essentials, rebuilding hope and self-reliance. We are open, weather permitting, Mondays 5pm-7pm and Saturdays 9am-11am. We are closed on federal holidays. We will post on our Facebook page about both scheduled and emergency closures.

Our Hours

Mondays: 5pm-7pm
Saturdays: 9am-11am

The Community Crisis Center is closed during severe inclement weather and on Federal Holidays.
Upcoming Closures and Schedule Changes 

Updates will be posted on our Facebook page.

The Community Crisis Center will be closed for food distribution Saturday, April 4, through Monday, April 6, for the holiday weekend.

Reopen: Saturday, April 11, at 9:00 AM 

Thank you for your understanding!

About Immanuel Lutheran Church
Our Mission

We are God's family on a journey of love with Jesus, rich in history and tradition yet open to the Spirit, welcoming all in worship and learning, growing in faith as the Spirit leads us, reaching out with our neighhors to serve those in need, sharing with others God's all-embracing love. 

We invite you to walk with us on this journey.  

Community Outreach
Food Distribution

The Little Food Pantry is a “take what you need, give what you can” outdoor cupboard ministry.




Our Wednesday Food Distribution

provides groceries for families in need every week from 10 a.m. to 11:30 a.m.




We also support Table of Grace which

provides a hot meal to the homeless on

Tuesdays in Westminster.




NOTE:  Food Distribution is cancelled on Wednesday, January 28, 2026.  Our pantry is closed when schools are closed or delayed due to weather.

Stephen Ministry

Stephen Ministers are lay congregation members who have had extensive training to provide one-to-one Christ centered care to those experiencing a difficult time in life. This is a confidential ministry. We are not counselors but rather Christians willing to walk alongside our hurting brothers and sisters dealing with life issues such as the death of a loved one, divorce, job loss, chronic or terminal illness, or broken relationships.




If you need the reassurance of God’s love, and consolation in the fact that someone cares enough about you to share what you are struggling with, take that first step and speak to Chris Crouch (crouchchristine@msn.com)

Community Breakfast

Join us for a hot breakfast buffet on the third Saturday of each month. The menu includes, but is not limited to, pancakes, eggs, bacon, sausage, casseroles, pastries, biscuits, fruit, coffee, and juices.




7 a.m.-11 a.m


$10 for 13+

$6 for children 5-12

Children under 5 FREE

Christian Education

Our Sunday School program is a blend of traditional Sunday School, Children's Church, and Bible Study. Classes meet during the Sunday service from September-May.

Note: The church office is open Monday-Thursday, 8:30 a.m.-12:30 p.m. 




The season of Easter, or Eastertide, is a time of embracing the promise and hope of Resurrection. It is a time of leaning into the stories that matter. Come and gather around the stories of those who wrestled with the Easter event and some of the words of preparation that Jesus gave to help us live the reality of eternity now. It is clear from these stories that Jesus wanted to equip us to thrive in a season of Easter promise. This season, we hope you’ll engage in stories that matter.

Please join us for worship April 12 at 11 a.m. as we look at the story of Jesus' first resurrection appearance to his disciples, found in John 20:19-31, which can be read at the link below.

🌐 This week's scriptures




Our new Sunday school class has a curious name: "Embracing an Adverbial Faith."  The adverbs in question come from the United Methodist vision statement, which calls us to love boldly, serve joyfully, and lead courageously.  

Our first class meets April 12, 9:30-10:30 a.m., with a lesson about how disciples are formed by Jesus Christ.  The scriptures for the lesson, Psalm 119:1-8, Ephesians 4:11-16, and Luke 5:1-11, can be read at the link below.  

Please join us, and please keep Oaklette and its mission of making Gospel of Jesus Christ real for all people through worship, education, and outreach in your prayers.

MISSION
As disciples of Jesus our mission is:
Evangelization: to evangelize ourselves, our families, our parish and local communities, and our world.
Liturgy: to celebrate our faith with joy through vibrant and prayerful worship.
Education: to educate and become educated in the truths of the Gospel and in the formation of conscience.
Service: to reach out in love and service to those in need.
Stewardship: to develop the material, financial and human resources of the Church and to manage them as faithful stewards.

SOCIAL SERVICES

The Social Services Department provides many and varied programs and assistance for families, children, and adults in need. The Department's current Director is James Howard.

A three-member citizen board, The Greene County Social Services Board, is responsible for the direction of the department and its programs. This Board is appointed by the Greene County Board of Supervisors.

In accordance with Federal law and U.S. Department of Agriculture policy, this institution is prohibited from discriminating on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, political beliefs, or disability. (Not all prohibited bases apply to all programs.)

To file a complaint of discrimination, write USDA, Director, Office of Civil Rights, 1400 Independence Avenue, S W, Washington, D.C. 20250-9410 or call 866-632-0002 (voice) or 800-877-8339 (TTY) or 800-845-6136 (Spanish). USDA is an equal opportunity provider and employer.

(No todas las bases de prohibición se aplican a todos los programas.)

Para presentar una demanda de la discriminación, escriba USDA, el Director, la Oficina de Derechos Civiles, 1400 Avenida de Independencia, S W, Washington, D.C. 20250-9410 o llamada 866-632-0002 (voz) o 800-877-8339 (TTY) o 800-845-6136 (español). Our Mission:

Provide nutritious food and a smile to those who need it most.


Meals on Wheels of Germantown
© Copyright 2024. All Rights Reserved


Meals on Wheels of Germantown

Serving the Germantown Community Since 1991


Meals on Wheels of Germantown delivers nutritious, well-balanced, diabetic-friendly meals to elderly, ill and disabled homebound residents of Germantown, Maryland. We also provide contact with trained volunteers who create a valuable link to the community. These services are provided to persons of any age, race, or economic status who are unable to prepare their own meals, per county guidelines. Subsidized meals are available for those unable to afford the cost of their meals. Our meals are provided by Montgomery County Aging & Disability.





We currently deliver boxes of 7 frozen meals (clients may receive one box or two per week) on Tuesdays between 11:30am and 1:30pm. Clients need to be home at that time to receive service.





If you are interested in volunteering as a delivery driver or becoming a client, please call our voicemail at 301-706-0262. Service is subject to availability on the routes. We only serve people living in zip codes 20874 and 20876. We do NOT serve residents of Churchill Senior Living - they have their own program, please contact management there for more information.

Our Sunday Church Service is a Traditional/Contemporary Service that combines prayer, scripture, preaching, and music to honor the Lord. Our church is a place where all can feel welcome and accepted. For more information, please contact Auxiliary Captains Mark and Dorothy Gates at (540) 885-8157

(540) 885-8157
StauntonVA@uss.salvationarmy.org
OUR LEADERSHIP

Salvation Army Programs are led by Salvation Army Officers. These officers are responsible to assess needs, deliver programs, and directing ministries for our local community. From serving food to balancing the books, they function as Executive Directors and Pastors for the Staunton Corps.

Office Hours

Monday – Friday
9 AM – 4 PM
(Closed for lunch 12 - 1 PM)

For Donation Pickup Call 540-886-9830
Tuesday – Saturday
9 AM - 4 PM

On Saturdays or Sundays, please drop donations in the red donation bin. All donations Monday-Friday must be dropped off at the warehouse, not at the store.

Auxiliary Captains Mark and Dorothy Gates

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

Come and Join Us For Worship & Fellowship!

We meet for in-person worship every Sunday morning at 10:00. We warmly welcome you to join us. We can also send you a link for our virtual service at your request - contact the church administrator for more information.  

We are searching for a full-time lead pastor to join our multigenerational, multi-ethnic congregation. Candidates should be committed to the Reformed tradition and to strong, Biblical preaching and teaching. An M.Div degree or equivalent is preferred. For more information, please visit this link to our church profile. Resumes and questions can be sent to search-team@sscrc.org.

We are a group of people of all ages and varied backgrounds that God graciously invites to worship the Lord. We believe that God lovingly equips us to love and serve the Lord, our neighbors and God's whole creation. We seek to gratefully respond to God's amazing grace by following Jesus into our homes, neighborhoods, communities and the whole world.

Community Ministries

Arcola Towers Bible Study—Bible study for residents at the Arcola Towers Apartment Building, meets Monday morning. For information or questions contact coffeebreak@sscrc.org. 

Food Pantry—We have a monthly community food pantry on the second Tuesday of each month from 3—5pm. For more information see the Food Pantry page of our website.

Youth Ministries:

Nursery—available during the service for infants and toddlers.

Children in Worship—part way through the service our youngest members (ages 3-approx 2nd grade) are dismissed for a special time of worship designed for them.

Sunday School—during the school year.

Adult Ministries:

Interested in Learning About our Church? Contact pastor@sscrc.org. 

Men’s Bible Study—beginning a study on the Heidelberg Catechism. Meets on the third Wednesday evening of the month at 7pm. Questions? contact Russ VanDyke (russvd1027@gmail.com)

Women’s Bible Study—Our women’s ministry is currently meeting weekly (one group meets Monday evenings at 7, and the other group meets Wednesday mornings at 9:45). We are studying the gospel of Luke. For more information visit our webpage Women's Coffee Break Bible Study and/or email us at coffeebreak@sscrc.org.

Food Pantry

The SSCRC Food Pantry aims to reduce food insecurity in an environment that encourages relationships among  participants, volunteers, the congregation, and the broader community.

What we do
Monthly Choice Pantry


Every second Saturday of the month, our volunteers operate a physical Choice Pantry  from 10 am – 12 pm. An array of healthy and nutritious food options are available at no cost to members of the local community. Register using the link below:

 

https://forms.gle/E98cgGJDq8Aw4XUg6

Weekly Mobile Pantry


With the help of over 30 volunteers, our teams cook and assemble 220+ hot meals every Saturday morning. Our team then distributes these meals to in-need citizens on the streets of Pennsylvania Avenue, and Eutaw Street, in downtown Baltimore, Maryland.

 

Partnerships



As of November 2025, we are official Network Partners of the MD Food Bank. This partnership allows for the operations of our monthly choice pantry and further networking opportunities. Reach out as an ambassador of your organization for potential partnerships.

 

 

Our Impact

Since our inception on March 12, 2022, we have achieved these milestones which motivate us to continue our work every single day.

WELCOME TO

HARVEST CHURCH OF GOD

No matter where you are on life’s journey—whether walking in faith or still searching—you are welcome here.

 

At Harvest Church of God, we believe that God’s love reaches every corner of creation. Through Jesus Christ, we are invited into deep, life-giving fellowship with Him. In a world that often feels chaotic, uncertain, and disconnected from truth, we stand together as a family rooted in grace, grounded in Scripture, and committed to living out the enduring love of God.

 

We are a community of believers who gather not just for worship, but for transformation. Each time we come together, we lift our hearts in praise, open God’s Word with expectation, and allow His Spirit to shape us. These gatherings are more than moments—they are movements of faith, healing, and hope.

 

But our life together doesn’t end at the altar. We serve with compassion, reaching out to our neighbors both near and far with the heart of Christ. We celebrate life through shared meals, joyful events, and meaningful connections. Whether it’s a backyard cookout, a community outreach, or a quiet moment of prayer, we believe God meets us in every act of love.

 

Harvest is a place where faith comes alive, friendships are formed, and purpose is renewed. Our website offers just a glimpse of who we are—but the real story is written in the lives we live together.

 

So come as you are. Bring your questions, your hopes, your story. There’s a place for you at Harvest—and we’re honored to walk this journey with you.

 

Please Join Us For An Upcoming Worship Gathering. There’s A Place For You Here At Harvest Church Of God.

Food Assistance
Are you in need of more food?

Income eligible households who reside in the CEA service area may register to receive supplemental groceries once per month. People who are currently unhoused may register to receive supplemental shelf-stable foods once per week.

Eligibility

Our eligibility requirements to receive supplemental groceries are:

You must live within our service area which is Greater Catonsville. CEA’s boundaries are: Security Boulevard to the north; Baltimore City line on the east; Wilkens Avenue on the South; and the Howard County line on the west. Click here for a live Google Map of the CEA Service Area. If you are currently unhoused, the service area requirement does not apply.
You must be below moderate income based on the Federal guidelines published by the Housing and Urban Development Department. These are: the total household income before any deductions from all sources (including but not limited to gross wages, retirement, disability and any other government or private benefits) must fall in one of the following ranges:

Care Today. Strength Tomorrow.

JSSA is a trusted nonprofit community health and social wellness agency helping our neighbors and their families through their most vulnerable moments.

DONATE NOW
EXPLORE SERVICES 

JSSA focuses on four areas of care: supporting people’s mental health and strengthening family relationships, helping people with disabilities find meaningful jobs, caring for older adults so they can safely age at home, and offering dignity and comfort to hospice patients.

We’ve learned that everyone needs support at some point. Our goal is to make essential care and quality of life accessible to everyone.

WATCH NOW
Our Services
VIEW ALL SERVICES
Featured Service
Hospice
JSSA ensures hospice patients experience comfort, respect, and dignity at the end of life. Our steady presence helps our neighbors and their loved ones experience quality of life even during this difficult moment.
LEARN MORE 

"We appreciate your efforts to support not just your patients, but their families. My mom's case manager has become an important part of our family. Thank you for helping us provide the best care possible for my mom."”

— Cynthia, Daughter of JSSA client

JSSA's 2025 Annual Report

The stories in our 2025 annual report illustrate JSSA’s meaningful outcomes for people in three critical areas: self-sufficiency, well-being, and quality of life. JSSA’s high-quality programs and our integrated approach to care enable us to support the complex, interdependent needs of clients across their lifespan. With compassion, warmth, and acceptance, we meet our neighbors where they are while addressing the underlying causes of their challenges. We are proud of the solutions JSSA delivers to nurture physical health, mental health, and quality of life.

A Community Care Hub in Southwest Baltimore
OUR MISSION

To build a stronger community where food access, opportunity, mentorship, and essential services work together to help families thrive.

THE NEED

Southwest Baltimore faces challenges including food insecurity, unemployment, housing instability, and limited access to coordinated services.

Many systems are fragmented, making it difficult for families to find the help they need.
Our community needs one place where support is connected and accessible.

In Addition to Food, 
Our Community Needs
OUR SOLUTION

U Empower of Maryland operates a Community Resource Hub that brings food, workforce development, and supportive services together under one roof. By coordinating resources in one place, we reduce barriers and help individuals and families build long-term stability.

OUR APPROACH

Immediate support + job pathways + strong partnerships
= building a healthier, more stable community.

OUR IMPACT

Serving Southwest Baltimore since 2014.

Providing food.
Connecting resources.
Creating opportunities.
Developing future leaders.

WOMEN'S BOOK STUDY

FRIDAY, APRIL 10, 6:30-8:30PM

Join us as we discuss In His Image by Jen Wilkin. By exploring ten characteristics of who God is—holy, loving, just, good, merciful, gracious, faithful, patient, truthful, and wise—this book helps us understand who God intends for us to be. Through Christ, the perfect reflection of the image of God, we will discover how God’s own attributes impact how we live, leading to freedom and purpose as we follow his will and are conformed to his image.

We’re Here for You

We recognize that everyone’s situation and needs are different. Our twelve programs allow us to meet individual client’s needs. GoochlandCares delivers quality, client-centered services with compassion and dignity.

Services
About Us

GoochlandCares has been a safety net for our community since 1952. Our mission is to provide basic human services and health care to our Goochland neighbors in need. GoochlandCares envisions an equitable community in which all persons have their basic needs met, empowering them to lead lives of self-dignity and well-being.

Learn more
GoochlandCares is Growing!

To continue meeting the needs of our neighbors, GoochlandCares has purchased the property next door to our current building.

Learn more
Get Involved

Make a difference in your neighbors’ lives by donating your time, talents, or services. Learn more about our volunteer opportunities and how to apply today.

Volunteer
Your Gift Matters

Join us in serving our neighbors whose lives are impacted by poverty. Your financial support truly makes a difference. We have supporters that make one-time donations or annual pledges. Learn more about the different ways you can donate today

Donate

I shop at the Food Pantry every week and I use my credits at the Clothes Closet to get household items and clothes. For the first time in my life, I get regular dental care. Last year, Financial Assistance paid a utility bill. I would not have been able to keep the electricity on that month. Coming here is like coming to see friends.
When I think about GoochlandCares, it puts a smile on my face.”

Join Us for Worship
Sundays @ 10am
Visit our YouTube channel to join us via Live Stream, or to watch previous services.  
We are a people working to live out grace, peace, and love in our community as we share God’s kingdom.

We’re a church family rooted in the Anabaptist and Pietistic traditions. We have a passion for our community and world.

There is a place for you.

Whether you connect with God most through traditional music, contemporary music, times of prayer, times of Scripture reading, teaching, or anything in between, there is a place for you here at BCOB.

Ready to take the next step? 

One of the primary ways visitors connect with us is through attending one of our Sunday morning worship times. We welcome individuals and families to join us for worship as a way to connect with God, discover where he is already at work, and be encouraged to join in that work in our local community.

Learn More
Single, married, kids, no-kids, whatever your situation in life, we hope you’ll check out our family of faith here at Brownsville.

How you Can Help Elkton Area United Services
Food Pantry

Food Pantry helps area families stretch their food dollars.  Eligibility for this program is based on federal mandated income guidelines.  Food Pantry patrons must sign up with Client Services before receiving a food box.  

VOLUNTEERS NEEDED: Information 298-8685 #5 or volunteers@elktonatheart.org

The Pantry is currently open every Tuesday from 12:00 PM to 1:45 PM and Friday’s 12:00 PM to 1:45 PM.   Clients are asked to park and a volunteer will come to you.    Stay Safe

In accordance with federal civil rights law and U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA)
civil rights regulations and policies, this institution is prohibited from discriminating on
the basis of race, color, national origin, sex (including gender identity and sexual
orientation), disability, age, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity.
Program information may be made available in languages other than English. Persons
with disabilities who require alternative means of communication to obtain program
information (e.g., Braille, large print, audiotape, American Sign Language), should
contact the responsible state or local agency that administers the program or USDA’s
TARGET Center at (202) 720-2600 (voice and TTY) or contact USDA through the
Federal Relay Service at (800) 877-8339.

To file a program discrimination complaint, a Complainant should complete a Form AD-
3027, USDA Program Discrimination Complaint Form which can be obtained online

at: https://www.usda.gov/sites/default/files/documents/USDA-OASCR%20P-Complaint-
Form-0508-0002-508-11-28-17Fax2Mail.pdf, from any USDA office, by calling (866)

632-9992, or by writing a letter addressed to USDA. The letter must contain the
complainant’s name, address, telephone number, and a written description of the
alleged discriminatory action in sufficient detail to inform the Assistant Secretary for Civil
Rights (ASCR) about the nature and date of an alleged civil rights violation. The
completed AD-3027 form or letter must be submitted to USDA by:
1. mail:
U.S. Department of Agriculture
Office of the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights
1400 Independence Avenue, SW
Washington, D.C. 20250-9410; or
2. fax:
(833) 256-1665 or (202) 690-7442; or
3. email:
Program.Intake@usda.gov

Providing vital services and a pathway to greater stability.

Interfaith Works (IW) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that provides emergency shelter, supportive housing, essential needs, and employment programs to over 35,000 residents of Montgomery County, MD, every year.

While Montgomery County, a part of the Washington, DC, metropolitan area, is considered affluent, more than 72,000 residents live on incomes below the Federal poverty line.

A Distinct Identity

The Rachell L. Gray Community Foundation knows that our strength lies not only in the words we stand by, but most importantly through the actions of our initiatives. Our Nonprofit was launched in December 2017 and named because of the community service of Pastor Rachell Gray. We are excited about the work we do and live by our motto "Community Focused, People Driven".

Recent Events
in North Brentwood

Mayor Robinson with Diana Fennell, MD State Delegate, District 47A, PG County. Delegate Fennell shared this video from North Brentwood’s ‘Color, Art Uncorked.’

​

The gathering is part of the Town’s Color-me-Fridays event series (6-8 pm), each Friday in September 2025 at the historic Sis’s Tavern.

​

For a list of Upcoming Events, go to our Events List page. 

2026 Black History Month in North Brentwood

The Town’s BHM Kickoff event, “Echoes of the Motherland,” on February 7, showcased a drama about Harriet Tubman, Frederick Douglass and Langston Hughes presented by Oxon Hill High School. The annual Billion Pool Tournament took place on February 14.

​

The final BHM month event, on February 28, was a time of reflection (e.g.,honoring the late Rev Jesse Jackson), and celebration of 100 years of BHM. Attendees included event co-host Wanika Fisher, Mayor Petrella, and more. Entertainers included Maestro the Cellist.

In the News

PGCTV Interview with Mayor Robinson

 

"They were jammin'..." says Mayor Petrella Robinson, talking about Black headliners who played Sis's Tavern years ago.

​

See Prince George County TV's March 3 "Bridging Black Entertainment & Community" story and interview with Mayor Robinson, about the renovation of historic Sis's Tavern and keeping the history of Black culture alive in North Brentwood.​

PGCTV Interview: Mayor Robinson & Town Council Member Jacqueline Morales

 

On Tuesday, March 3, 2026, Mayor Petrella Robinson and Councilwoman Morales were profiled and interviewed by Prince George County TV - about the legacy of women in leadership, in North Brentwood and beyond. The clip is 18:38 (18 mins, 38 seconds) into the full broadcast.

Mayor Robinson on the Radio

Mayor Petrella Robinson was profiled on the radio station 95.5 WHUR for Women's History Month in March 2024.

​

(On the left is a photo of the Mayor speaking at the Town's Memorial Day ceremony in 2022.)

​

Listen to the account of her career and tenure as North Brentwood's Mayor since 2007.

Women's History Mayor Petrella Robinson

SERVE MOBILE PRODUCE PANTRY

SERVE is proud to partner with Mary Washington Healthcare to host a Mobile Produce Pantry, providing free fresh produce to neighbors experiencing food insecurity. This initiative helps address the social determinants of health by improving access to nutritious food within our community.

The Mobile Produce Pantry currently operates on the 2nd and 4th Thursdays of each month at Stafford Hospital, located in the Stafford Medical Pavilion parking lot near the corner of Hospital Center Boulevard and Care Lane.

Additional locations will be added in the near future. More details will be shared soon.

    

OUR MISSION
Serving Children & Families since 1979

Mission Statement:
With the support of our community partners, SERVE’s mission is to strategically address food insecurity, provide emergency relief, and expand programs that bring HOPE and UPLIFT our neighbors.

SERVE is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization serving the communities of Stafford, Spotsylvania, Caroline, and King George Counties, portions of Prince William County, and the City of Fredericksburg.

Vision Statement:
To engage in activities that promote the general health and well-being of our community.

There are many ways to give back to the community with SERVE

Ways to Give
DONATE

We are able to meet our mission because of the generosity of our friends and neighbors. Support the emergency needs of our neighbors in crisis. ​

View Donations
VOLUNTEER

Help in our food pantry warehouse, donate non-perishable dry goods for the food pantry, help plant or maintain our community garden.

SERVE Volunteer
FOOD IS MEDICINE

Learn more about our monthly Health and Wellness events.

Food is Medicine
OUR PROMISE

Our promise is to the entire Stafford County community. We promise to help our neighbors in emergency need of food pantry assistance and financial assistance . This means we will work hard to raise awareness of the need in Stafford County and to gain support from and for our communities less fortunate. These efforts will encompass a great multitude of actions, all of which we will need help with! We are able to provide a hand up for our neighbors in emergency need because of your support of SERVE.

Much of this support comes through individuals as well as through organizations and agencies like the Stafford County Government, the Rappahannock United Way and the combined federal campaign, the Fredericksburg Regional Food Bank,  The Community Foundation including The Honeywell Charitable Fund and a variety of other partnerships with businesses, churches and other organizations! We  and our guests are very grateful for the support and blessings we receive.

Learn More About SERVE
NON-DISCRIMINATION POLICY

In accordance with Federal Civil Rights Law, SERVE, its’ employees and volunteers are prohibited from discriminating based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity (including gender expression), sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, family/parental status, income derived from a public assistance program, political beliefs, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity, in any program or activity conducted or funded by SERVE. Any discrimination related complaint may be filed with either the Office of the Attorney General CivilRights@oag.state.va.us. or through SERVE’s Compliance Officer, Scott Uchytil,   scott.uchytil@serve-helps.org

SERVE is an equal opportunity provider and employer.

WHO IS ADOPT-A-BLOCK?

Adopt-A-Block began as a compassionate outreach ministry of Emmanuel Wesleyan Church. Food insecurity proved to be a challenge for so many families in Salisbury, that it served as an entry point for serving our community. Starting with a handful of volunteers and a pickup truck, Adopt-A-Block set out to provide food to families at no cost or obligation. Over the years, the outreach mission grew and not only were families able to receive food, but they were also able to begin the process of having other needs met through prayer, generous donations, and referrals to other social and community agencies.

Adopt-A-Block’s primary means of outreach is through our food pantry located at 818 Benny Street, Salisbury MD 21804. 

Families can schedule appointments through our client choice food pantry, receive food through our periodic open food market days, or receive emergency food boxes (limited supply). Adopt-A-Block does its best to work with our neighbors to improve their quality of life one food box at a time!

Della’s Closet is a 501(c)3 (pending) non-profit organization located at 1603 Market Street in Pocomoke City, Maryland. Our goal is to provide a variety of services to children in foster care and families in need in Worcester, Somerset, and Accomack (VA) Counties, including:

Providing donated or purchased clothing free of charge for young people in need in our community.

 

Offering games, toys, and other gifts for underprivileged and foster kids on birthdays and other holidays.

Distributing personalized luggage for children in foster care to help give dignity and assistance to those forced to move to new homes.

 

Providing “first-night bags” containing food, clothing, personal items, and other necessities to children moving to new foster homes.

Donating office space to other local charitable organizations, assisting them in their ability to help the community.

 

Offering free meeting space to local non-profit organizations, aiding in their ability to help the youth in our community.

We are currently accepting donations of new or gently used clothing for children and young people of all ages. Our gift drive will be a year-round effort, and we will gladly take donations of new toys and games suitable for children anytime. In addition, we accept monetary and in-kind donations from individuals and community organizations.

Please make checks payable to:

 

Della’s Closet

P.O. Box 268

Pocomoke, MD 21851

 

To schedule a drop-off at our Pocomoke location, please call or e-mail:

 

Joshua Nordstrom

(443) 389-0220

info@dellascloset.org

 

Thank you for your generosity!

LIVING FAITH LUTHERAN CHURCH
Who we are

At Living Faith Lutheran Church, our mission is to live our faith in Jesus through joyful service and love for our neighbors.

To serve this mission, we:

share our faith through meaningful worship

offer a welcoming environment for all people

support those who choose to take a risk for Christ

encourage spiritual growth

use our gifts to serve the community

Living Faith Lutheran Church celebrates diversity and welcomes everyone seeking God’s love and grace to participate fully. Whatever your faith background, no matter whom you love, whatever your culture, ethnicity, economic reality, family structure, age, education, or ability…YOU are welcome. 

To learn more about our ministry and ways to get involved at Living Faith, check out our Ministries Page.

When we worship
Join us at 10:30 am on Sundays!

Each week we gather, sing, pray, engage with scripture, and share in Holy Communion. We believe Communion is a gift of God, for the people of God, and everyone is welcome at God’s table. If you are not able to join us in person, all our worship services are streamed on youtube. You can find the link on our Worship Page.

What to expect
What should I wear?

Come as you are! At our services you'll find everything from jeans and flip-flops to suits and ties.

What about kids?

Bring the kids! We welcome young families, nursing mothers, and children of all ages in all our services. There is also a child-friendly area with quiet toys and activities available in the sanctuary.

What is worship like?

As Lutherans, we practice a standard liturgy including confession and forgiveness, Bible readings, music, a short sermon, and communion at every service. Our worship is accompanied by our organist and guest musicians.

Volunteer at Paul’s Place
PAUL’S PLACE IS LEADING CHANGE

Dear friends,

Paul’s Place is a lifeline for our neighbors navigating challenging barriers to food, shelter, medical care, benefits, and more.

Our mission is to improve the quality of life in Southwest Baltimore.  To fulfill that mission, Paul’s Place has remained a steady presence that Southwest Baltimore residents know they can rely on day after day. Your support makes stories of transformation and hope possible!

Mark Mittelman, Executive Director

OUR IMPACT

We welcome approximately 300 people to Paul’s Place each weekday.  A variety of programs and services are offered including laundry and showers, hot lunch, case management, access to our peer recovery advocate, the computer lab (for job searching), our culinary training program, and our after-school programs at area elementary schools.  

In addition, we offer our free marketplace three days a week, nursing services two days a week, and an evening meal on Thursdays.

Paul’s Place relies on volunteers to offer a wide variety of high-quality programs. Volunteers assist with many of our programs including Hot Lunch, Market Place, Dinner Service, and Food Preparation efforts. Volunteers also provide essential support through organizing clothing, toiletry and food drives. 

Who can volunteer? – Paul’s Place can accommodate children as young as 10 and groups up to 15 people. Pre-registration required.
How do I sign up? – Please register to volunteer by visiting our Get Involved page or go directly to paulsplace.rosterfy.com. 
FEATURED PROGRAM

Learn more about Paul’s Place

ABOUT US

Paul’s Place is a nonprofit, 501(c)3 organization whose mission is to improve the quality of life in Southwest Baltimore. Paul’s Place has been a steadfast source of support for residents of the Southwest Baltimore communities for over 40 years – starting as a two-day-a-week soup kitchen and becoming a one-stop destination with a wide array of resources for hundreds of individuals every day. Paul’s Place now offers more than 20 programs to help guests along a path to stability. Paul’s Place provides programs, services, and support that strengthen individuals and families, fostering hope, personal dignity and growth.

Arlington Corps
Arlington Corps

In Arlington County, Virginia, where over 80% of residents seeking help are from the Hispanic community, breaking the cycle of poverty begins with speaking the same language.

The fluent Spanish speakers in the Arlington Corps offer support to struggling families from more than ten Spanish-speaking countries. Services range from eviction prevention and utility assistance to grocery vouchers and back-to-school supplies. The success of this fellowship is reflected in the numbers that turn out for summer carnivals and holiday dinners. For these families, this really is home away from home.

For the younger population, there's a youth program that meets every Friday night that encourages kids to take a lifelong journey with God, through service projects, bible-based studies, arts and crafts, sports and more.

Community Programs

Social Services: Monday through Friday from 9am-4pm (12pm-1pm closed for lunch)
Food Distribution: 1st and 2nd Thursday of the month from 9:30am-11:30am and 1:30pm-3:30pm (while supplies last) 
J.A.M. (Jesus and Me) Youth Program for kids K-12: Fridays at 7:00pm
Ties with my Baby, an educational program for Moms: 3rd Thursday of the month (Registration is required)

Spiritual Care and Worship

Worship Service on Sundays at 11:00am
Sunday School on Sundays at 10:00am
Bible Study on Fridays at 7:00pm
Women's Group on Fridays at 7:00pm

Seasonal Assistance

Summer Camp Registration (begins at the end of April)
Back To School Supplies
Thanksgiving Assistance
Christmas Assistance (applications taken in the beginning of September)

 

For ongoing updates about events at the Arlington Corps, please check out our Facebook page: The Salvation Army Arlington Corps | Arlington VA | Facebook

 

Contact Information 

Arlington Corps
518 South Glebe Road
Arlington, VA 22204
(703) 979-3380

Corps Officers

Major Alvaro Porras
Major Danitza Porras

 
Cuerpo de Arlington

En el condado de Arlington, Virginia, donde más del 80% de los residentes que buscan ayuda proceden de la comunidad hispana, romper el círculo de la pobreza empieza por hablar el mismo idioma.

Los empleados hispanohablantes de Arlington Corps ofrecen ayuda a familias con dificultades de más de diez países hispanohablantes. Los servicios van desde la prevención de desalojos y la asistencia para el pago de servicios públicos hasta vales de comida y material para la vuelta al colegio. El éxito de este ministerio se refleja en el número de personas que acuden a los carnavales de verano y a las cenas navideñas. Para estas familias, éste es realmente su hogar lejos de casa.

Para la población más joven, hay un programa juvenil que se reúne todos los viernes por la noche y que anima a los niños a emprender un viaje de por vida con Dios, a través de proyectos de servicio, estudios bíblicos, manualidades, deportes y mucho más.

Programas Comunitarios

Servicios sociales de lunes a viernes de 9 am a 4 pm (de 12 a 13 h cerrado para el almuerzo)
Distribución de alimentos: 1er y 2do jueves del mes 9:30am - 11:30am y 1:30pm - 3:30pm o hasta agotar existencias.
Lazos con mi Bebe, un programa educacional para Mamás el 3er jueves cada mes [ Se requiere registración].
J.A.M. (Jesús y Yo) Programa Juvenil para niños K - 12 todos los viernes a las 7:00pm 

Cuidado Espiritual y Adoración

Escuela Dominical los domingos a las 10:00 am
Servicio de Adoración los domingos a las 11am
Grupo de mujeres los viernes a las 7 pm.

Asistencia Estacional

Inscripción en el campamento de verano [ Comienza a finales de abril]
Material escolar
Ayuda de Acción de Gracias
Asistencia de Navidad (Las solicitudes se tomarán a principios de septiembre)

 Para actualizaciones continuas sobre nuestros eventos en el Cuerpo de Arlington - por favor visite nuestra página de Facebook -

The Salvation Army Arlington Corps | Arlington VA | Facebook

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

Who are we?

We are a loving church family who follows the teachings of Martin Luther as expressed in Ephesians 2:8 “For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith – and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God.“

What are we?

We are a Missouri Synod congregation, established in 1931, of approximately 150 communicant members.

Where are we?

We are located in Salisbury, MD on the corner of Camden Ave. and South Blvd.

Why do we exist?

We exist, as a church body in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to learn of Him through His Holy Scriptures, to worship Him only, and to serve Him by meeting the needs of others and by sharing the gift of salvation through the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

We are a member church of the Lutheran Church Missouri Synod. Click on cross icon to visit L.C.M.S. website.

Serving Seniors in Need

STM recently shared fresh produce with seniors in need at the Muryang Buddhist Temple and Global Mission Church. The silent struggle of food insecurity among middle-class seniors is a significant issue that often goes unnoticed. Despite owning homes, receiving pensions, or living in comfortable neighborhoods, many seniors face  challenges in affording or accessing nutritious food. This problem is exacerbated by fixed incomes that donot keep pace with rising living costs, mobility challenges, and the fear of burdening family members. need. Our thanks to Judge Chung Pak and Alanna Lee for coordinating these donation efforts.

Food Insecurity Stat from our friends at Takoma Park Meals on Wheels

7 million older Americans faced food insecurity in 2022
12.8% of U.S. households experienced food insecurity in 2022
13.7% of U.S. households were food insecure at some time during 2024
86.3% of U.S. households were food secure throughout 2024
18.4% of households with children under age 18 were food insecure in 2024

6,459 acts of kindness inspired by Tommy

A huge thanks to everyone who joined us in honoring Tommy Raskin at Kindness Day 2026 with 6,459 acts of kindness inspired by Tommy. The day was filled with making and sharing items including friendship bracelets, toiletry kits, snack bags, cups of cheer blankets, clothing, to benefit those in need.  Our thanks to those of you who are coming out to volunteer with us, to all the nonprofits, groups and individuals who hosted tables (Linus Project, Homeshare, TPSS CSN, MoCo IRC, Blair Unidas, For Such A Time As This, Bombas, Sudanos, Takoma Art Library, PBES PTA, Olive Lounge, Rachel Gold, Kathleen Nixon, Difference Makers Bakers,Nonprofit Wellness, Miller Coaching Services, Seven Oaks Evanswood Citizens Association (SOECA), John Salmen, John F. Kennedy High School Leadership Training Institute (LTI), Not Child’s Play/Risa Shaw, STM at Richard Montgomery High School, I Support the Girls, Let’s Play America, STM at Walter Johnson High School, Girl Scout Troop 34054, Personal Care Foundation, Kindness corner at Montgomery Blair High School, STM at Bethesda-Chevy Chase High School, Crochet Club for Teens, ProjectKnitwell, Girl Scout Troop 31456, Girl Scout Troop 34262, Marcelle Fozzard, Sophie Holt, and Lindsey Archuletta) and to all the businesses that held instore kindness events including the TPSS Coop, Old Takoma Ace Hardware, Tabletop, and Takoma Bev Co!

Senior Food Distribution

Delivering food to our seniors on the last Thursday of each month.


LEARN MORE

How can you help?

There are many ways in which you can participate in this ministry.  We need packers, sorters, runners and drivers.
We accept donations of canned goods, paper products, health & beauty aid, diapers, formula on an on going basis.  Simply drop off your canned meats, canned vegetables, canned fruit, and soups at the collection barrels INSIDE McComas and Armstrong entrances.   Our homeless would especially enjoy single serving, ready to eat foods in E-Z open or pop-top containers as they do not have access to can openers or cooking facilities.

Community Impact

Tri-County Community Action Agency (TCCAA) is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to assisting and representing the interests of low-income residents of Halifax, Mecklenburg, and Charlotte counties in Southside Virginia. TCCAA has continuously administered Federal, State and Local Programs in Halifax County, VA since 1967.

Chase City, VA | Grand Opening | Take A Tour
Transformation Station
Strategic Initiatives

Our strategic initiatives help to empower people and strengthen communities to succeed during times of change by providing resources, education and advocacy through a variety of programs we administer.  Our clients are found in a variety of settings in Halifax, Mecklenburg, and Charlotte Counties in Southside Virginia. What unites them is a burning desire to adaptively transform their community by achieving success for themselves and their families in the face of increasing challenges.

Expand Programming for Children
Disrupt the cycle of poverty by expanding programs for children in order to increase self sufficiency for families
Increase Workforce Development Opportunities
Increase workforce development opportunities for individuals throughout the region
Increase Awareness of Needs
Become the voice of those living in poverty throughout the region by increasing awareness of needs
Advocate for Affordable Housing

We Deliver Independence

For aging, homebound and disabled adults throughout Central Maryland, we do more than deliver nutritious meals. We provide support and safety-net services that help sustain health, promote independence, and improve overall quality of life.

Get Involved
Apply For Meals
Mission Video

Serving Central Maryland Since 1960
Areas We Serve

Our Holistic Approach to Health and Well-Being

At Meals on Wheels of Central Maryland, we believe in more than just delivering a meal; we offer a holistic approach to enhancing the health and well-being to aging adults. Our “More than a meal℠” service is designed to provide not only wellness, safety and security but also companionship, support, and independence for seniors in Central Maryland.

About Us
How We Serve

By providing nutritious meals to people who are homebound and unable to shop or cook for themselves – because of age, disability or illness

More
Ways to Give

Support daily meal delivery and safety checks of homebound individuals to ensure their health, independence and well-being.

More
Volunteer

Help empower aging adults to maintain their independence at home. Your service enables aging adults to remain safely and independently with dignity.

More
Meals Connect

Loneliness can be as detrimental to health as poor nutrition. Our dedicated volunteers and staff provide friendly visits and meaningful interactions. We foster connections that combat isolation and enrich the lives of those we serve.

meals delivered a year!

Welcome to the Redeemed Christian Church of God House of Prayer (HOP). The year 2026 is a year of Open Heavens and mega shift for new songs/beginning fruitfulness, promotion and enlargement of coast.. Isaiah 43:18-19; Jer 30:19; Genesis 1:28;  Isaiah 25:6-8; Job 9:10; Ps 103:7;  Joel 2:7. Brethren, get ready for your new songs and breakthroughs  in Jesus name.  Psalms 126:1-2 says”when the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream….. . Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing: then said they among the heathen, The Lord hath done great things for them. Get ready for a miracle and celebration  this year 2026; and by His grace, you are empowered for overflowing manifestations in Jesus name, amen.
You are blessed. Shalom.

Welcome to Centre

Centre UMC is a passionate community of Christian disciples located in the heart of Forest Hill, MD. Our life together in Christ revolves around four key areas: weekly worship with Holy Communion; regular Bible study in small groups; fervent prayer; and care for our neighbors through our God Provides Food Pantry. We would love to welcome you to worship God with us this Sunday morning.

Our Sunday worship service is at 9:00 a.m. Sunday School meets after worship during the school year. Centre UMC belongs to the West Harford Cooperative Parish.

The West Harford Cooperative Parish

Our sister church is William Watters Memorial UMC, at 1452 W. Jarrettsville Road in Jarrettsville. As a parish our hope is to, “Welcome one another, just as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God!”

A calendar of events is below.

-Online giving: we now offer online giving. Give today by clicking on the green button. And thanks for your contribution!

Who We Are

We are committed to answering God’s call to worship and serve in this place. We offer everyone an opportunity to join us for inspiring worship, insightful preaching, fulfilling companionship, and rewarding service. 

We are part of the Episcopal Church, USA, which is a member of the worldwide Anglican Communion.  We love the heritage and tradition of our Church, which are reflected in our building and liturgy.  The variety of activities that we offer outside of worship show our commitment to help each other grow, in relationships with God, each other and the surrounding neighborhoods.  And we love to eat and have fun together!

We believe that God is at work here.  Come and see!  We welcome guests at worship as well as all of our events, and are always happy to answer questions.
