Eastern Shore Wellness Solutions (ESWS), a non-profit community based organization, creates an environment which unites and strengthens personal and cultural differences, builds comprehensive health knowledge and skills, empowers community members to value and engage in healthier lifestyle behaviors, and improves health outcomes through education in order to establish Health Equity for all populations.

Our vision is to provide health education, training, and skill building to community members, along with reducing and eliminating the social determinants that impact health outcomes in minority communities.

About
In a world where you can be anything…. BE KIND!!
Helping Hands and Caring Hearts is a non-profit based in DC, Maryland, Virginia and West Virginia

Helping Hands and Caring Hearts is an IRS approved 501(c)(3) nonprofit created in 2022 to inspire, strengthen and empower individuals to make their own choices, and not be afraid or intimidated to ask for and get the help they need to be able to have a better life, and to break the systemic cycle.

Helping Hands and Caring Hearts helps everyone. We are not like other nonprofits, we do not focus on one problem. We focus on the need no matter where it is, who it is, or what it is. We can’t help if you don’t ask.

Helping Hands and Caring Hearts is not limited to a certain area. We help everyone and anyone that comes through our door.

We have a fully stocked food pantry at the Monrovia Maryland location (FOR FREDERICK COUNTY RESIDENTS) with frozen meats and fresh fruits and vegetables, along with all the other needed non perishable items and personal care items.

HISTORY

In 1996, three historic churches in East Baltimore - Trinity Lutheran, Bethany English Lutheran & Martin Luther Lutheran - merged to form Amazing Grace Evangelical Lutheran Church. 

​

We are now in our 30th year!

​

A truly multicultural, come as you are congregation, Amazing Grace seeks to be a  safe, healthy, healing place in the east Baltimore neighborhood of McElderry Park. Leaning into God's vision for us and the world, we walk together following Jesus, working to  bring healing, peace and justice to ourselves, neighbors, and the community.

MISSION

Connecting people with the life changing power of Jesus Christ and working for a healthier East Baltimore! 

OFFICE HOURS

Something's happening seven days a week here at Amazing Grace. If we're not in a meeting or program the front door under the blue awning will be open so come on in to say hello or grab a cup of coffeee.

Home

About Us

Our Ministries

Connect with us

Give Now

Plan a Visit

OUR
MINISTRIES

We take a whole Body Approach to Ministry...

Our focus is invested greatly into people and not structures as people are the true value of God's Kingdom...

ROAR Kid's Ministry

ROAR Kids Ministry has been birthed to inspire the youngest hearts to understand the joy and peace of Knowing God's Love and the fact that they are the true treasures of life... Join us for a fun filled experience that is sure to keep your elementary aged children engaged, learning and growing from their very first step into the ROAR Kids Den...

Check out our latest video with special guest, "Bananas", the monkey !
Watch Now
Men of Action

MEN OF ACTION was developed to give all of our men an opportunity to fellowship, grow and connect to other men to support and strengthen the high calling of being a Man of God...

THRIVE Youth Ministry

ROAR Youth Ministry was established to bring Middle and High Schoolers together for a safe and inpirational atmosphere... When you join us, come ready for lots of laughs, food, fun and an extra large dose of inspiration. All of this as we learn and grow in our pursuit of a supernatural God...

"Lioness Arising" Women's Ministry

Our WOMEN'S MINISTRY exists to give all women an open door to the truth of their value...There is a place in God's plan for them to become powerful and life changing...We welcome you to join our Ladies as they grow and learn the specifics of what a full life and identity in Christ can look like...

Gateway To Hope Food Pantry

Our Food Pantry was established  in 2019. We have chosen to be a Church that meets the needs of our community by helping to keep food on the tables of many of our less fortunate neighbors...Feeding hundreds as well as assisting with clothing needs have become a major staple of our Ministry and outreach.If you desire to be a part of an organization that makes a real difference in the lives of all of our neighbors and community, join us and support our ever increasing food and clothing outreach...

We are Family

At Gateway West Church, WE ARE FAMILY... You will often hear our Church family say, " I See You... I Celebrate you...

I want to help Accelerate You...This is the truth in our Church family as we fellowship, encourage and grow together in our pursuit of God...

Join us and find out why we are growing in our spiritual health, confidence as Christians and Passion in servanthood...When all this good is happening, you will understand why our parking lots are full and being expanded...we are breaking down walls for growth and building bridges for relationships with God and people...

Gateway West Church

Office: 301-724-2378

Pastor: 443-255-9152

Email: pastorjason99@gmail.com

Cumberland, MD 21502

JUBILEE YEAR OF ST. FRANCIS
Dear brothers and sisters,
2026 is a special year for us friars. For a few years now, we’ve had a number of special 800-year anniversaries in the Order. From the founding of the Order and the writing of the Rule of Life to the Christmas miracle at Greccio, and the celebration of St. Francis receiving the Stigmata, the mystical wounds of Christ.
Now, this year we are celebrating 800 years since the death of St. Francis on October 4, 1226. As a result, Pope Leo XIV declared this year the “Jubilee Year of St. Francis” starting this past January and going until next year. To help us celebrate, we’ll put a saying of St. Francis or a short story from his life in the bulletin every week.

ABOUT US
Our History

N8 House Inc. (DBA Gabrielle’s Place was established in 2011. Our mission is to change the lives of families affected by poverty through personal responsibility. We began providing residence in Atlanta with a variety of long and short-term programs that include food resources, employment, education, and counseling. During the Covid Pandemic of 2020 we expanded services to our hometown of Baltimore, Maryland. We continued to offer our core services and strengthened our presence by collaborating with organizations that aligned with our mission. These collaborations and partnerships include The Maryland Food, Grand Journey Behavioral Health Solutions, Aldi's Food Market, Safeway, Eddie's Food, Giant Food, and more.  


For the past decade we have been committed to providing services to families in poor socio-economically deprived communities. Our program places an emphasis education, and job training as being the keys to unlock the doors of impoverished communities. Our vision is to create stronger individuals through education and job training. Through this approach we have an 85 % success rate for creating college and trade school graduates. We firmly believe that stronger indivduals create stronger famillies, and stronger families help strengthen communities overall. 

Our Mission

To change the lives of families affected by poverty through personal responsibility.

DONATE 2 SUPPORT
Your donation helps to provide food for a family of five.
$50.00
DONATE

Your support and contributions will enable us to meet our goals and improve conditions. Your generous donation will fund our mission. Sponsor 100lbs of food for a family and receive a custom Gabrielle's Place tote bag.

GABRIELLE’S PLACE MONTHY COMMUNITY PRODUCE DISTRIBUTION




WORKING TOGETHER TO STRENGTHEN OUR COMMUNITY
Afterschool Tutoring

We believe in empowering children to become agents of change in their communities through education. Your donation can help us provide after school and summer tutoring to children in the areas of Math & Reading.

Healthy Eating & Living

We encourage you to become a part of our #Pantry2Table family. We share healthy eating practices and delicious recipes that are made from our monthly community produce distribution and our weekly food pantry items. Please follow us on line www.facebook.com/GabriellesPlace to join in on the fun.

Youth & Young Adult Entrepreneur Academy

In the every changing world today we see more and more of our youth with the desire to become business owners. We offer quarterly workshops that help aid and assist youth, and young adults with developing effective business models and solid business structures. 




Summer Camp Sponsors

Our summer program is designed to provide an extensive range of fun for all ages. We offer sewing, culinary, field trips, group projects, summer tutoring, and more. Free breakfast, lunch and snack are included. This summer camp is designed to keep our youth in a positive atmosphere that nurtures growth, friendship and beautiful summer memories.  Please help keep this program going by sponsoring a child for cam in our 2025 session from June 18-August 25

Volunteer With Us

We need volunteers to help us run our programs effectively. Whether it's event planning or administrative tasks, your support can help us achieve our goals.

Behavioral Health Solutions

Grand Journey Behavioral Health Solutions is our partner in providing a wide range of counseling solutions that include: individual, group, family, and substance abuse counseling. They are professional licensed therapist in the state of Maryland.

LOVE THY NEIGHBOR

King George County Community Food Pantry

Welcome to Love Thy Neighbor Food Pantry

At Love Thy Neighbor Food Pantry in King George, VA, we are committed to providing nutritious food and essential resources to individuals and families facing food insecurity. Our mission is to alleviate hunger in our community by offering free groceries, including fresh produce, dairy, and pantry staples, to those in need.

About Us

Love Thy Neighbor Community Food Pantry was established in 2012 to provide nutritional basics and reassurance to others in need.  Our first food pantry event was held in December, 2012.  We are a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization in King George, Virginia. 

​

Love Thy Neighbor provides a shopper’s choice food pantry, free of charge.  Our focus is on food and hygiene items.  Many of our guests are seniors, veterans, disabled, unemployed, or living paycheck-to-paycheck trying to make it work. ​

Learn More
Get Involved

Getting involved with Love Thy Neighbor Food Pantry in King George, Virginia is a meaningful way to support your neighbors and strengthen the community. The pantry relies on dedicated volunteers, generous donations, and caring partners to provide food, hope, and compassion to families facing hardship.

 

Whether you can lend a hand during distribution days, contribute nonperishable items, or make a financial gift, your support helps ensure that no one in our community goes hungry. By joining Love Thy Neighbor, you become part of a mission built on kindness, dignity, and service to others.

Learn More
Your Gift Matters

Supporting Love Thy Neighbor Food Pantry in King George, Virginia through a donation is a direct way to make a difference in the lives of families facing food insecurity. Every contribution—whether monetary, nonperishable goods, or household essentials—helps provide nourishment, hope, and stability for neighbors in need.

 

Your generosity ensures that the pantry can continue its mission of feeding the hungry and uplifting the community with dignity and compassion. By donating, you are not just giving food—you are giving comfort, kindness, and the reassurance that no one has to face hardship alo

Learn More

“My husband and I are both seniors on social security just getting by. The one month we were down to about $10 and really low on staple foods. We had frozen burger, chicken, and a roast I think, but needed items to round out the meals. Just to get us through the 3 days until our 1st check hit.

​

Because of the LTN pantry, I was able to come by and pick up canned veggies, beans, cheese, soups, some pasta sauce, and even got a bag of frozen chicken breasts. I barely got a small grocery cart half filled but it was enough to get us thru the dry spell. We ate well and kept our bellies full.

​

All our bills were current, but meds and drs visits really cut into our $$$ that month. It was the 1st time I’ve been to the pantry since its inception. Thanks for helping us out. I was embarrassed to show up, but everyone there made me feel welcome. No questions asked. I knew a couple of the volunteers, but they made me feel welcome too.

What a blessing LTN was for us. Thanks for all you do for others.”

Read More
Shop the Pantry

Shopping at the Love Thy Neighbor Food Pantry in King George, Virginia is a welcoming experience designed to provide families with the nourishment and essentials they need in a dignified way. The pantry offers a variety of food and household items at no cost, allowing neighbors to choose what best fits their family’s needs.

 

By creating a respectful, supportive environment, Love Thy Neighbor ensures that those facing challenges can find relief and hope while maintaining the dignity of making their own selections.

Learn More

Online Auction - Ended 2025
 

Neighbors helping Neighbors

"Bristol Faith in Action (BFIA) is about neighbors helping neighbors.  One of our missions is to help our neighbors maintain stable home lives for their families by providing rental assistance and essential services during times of financial crisis.  Since 2002, BFIA has helped more than 77,273  unduplicated individuals and families by providing more than $3,281,220 toward rent essential services, food and commodities helping those families maintain their homes. With housing costs rising dramatically, the need is greater than ever.

​

BFIA has created a special fundraising event “Strings of Hope” to meet the challenge of increasing demand for rental assistance.

About "Strings of Hope"

Strings of Hope: Online Auction by Bristol Faith in Action

​

Bristol Faith in Action proudly presents "Strings of Hope," an online auction to raise funds to support individuals in need in the Bristol, TN and VA areas. 100% of funds raised will go directly towards rental assistance to our neighbors. 

 

For this special event, local artists have transformed stringed instruments into unique works of art, generously donating their time and talent to support our cause. Their beautiful creations highlight their artistic abilities and brings attention to our auction, and the mission of Bristol Faith in Action. 

 

A Work of Art Gallery has partnered with us to support and promote this initiative. They will showcase the artists' works in their storefront to spread the word and demonstrate their commitment to our community. They will be on display from September 1 through September 30. The online auction goes live on September 17 through September 30. 

 

You can support this cause in several ways: by spreading the word, making a direct donation, visiting and supporting A Work of Art Gallery, or bidding on a one-of-a-kind piece of art. Bristol Faith in Action is dedicated to helping families remain stable in their homes during times of need. Your support is crucial in making a difference in our community every day.

​​​

Upcoming is a Meet the Artist and review the guitar art event on August 28 at 1556 Euclid Ave. Bristol, VA, open to the public from 5:30 - 7:30.

Who does GEDCO Serve?

GEDCO offers a wide range of supportive services to residents in Northeast Baltimore, specifically focusing on the zip codes 21210, 21212, 21239, and 21218. Serving as a one-stop-shop for these areas, GEDCO provides financial and eviction assistance, food support, and various housing opportunities. In addition to these core services, GEDCO collaborates with other organizations to provide residents beyond our area of focus access to employment assistance, affordable housing, and references to additional resources.

Enhancing Lives Through
Housing and Services
Welcome To our New Board Members!

Help us welcome three new faces to the Board! Naima Holland, Michael Sontag, and Melissa Warren will have been elected and officially start on GEDCO's Board of Directors on April 1st, 2026!

Click here to meet the rest of our team!

Emergency food and financial aid to more than 4,100 individuals annually

Welcome to Stephens City United Methodist Church

We are a warm and welcoming community of believers dedicated to serving the needs of our community through our outreach ministries. Our ministries, including Messy Church, Vacation Bible School, and a variety of outreach programs, offer opportunities for all ages to connect, grow, and serve. Join us as we live out our faith through worship, fellowship, and community engagement.

OUR LEADERSHIP

Salvation Army Programs are led by Salvation Army Officers. These officers are responsible to lead by assessing needs, delivering programs and directing ministries for our local community. From serving food to balancing the books, they function as the Executive Directors and Pastors.

Officer: Lieutenant Antione D. Terrell

Office Hours

Mon-Fri
8:30 AM AM – 4:30 PM

Social Service Hours
Mon and Thurs
9 AM – 5 PM

 

Lieutenant Antione D. Terrell

The Salvation Army Mission Statement

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian Church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.





Commitment to Diversity

First United Methodist Church

Open Hearts • Open Minds • Open Doors

Welcome to First Church!
Sunday Worship
Early Service: 8:30 AM
Sunday School: 9:30 AM
Worship Service: 11:00 AM


For over 185 years, First United Methodist Church of Martinsville has remained faithful to the Word of God, proclaiming His greatness through worship, scripture study, and Christian action. These three basic ministry fields lie at the core of the numerous and diverse programs offered by our church.

Centrally located at the corners of East Church Street and Lester Street in Uptown Martinsville, First Church is easily accessible from all parts of Martinsville and Henry County. Check our Location Page for customizable driving directions.

Come and worship with us this Sunday!

The United Methodist Church forms disciples of Jesus Christ who, empowered by the Holy Spirit, love boldly, serve joyfully, and lead courageously in local communities and worldwide connections.

OUR AVAILABLE PETS

DONATE NOW
Item 1 of 5

Richmond Animal League is committed to saving lives by giving hope, help, and loving homes to animals in need. Our vision: All cats and dogs are in a loving home with access to the support they need to thrive.

Pet Adoption

Every pet deserves a loving home and you can help make that happen today! We provide medical care, vaccinations, spay/neuter, and socialization with our loving team of volunteers and staff so when you’re ready to open your home, they are ready to move in.  

HOW TO ADOPT
Spay & Neuter Clinic

Our on site clinic provides shelter partners, RAL shelter pets, community cat programs and the public with access to affordable spay/neuter, and vaccination services that save lives and help reduce the homeless animal population.

ABOUT OUR CLINIC
Support

There are so many ways for you to support RAL: monetary donations, supplies, volunteering your time, fostering a pet, attending an event or hosting a fundraiser. As a non-profit organization, it’s your support that makes all of our lifesaving work possible.

DONATE NOW
The Latest at RAL
Why We Don’t Breed Label Our Dogs at RAL

Ever wondered why we don’t list breeds? Learn how focusing on each dog’s personality helps them find the right home.

Read More →
 
Employment
Full-Time Volunteer Coordinator

RAL is seeking a volunteer coordinator to support our volunteer program and dedicated team of volunteers who play a crucial role in achieving the mission of Richmond Animal League.

Read More →
 
Foster
When Love Has Somewhere to Go: The Fergusons’ Foster Journey

There are so many reasons people choose to foster. For some, it’s about helping a pet in need. For others, it begins in a quieter place, shaped by loss, love, and the desire to make space for something meaningful again.

Today, we’re sharing reflections from the Fergusons, whose fostering journey began during a tender season and grew into something deeply transformative, for the dogs in their care and for their own hearts.

Read More →
MORE RAL RESOURCES & NEWS
Our Location Hours 

 Adoption Center:

Our adoption center is open for walk-ins Tuesday-Friday, 3 pm - 6 pm, and Saturday & Sunday, 12 pm -4 pm!


Administrative offices:

Monday - Friday, 8:30 am – 5:30 pm

RAL’s Pet Pantry Pickup Hours:

Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM

If you need food or would like to see what's available, text us at 804-372-5226. You can also use this number to request a pickup outside of our regular hours—we’ll do our best to accommodate when we can.

Our Pet Pantry relies on donations, so availability can vary. If you’re coming by for food, we recommend texting ahead to make sure we can assist you.

Donations:

Donations can be dropped off at any time in our outdoor collection bins located at our shelter at 11401 International Drive or at our clinic located at 801 Branchway Rd.

Join our community of neighbors helping neighbors!

We’re Meals on Wheels of Takoma Park/Silver Spring in Maryland. Woven into the fabric of our community, we deliver healthy, delicious food to our homebound neighbors. Guided by this mission, we work towards our vision of a more compassionate, resilient, and interconnected community. Welcome!

“Demand Surges for Meals on Wheels”: Our Clients and Volunteers Featured in AARP News
April 10, 2026
In a March story in AARP News, our homebound clients, volunteers, and staff at Meals on Wheels of Takoma Park/Silver Spring helped shine a light on the many benefits of our community-based nonprofit service, now in its 56th year.
Become a client

Are you or a loved one unable to shop for or make meals due to age, disability, illness or injury? Do you live in Silver Spring, Takoma Park, Adelphi, Langley Park, or Chillum? If so, it would be our honor to serve you or a loved one.

READ MORE
Donate

Your support means the world to us. Without your commitment, we can’t continue to be of service. You’re an important part of our community and we thank you!

READ MORE
Volunteer

Our volunteers keep our kitchen humming and our wheels rolling! Help us prepare fresh meals in our Takoma Park kitchen or get our deliveries to those who need them most.

READ MORE

Meals on Wheels of Takoma Park/Silver Spring has earned Candid’s Gold Seal of Transparency. This seal is earned through demonstrating excellent governance, management, and transparency practices.

We have been designated a critical local nonprofit by a team of community advocates from foundations and peer nonprofits in the Greater Washington region. Meals on Wheels of Takoma Park/Silver Spring is proud to be part of Spur Local’s Class of 2025! Read more

Our 2024 Impact

Meals on Wheels of Takoma Park/Silver Spring is a proud member of Meals on Wheels America, a membership association that provides research, advocacy, educational programs, and networking opportunities to strengthen local organizations like ours.

Every year, we participate in the Subaru Share The Love Event. If you’re interested in purchasing or leasing a new car, consider a Subaru — and consider purchasing between mid-November and the end of the year. During those six weeks each year, you can select Meals on Wheels as a charity recipient and our program will receive a donation from Subaru. Subaru’s Share the Love Event has helped deliver nearly 5 million meals nationwide and thousands here in Takoma Park and Silver Spring.
